org:east-coast-crips-66.description: The 66 East Coast Crips (Six-Six) are a subset of the East Coast Crips on the East Side of South Los Angeles. They are part of the 6-Pacc alliance alongside the 62, 68, and 69 East Coast Crip sets, operating between Avalon Blvd and the 110 Freeway.

org:east-coast-crips-97.description: East Coast Crips are a predominately African-American street gang on the eastside of South Los Angeles and Watts. They are aligned with all other East Coast Crips sets and are part of the Neighborhood Crip car.

org:neighbor-hood-crips-106.description: The 106 Neighborhood Crips are an African-American Crip set operating around 106th Street in South Los Angeles.

org:53-five-tray-avalon-gangster-crips-i.description: Avalon Gangster Crips are a predominately African-American street gang on the eastside of South Los Angeles. They are based in the community around South Park, where award winning singer Barry White grew up. During the 1950s and 1960s, the BusinessMen where the dominant group in the area prior to the 1965 Watts Riots.

sources.title: 53 (Five Tray) Avalon Gangster Crips in Los Angeles — StreetGangs

org:neighbor-hood-crips-lynwood.description: The Lynwood Neighborhood Crips are an African-American Crip set based in the city of Lynwood, California, south of Watts. Part of the Neighborhood Crips card that expanded from South LA into surrounding cities in the late 1970s–1980s.

org:burris-blocc-compton-crip.description: The Burris Blocc Compton Crips are a predominantly African-American Crip set based in the Compton area of Los Angeles.

org:eight-tray-83-gangster-crip-los-ange.description: Gangster Crips are a predominately African-American street gang, founded during the mid 1970s as a splinter gang off the Original West Side Crips, that included Stanley "Tookie" Williams, Big Bob the Hawk, Melvin Farmer, Judson Bacot, Angelo "Barefoot Pookie" White and Mad Dog.

sources.title: Eight Tray (83) Gangster Crip  Los Angeles — StreetGangs

org:101-crip-gang.description: African-American Crip set based on the East Side of South Los Angeles near 101st Street, emerged in the late 1970s as part of the broader Crip expansion across South Central LA.

org:play-boy-style-crips-106.description: Street Play Boy Style Crips are a predominantly Black American street gang on the westside of South Los Angeles. There are two other gangs that claim PBSC, the 95th Street faction located in Athens / Westmont in unincorporated Los Angeles County and the 82nd Street faction located in South Los Angeles.

org:down-ass-pimp-gang-109.description: A predominantly African-American street gang (DAP) located around 109th Street in South Los Angeles.

org:east-coast-blocc-crips-118.description: 118 East Coast Blocc Crips are a predominately African-American street gang located in South Los Angeles and part of the East Coast Crips alliance. They are only one of only two East Coast Crip sets that identify as Blocc Crips.

org:19th-street-crips-in-long-beach.description: The 19th Street Crips are a predominately African-American street gang located in the area of 19th Street and Lime just off the Pacific Coast Highway.

sources.title: 19th Street Crips in Long Beach — StreetGangs

org:tray-duece-crip-32crips.description: The Tray Deuce (32) Crips are an African-American Crip set based around 32nd Street in South Los Angeles, one of the northernmost Crip sets in the South Central area near the University of Southern California.

org:four-duece-42-crip-gang-w-s.description: The Four Deuce (42) Crips are an African-American Crip set based on the West Side of South Los Angeles around 42nd Street.

sources.title: Four Duece 42 Crip W S — Wikipedia

org:east-side-ridaz-59.description: An African-American Crip-affiliated gang based around 59th Street on the East Side of South Los Angeles, operating in the area east of the Harbor Freeway.

org:east-side-ridas-64.description: African-American Crip-affiliated gang based on the East Side of South Los Angeles around 64th Street, part of the East Side Crip tradition tracing back to Raymond Washington's original East Side Crips.

org:play-boy-style-crips-82.description: Street Play Boy Style Crips are a Black American street gang located on the westside of South Los Angeles. There are two other factions of Play Boy Style Crips; the 106th Street PBSCs located in South Los Angeles and the 95th Street PBSC located in West Athens / Westmont, an Unincorporated section of Los Angeles County.

org:play-boy-style-crips-93-95.description: Playboy Style Crips are a predominately Black American street gang located on the westside of South Los Angeles in an unincorporated Athens. The first click of this faction of the PBSCs was 95th Street and for a short time they had the 93rd Street representatives.

org:east-side-players-97.description: An African-American Crip-affiliated gang based around 97th Street on the East Side of South Los Angeles, part of the numerous numbered-street sets in the area between Century and Imperial Boulevards.

org:holmes-town-crips-97.description: A subset of the Holmes Town Crips operating around 97th Street in the Watts area of South Los Angeles.

org:a-line-crips.description: An African-American Crip set based in Watts, South Los Angeles, notable for their participation in the 1992 Watts truce alongside the Bounty Hunter Bloods.

org:bible-crips.description: The Bible Crips are an African-American Crip set based on the East Side of South Los Angeles, operating within LAPD's 77th Street Division. They emerged in the 1970s-1980s as the Crips expanded across South Central into a network of autonomous sets.

org:born-to-jacc-crips-73rdst.description: An African-American Crip set based around 73rd Street in South Los Angeles, with 'Jacc' referring to their practice of robbery.

org:colden-avenue-gangster.description: A street gang based near Colden Avenue in Los Angeles, claiming territory in the surrounding neighborhoods.

org:dshc-91st.description: The Down South Hustler Crips (DSHC) are an African-American Crip set operating around 91st Street in South Los Angeles, part of the Hustler Crips alliance on the south end of South Central.

org:dirty-old-man-gang.description: Historical street gang based in the Jefferson Park area of South Los Angeles, active since at least the mid-20th century.

org:hawthorne-thug-family-htf.description: The Hawthorne Thug Family (HTF) are a street gang based in the city of Hawthorne in the South Bay area of Los Angeles County.

org:home-boys-crimino-gang-in-the-west-l.description: Home Boys Crimino Gang are a predominately African-American street gang located in the West LA section of Los Angeles. They were active during the 1990s but in recent years they have not been visible.

sources.title: Home Boys Crimino Gang in the West LA area — StreetGangs

org:raymond-ave-crips-120th-street-in-we.description: Street Crips are a predominately African-American street gang located in the unincorporated area of Los Angeles County called Athens just west of the Los Angeles City border in California. Specifically they are between Normandie (west) and Vermont (east) Avenues from 120th Street (north) to El Segundo Blvd (south). Members of this set will often claim Los Angeles, and there is some presence of the Raymond Crips east of Los Angeles border of Vermont Ave.

sources.title: Raymond Ave Crips, 120th Street in West Athens Unincorporated Los Angeles County — StreetGangs

org:straight-ballers-society.description: A street gang active in the Los Angeles area during the 1990s–2000s, part of the party-crew-to-gang era in Southern California.

org:too-many-hoes.description: Too Many Hoes Gang (TMHG) is a predominantly African-American Crip set based in South Los Angeles, one of the smaller Crip cliques in the South Central area.

org:menlo-gangster-crips-103.description: 908836 702614A thoughtful insight and concepts I will use on my weblog. You?ve naturally spent a great deal of time on this.

org:105th-street-gangster-crips.description: Street Gangster Crips are a predominately African-American street gang located near 105th Street and Western in unincorporated Los Angeles County.

org:east-side-hustler-crips-115-118.description: A subset of the Hustler Crips operating along 115th and 118th Streets on the East Side of South Los Angeles.

org:harvard-gangster-crips-127.description: A Crip set operating around 127th Street in the Harvard Park area of South Los Angeles under the Gangster Crips card.

org:gangster-crips-47.description: The 47 Gangster Crips are an African-American Crip set operating around 47th Street in South Los Angeles under the Gangster Crips umbrella.

org:gangster-crips-48.description: The 48 Gangster Crips are a predominantly African-American Crip set operating around 48th Street in South Los Angeles under the Gangster Crips umbrella.

org:play-boy-hustler-crips-68-69.description: A subset of the Play Boy Hustler Crips operating around 68th and 69th Streets in South Los Angeles, combining the Playboy and Hustler Crip lineages in the area south of Slauson Avenue.

org:play-boy-hustler-crips-75.description: A subset of the Play Boy Hustler Crips operating around 75th Street in South Los Angeles, part of the mid-South LA corridor where numerous Crip sets overlap between Florence and Manchester Boulevards.

org:hard-time-hustler-crips-78.description: A subset of the Hard Time Hustler Crips operating around 78th Street in the Watts/South Los Angeles area, one of several cliques within the broader HTHC alliance that spans from 78th to 104th Streets.

org:hard-time-hustler-crips-88-93.description: A subset of the Hard Time Hustler Crips operating along 88th and 93rd Streets in South Los Angeles, part of the broader HTHC alliance that is one of the larger Hustler Crip formations in the area.

org:gangster-crips-96.description: The 96 Gangster Crips are a predominantly African-American Crip set based around 96th Street on the East Side of South Los Angeles, formed in the early 1980s under the Gangster Crips card.

org:gangster-crips-97.description: The 97 Gangster Crips are an African-American Crip set operating around 97th Street in South Los Angeles under the Gangster Crips umbrella.

org:gangster-crips-98.description: The 98 Gangster Crips are an African-American Crip set operating around 98th Street in South Los Angeles under the Gangster Crips umbrella.

org:asian-boyz-crips-eastside-abz.description: The Eastside Asian Boyz are a predominantly Cambodian-American Crip-affiliated gang founded around 1989 in Long Beach, California. The ABZ have grown to approximately 2,000 members across 14 states and are classified by the FBI as one of the largest Asian gangs in the United States.

org:102raymond.description: Inglewood The Ten Deuce Raymond Ave Crips are a predominately African-American street gang in Inglewood, California started in the late 1970s.

org:osage-legend-crips-inglewood.description: Legend Crips is a predominately Black gang in the City of Inglewood based off Osage Avenue. They are located between Praire Avenue (east) to La Brea (west) from Manchester Blvd (north) to about 97th Street (south).

sources.title: Osage Legend Crips, Legend Crips — StreetGangs
Osage Legend Crips, Legend Crips — StreetGangs
Osage Legend Crips, Legend Crips — StreetGangs
Osage Legend Crips, Legend Crips — StreetGangs
Osage Legend Crips, Legend Crips — StreetGangs

org:original-inglewood-crips.description: Original Inglewood Crips The Inglewood Crips were a predominately African-American street gang that formed around 1972. One of its early leaders was Mike Christian who later became a success professional bodybuilder.

sources.title: Original Front Hood Compton Crip — StreetGangs

org:imperial-village-crips.description: Imperial Village Crips are a predominately African-American street gang in the city of Inglewood in the Arbor Village on the Imperial Highway.

org:dragnet.description: Gardena Dragnet Gang are a predominately African-American street gang located in Gardena, California, a suburban city South of Los Angeles.

org:tongancrips.description: The Tongan Crip Gang (TCG) is a Tongan-American Crip set founded in Inglewood, California in the 1970s. Active in California, Utah, Alaska, Washington, and Auckland, New Zealand. The Salt Lake City branch formed in the mid-1980s after Tongan youth were intimidated by Latino gangs in Glendale. Members moved to Utah to escape LA law enforcement pressure. They use the Texas Rangers and Minnesota Twins baseball logos as symbols. In 2007, TCG and 18th Street were indicted for conspiracy to murder 33 MS-13 members.

org:rollin100s.description: Crip Alliance is a group of several independent Crips gangs that are geographically located north of Century Blvd in South Los Angeles County and the City of Hawthorne.

org:5-deuce-hoover-gangster-crips.description: African-American Crip set founded in the 1970s in South Los Angeles. One of the original Hoover Gangster Crips sets operating along Hoover Street between 52nd and 54th Streets.

org:59hoover.description: The 59 Hoover Criminals are a subset of the Hoover Criminal Gang on the West Side of South Los Angeles. The Hoovers originated in the late 1960s as the Hoover Groovers, later dropping their Crip affiliation to become independent Hoover Criminals, identifiable by their orange color.

org:83hoover.description: Hoovers are a predominately African American street gang located in South Los Angeles along South Hoover Avenue in South Los Angeles.

org:92hoover.description: Hoover are a predominately African-American street gang located in South Los Angeles and are the consensus oldest of all Hoover sets.

org:94-hoover-crips.description: Westside 94 Hoover are a predominately African American street gang located in the area of 94th Street & Hoover Avenues near Bret Harte Middle School. For many years they identified as Crips but in the 1990s they dropped the Crip identity because of their inability to have any allies that were Crips. Since the 1980s they have been involved in rivalries with some of the largest Crips gangs, including the Rollin 60s Crips, Main Street Crips, and several sets of East Coasts Crips.

org:112-hoover-criminals.description: The 112 Hoover Criminals Gang are a sub-set of the infamous Hoover Criminals Gang. The 112 Hoover Criminals Gang originated in the 1970s, around 107 Street and Hoover.

org:43-hoover-criminals-gang.description: African-American Crip set founded in the 1970s on 43rd Street and Hoover Street in South Los Angeles. Originally known as the 43 Hoover Crips before splitting from the Crip alliance to become the Hoover Criminals Gang (4-Tray Hoover Criminals).

org:77-hoover-block-crips.description: The 77 Hoover Block Crips are one of the original Hoover subsets on the West Side of South Los Angeles, named for their territory around 77th Street and Hoover Street. The Hoovers collectively have been described as one of the most belligerent gang organizations in South LA, feuding with virtually all surrounding gangs except their longtime allies the Main Street Crips.

org:107-hoover-criminals.description: The 107 Hoover Criminals are a subset of the Hoover Criminal Gang on the West Side of South Los Angeles along Hoover Street between Vermont and Figueroa Avenues, centered around 107th Street. In 2020, a joint FBI-LAPD investigation dubbed 'Operation Hoover Dam' led to federal indictments for distributing methamphetamine, crack cocaine, and PCP from a South LA storefront.

org:43-hoover-gangster-crips.description: The 43 Hoover Gangster Crips were a predominately African-American street gang located along Hoover Street between Santa Barbara (now Martin Luther King Jr. Blvd) and Vernon Avenues during the 1980s.

org:74-hoover-criminals.description: The 74 Hoover Criminals are a subset of the Hoover Criminal Gang founded around 1973 on the West Side of South Central Los Angeles near 74th Street and Hoover Street. Like other Hoover subsets, they dropped their Crip affiliation in the 1980s and became hostile to both Crip and Blood gangs. In 2005, a federal indictment in Seattle targeted the 74 Hoover Crips for drug trafficking operations that had spread from LA to the Pacific Northwest.

org:80-hoover-criminals.description: The 80 Hoover Criminals are a subset of the Hoover Criminal Gang, operating around 80th Street on the West Side of South Los Angeles along Hoover Street.

org:52-hoover-gangster-crips.description: 52 Hoover Gangster Crips are a predominately African-American street gang located in the Vermont-Slauson community of South LA in the City of Los Angeles. They are connected to both the Gangster alliance and the Hoover alliance and formed during the late 1970s.

org:hoover-criminals.description: The Hoover Criminals Gang (HCG) are a collection of African-American street gang sets founded in the 1960s along Hoover Street in South Los Angeles. Originally part of the Crips, they dropped the Crip identity in the 1990s and became independent, warring with both Crips and Bloods.

org:94-hoover-criminals-gang.description: Fo Hoover Criminals Gang), are an African-American street gang located on the West Side of South Los Angeles, California.

org:decavalcante-crime.description: The DeCavalcante crime family is an Italian-American Mafia crime family operating primarily in North Jersey, particularly in Elizabeth, Newark, Linden, and West New York. Founded around the 1900s by Phil Amari, it is considered by some to be the 'Sixth Family' due to its strong ties with New York's Five Families.

org:lucchese-crime-brooklyn-faction.description: The Lucchese crime family that control organized crime activities in the New York metropolitan area, predominantly based in Brooklyn and Staten Island. It was created in the early 2000s by merging the Vario Crew and the 19th Hole Crew.

org:bonanno-crime.description: The Bonanno crime family is an Italian American Mafia crime family and one of the Five Families that dominate organized crime in New York City. Founded by Salvatore Maranzano in the 1890s, it was later led by Joseph Bonanno.

org:gambino-crime.description: The Gambino family is one of the Five Families of New York and one of the most powerful in the United States. Originating from the Black Hand gang led by Ignazio Lupo and Giuseppe Morello in East Harlem, it evolved under Salvatore D'Aquila and later Al Mineo. The family was formally established as one of the Five Families by Salvatore Maranzano after the Castellammarese War.

org:the-commission.description: The governing body of the American Mafia, formed in 1931 by Charles 'Lucky' Luciano after the Castellammarese War. It consists of the bosses of the Five Families of New York City, the Chicago Outfit, and occasionally other families. The Commission oversees Mafia activities in the United States and mediates conflicts between families.

org:bufalino-crime.description: Italian American Mafia crime family based in Northeastern Pennsylvania, primarily in Scranton, Wilkes-Barre, and Pittston. Originated in the 1880s with Stefano La Torre, and was long led by Russell Bufalino until his death in 1994. Engaged in labor racketeering in the coal and garment industries, and colluded with New York's Five Families. The family was active from around 1900 until 2008.

org:san-jose-crime.description: Italian American Mafia crime family based in San Jose, California, founded in the early 1940s by Onofrio Sciortino. The family engaged in loansharking, gambling, counterfeiting, prostitution, and extortion, with around 25 made members in the 1960s. It was led by Joseph Cerrito and later Angelo Marino, and was active until the 2000s.

org:genovese-crime.description: The Genovese crime family is an Italian American Mafia crime family and one of the Five Families that dominate organized crime in New York City and New Jersey. Founded by Charles 'Lucky' Luciano in the 1930s, it is the oldest and largest of the Five Families. Known for its secrecy and white-collar rackets, it has been considered the most powerful Mafia family in the United States.

org:ovs.description: Ontario Varrio Sur (OVS) is a Chicano street gang from Ontario, California with approximately 450 members as of 2010. Contains two main cliques: Jr Black Angels and Black Angels. Has deep-rooted ties to the Mexican Mafia with over a dozen active La Eme members. Their primary activity according to Ontario PD is murder. OVS member Reuben 'Tupi' Hernandez rose to become La Eme godfather, challenging Benjamin 'Topo' Peters for control. In 2010, a joint federal-state RICO operation indicted 52 OVS members. San Bernardino County's largest gang case ended in 2015 with 485 years of combined prison sentences. Territory spans San Bernardino, Orange, Riverside, and Los Angeles counties.

org:rochester-crime-family.description: Italian American Mafia crime family based in Rochester, New York, founded in the 1950s by Constenze 'Stanley' Valenti. It operated primarily in the Greater Rochester area, engaging in racketeering, loansharking, gambling, extortion, and other illegal activities. The family split from the Buffalo crime family in the 1970s and was active until around 1993.

org:velentzas-organization.description: Greek-American criminal organization operating in New York City, founded by Peter Kourakos in the 1950s. Under Spyredon Velentzas, it controlled illegal gambling operations in Astoria, Queens, and allied with the Lucchese crime family. After Velentzas' life sentence in 1992, the group declined but remains active under Fotios Dimopoulos.

org:playboys-gang.description: American street gang founded in Los Angeles County, California in the mid-1950s, originally a car club. It has multiple cliques and is predominantly Hispanic, with an estimated 6,500 members. The gang is aligned with the Mexican Mafia (La EME) and engages in drug trafficking, assault, robbery, and other criminal activities. It remains active across several U.S. states and Mexico.

sources.title: Playboys, Ventures, Pulaski Park, Rice Boys - Chicago Gang History

edges.evidence: Gaylords also chummed up real close to Playboys and Ventures. Both groups came to Belmont-Cragin, Austin and West Humboldt Park in 1971 and came in peace with the Gaylords.
Playboys were one of the biggest rivals of Leavitt and Schiller Latin Kings

On other days Playboys would patrol down Ashland looking for trouble with Latin Kings and along the way they would see the football team and go after them since they were a group of Puerto Rican kids.
C-Notes would fight Playboys quite often in these early days.

org:10th--oregon-crew.description: The 10th & Oregon Crew is a predominantly Italian American organized crime gang operating in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, and South Jersey. Founded in the 1960s by Rocco Turra, the gang is active in drug trafficking, gambling, extortion, and loan sharking. It has both allied with and feuded against the Philadelphia crime family. The gang remains active as of the present.

org:angiulo-brothers.description: The Angiulo brothers were a leading Italian-American crime group based in Boston's North End, active from the 1960s until the mid-1980s. They were made men in the Patriarca crime family and controlled racketeering throughout Massachusetts. Their headquarters was the 'Dog House' convenience store. The organization was dismantled after FBI bugging, leading to convictions in the 1980s.

org:bergin-hunt-and-fish-club.description: The Bergin Hunt and Fish Club was a former hangout of the Gambino crime family in Queens, New York. Despite its name, it was not a legitimate hunting or fishing club but a front for criminal activities such as truck hijacking and loansharking. The club consisted of two connected brick-front stores located at 98-04 101st Avenue in Ozone Park, Queens.

sources.title: Bergin Hunt and Fish Club — Wikipedia

org:winter-hill-gang.description: The Winter Hill Gang was a loose confederation of American organized crime figures based in the Boston area, primarily active from 1961 to 2000. Founded by James 'Buddy' McLean and Howard 'Howie' Winter, it was predominantly Irish-American and involved in racketeering, drug trafficking, gambling, and weapons smuggling. The gang was the second most powerful criminal organization in New England after the Patriarca crime family.

org:black-mafia.description: The Black Mafia was a Philadelphia-based African-American organized crime syndicate founded around 1969 by Samuel Christian. It controlled drug trafficking, extortion, and other rackets in Philadelphia and the Delaware Valley during the 1970s and early 1980s. The group was known for its use of intimidation and violence, with over 40 murders attributed to it. It became largely defunct by 1984, though a successor group, the Junior Black Mafia, operated until 1992.

org:barriox13.description: Barriox13 is a highly active Sureños street gang based in South Los Angeles, founded in the early 1970s near El Segundo Boulevard and San Pedro Street. The gang is divided into West Side and East Side factions, with over 5,000 members loyal to the Mexican Mafia. It is involved in narcotics, homicide, drug and human trafficking, and has expanded into Southern California and Mexico.

org:logan-heights-gang.description: The Logan Heights Gang is a Sureño street gang based in Southeast San Diego, California, founded in the 1970s from the unification of several Mexican-American gangs. It is composed of five main subsets and has spread to other U.S. cities and Tijuana, Mexico. The gang is involved in drug trafficking, murder, kidnapping, and human smuggling, and has alliances with the Tijuana Cartel and Mexican Mafia.

org:philadelphia-greek-mob.description: The Philadelphia Greek Mob was a loose-knit Greek-American criminal faction operating in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, closely allied with the Italian-American Philadelphia crime family. Active primarily from the 1970s to the 1980s, it engaged in racketeering, drug trafficking, extortion, loansharking, and illegal gambling.

org:the-council.description: African-American organized crime syndicate in New York City that controlled the heroin trade in Harlem during the 1970s. Formed by Nicky Barnes in 1972, the seven-man organization handled local criminal disputes and drug distribution. It was heavily connected to the Italian-American Mafia, supplied by the Lucchese crime family. The Council dissolved in 1983 after Barnes became a federal informant.

org:cowboy-mafia.description: The Cowboy Mafia was a nickname for a group of marijuana smugglers operating in the United States during the 1970s. Based in Texas, they imported over 106 tons of marijuana from Colombia using shrimp boats. The group was arrested in 1978 and 26 members were convicted in 1979. Rex Cauble, considered the headman, was sentenced to 95 years.

org:cornbread-mafia.description: The Cornbread Mafia was a group of Kentucky men who operated the largest domestic marijuana production operation in U.S. history, based in central Kentucky. Founded in the late 1970s by Joe Keith Bickett and others, the group grew marijuana across 30 farms in 10 states. The term was first publicly used by federal prosecutors in 1989 after a massive bust. The group was known for its code of silence, which prevented a Continuing Criminal Enterprise prosecution.

org:chaldean-mafia.description: The Chaldean mafia is a collective of criminal organizations composed of Chaldeans that emerged in the United States during the 1980s. It is active primarily in Detroit, as well as San Diego and Phoenix. The groups are involved in murder, arson, weapons trafficking, narcotics trafficking, money laundering, fraud, theft, counterfeiting, racketeering, alien smuggling, assault, kidnapping, and armed robbery.

org:new-springville-boys.description: The New Springville Boys was an Italian-American recruitment gang or farm team for the American Mafia, specifically the Bonanno and Colombo crime families. Based in Staten Island, New York, the gang operated from the 1980s to the 2000s, engaging in burglary, robbery, loan sharking, money laundering, drug trafficking, and hijacking. Members were indicted for burglarizing night-deposit boxes across the country and other crimes.

org:ozone-park-boys.description: The Ozone Park Boys are a Gambino crime family Mafia crew based in Ozone Park, Queens, New York City. Founded in the 1980s, they are known for a wide range of criminal activities including a $30 million-a-year sports gambling enterprise, auto theft, murder, and drug trafficking. The crew operated under Ronald Trucchio during the 1990s and remains active.

org:mexikanemi.description: Texas Mexican Mafia, is a Mexican-American prison and street gang established in the Texas state prison system in 1984. It operates independently from the California Mexican Mafia and engages in drug trafficking, loan sharking, money laundering, and other illegal activities. The gang has a formal written constitution and a hierarchical structure.

org:loomis-gang.description: The Loomis Gang was a family of outlaws operating in Central New York during the mid-19th century. Founded by George Washington Loomis Sr. and his wife Rhoda Mallet, the gang specialized in horse theft, livestock rustling, counterfeiting, and burglary. They expanded into a multi-state syndicate under Wash Loomis in the 1840s, buying protection from authorities and cultivating local goodwill to avoid prosecution.

org:roach-guards.description: The Roach Guards were an ethnically Irish criminal gang in the Five Points neighborhood of New York City in the early to mid-19th century. Founded by Ted Roach, the gang originally protected liquor merchants before engaging in robbery and murder. They fought rival gangs such as the Bowery Boys and declined during the 1850s, disappearing by 1865.

org:shirt-tails.description: The Shirt Tails were a mid-19th-century street gang based in the Five Points slum in Manhattan, New York City. They wore their shirts outside their pants as insignia and concealed weapons beneath. Active from the 1830s to the 1860s, they participated in the New York Draft Riots and disappeared shortly before the American Civil War.

org:atlantic-guards.description: The Atlantic Guards were a 19th-century American street gang active in New York City from the 1840s to the 1860s. They were one of the original Bowery gangs, known for nativist vigilante activities and street fighting against Irish American gangs like the Dead Rabbits. They wore a red stripe on their trousers as a uniform and were allied with the Bowery Boys. The gang participated in the Dead Rabbits Riot of 1857.

org:slaughter-house-gang.description: A prominent street gang in New York's Fourth Ward during the late 1880s to the mid-1990s, known for violent muggings and murder. They lured victims into dive bars or used a bucket of ash to blind them before robbing and sometimes killing them. The gang was led by George Leese, a river pirate and illegal prize fight organizer. They dominated the Fourth Ward until police drove them out after the American Civil War.

org:honeymoon-gang.description: The Honeymoon Gang was a mid-19th century New York street gang based in the East Side's 18th Ward. Known for brutal attacks on passersby, they operated with impunity until 1853 when police Captain George W. Walling formed the Strong Arm Squad to suppress them. The gang was disbanded within two weeks, with members fleeing the district.

org:charlton-street-gang.description: The Charlton Street Gang was a group of river pirates active on the Hudson River in New York City during the mid-19th century. They raided small cargo ships and later moved upriver, stealing a sloop in 1869 under leader Sadie the Goat. The gang engaged in kidnapping for ransom and flew the Jolly Roger flag. They dissolved by the end of the 1860s after conflicts with vigilantes.

org:grady-gang.description: The Grady Gang was a New York City sneak thief gang during the 1860s, organized by fence John D. 'Traveling Mike' Grady after the American Civil War. The gang operated in Broadway's 'Thieves Exchange' and was known for the robbery of $2 million from financier Rufus L. Lord in 1866. Most members retired by the end of the decade, but Grady continued as a fence.

org:tub-of-blood-bunch.description: Early New York waterfront street gang active in the late 1860s, based in the Corlears' Hook section of the East River. Formed from members of various gangs after a police cleanup of the Fourth Ward. Operated from a bar called the Tub of Blood. The gang faded into obscurity by the 1880s.

org:whyos.description: The Whyos were a dominant street gang in New York City from the late 1860s to the early 1890s, formed from remnants of Five Points gangs after police crackdowns. They controlled much of Manhattan, engaging in street fighting, murder, robbery, and extortion. The gang was predominantly Irish and required members to have killed at least once. They were eventually defeated by the Monk Eastman Gang.

org:19th-street-gang.description: The 19th Street Gang was a New York City predominantly Irish street gang during the 1870s, known for anti-Protestant violence. Led by Little Mike, the gang consisted of young pickpockets, muggers, and sneak thieves operating around 19th to 34th Street. They targeted defenseless victims but sometimes spared local Catholics. The gang disappeared by the end of the 1880s.

org:baxter-street-dudes.description: The Baxter Street Dudes was a New York City teenage street gang active in the 1870s, consisting of former newsboys and bootblacks. Founded by Baby-Face Willie, they operated the Grand Duke's Theatre from a basement on Baxter Street, which became their headquarters and a popular underworld hangout. The gang wrote and performed plays, musicals, and variety shows, and engaged in tax evasion, thievery, and charity.

org:batavia-street-gang.description: New York independent street gang based in the Fourth Ward during the 1890s. Affiliated with the Eastman Gang, they were rivals of the Cherry Hill Gang. Known for a jewelry store robbery to fund costumes for a ball. Five members were arrested and tried in the incident.

org:neighbors-sons.description: The Neighbors' Sons was a New York street gang active in the early 1900s, operating in neighborhoods between Bleecker and Grove Streets. They were rivals of the Gopher Gang and the Hudson Dusters. The gang was involved in violent brawls and altercations with law enforcement, and was broken up during police campaigns against street gangs in 1916.

org:white-hand-gang.description: The White Hand Gang was a collection of Irish American gangs on the New York City, Brooklyn, and Red Hook waterfronts from the early 1900s to 1925. They organized against the growing influence of Italian gangsters, choosing their name as a response to the Sicilian Black Hand gangs. Known for being virulently anti-Italian and particularly violent, the gang suffered from unstable leadership and internal killings, leading to its demise.

org:marginals.description: Paddy Irish gang, was a New York street gang active in the early 1900s. Based on the Lower West Side of Manhattan, they were involved in extortion and labor slugging. Under leader Thomas F. 'Tanner' Smith, they succeeded the Hudson Dusters in their territory. The gang's reign ended with Smith's murder in 1919.

org:shapiro-brothers.description: The Shapiro Brothers were a group of Jewish-American mobsters from New York City, based in Williamsburg, who led a faction in the garment industry. They were involved in a violent conflict with Jacob Shapiro and Louis Buchalter for control of the industry in Brooklyn. All three brothers were killed by rivals between 1931 and 1934.

org:south-brooklyn-boys.description: The South Brooklyn Boys (SBB) was a New York City street gang formed in the 1950s from several Italian-American neighborhood gangs in South Brooklyn. The gang engaged in assault, drug trafficking, extortion, bookmaking, and murder, and had loose affiliations with the Italian-American Mafia. It was active from the 1950s until the 2000s.

org:mau-maus.description: A 1950s Puerto Rican street gang based in Brooklyn, New York City, formed in 1957 by youths from the Fort Greene Housing Project. Known for its violent activities including murder and drug trafficking, the gang gained notoriety through its leader Nicky Cruz, whose story was documented in the book and film 'The Cross and the Switchblade'. The gang was active until around 1962.

org:fruit-belt-gang.description: Street gang based in Buffalo, New York, founded in the 1960s-1970s in the Fruit Belt area. Primarily African American, it engages in drug trafficking, weapons smuggling, and murder. The gang is in direct opposition to the Central Park Gang and has been involved in ongoing violent conflicts.

org:savage-skulls.description: The Savage Skulls are a mostly Puerto Rican and African American street gang founded in the Hunts Point area of the Bronx, New York, in the late 1960s. They gained popularity in the 1970s and declared war on drug dealers in Hunts Point. The gang was involved in conflicts with rival gangs and appropriated Nazi symbolism. They were featured in a 1979 documentary film.

org:black-spades.description: The Black Spades is a predominantly African-American street gang formed in the Bronxdale Houses in New York City during the late 1960s. It gained popularity in the 1970s and spread to other boroughs and states. In 2019, the gang rebranded as TBS New Direction, a community service group focusing on anti-violence and food distribution.

org:flying-dragons.description: Chinese American street gang prominent in New York City's Chinatown from the 1970s to the early 1990s. Formed in 1967 by immigrants primarily from Hong Kong, they were affiliated with the Hip Sing Tong. Engaged in extortion, kidnapping, murder, racketeering, illegal gambling, and heroin trafficking. The gang was largely dismantled after a 1994 federal indictment of 33 members.

org:westies.description: The Westies were a New York City-based Irish-American organized crime gang operating from the early 1960s until 1988. They were involved in racketeering, drug trafficking, and contract killing, and were partnered with the Italian-American Mafia. Based in Hell's Kitchen, Manhattan, they were responsible for 60-100 murders between 1968 and 1986.

org:ghetto-brothers.description: The Ghetto Brothers were a gang and music group founded in New York City's South Bronx in the late 1960s. They were involved in politics, particularly the Puerto Rican independence movement, and aimed to uplift young Latino and Black men. The gang spread to much of the Northeastern United States and later merged with the Savage Nomads to form Los Solidos in Connecticut.

sources.title: Ghetto Brothers Organization/Yates Boys Organization - Chicago Gang History

org:east-harlem-purple.description: The East Harlem Purple Gang was a group of Italian-American hitmen and heroin dealers who dominated distribution in East Harlem and the Bronx during the late 1970s–early 1980s. Known for extreme violence including dismembering victims, they reportedly killed at least 17 people by December 1977. Surviving members were absorbed into the Lucchese crime family's 116th Street Crew.

org:savage-nomads.description: The Savage Nomads were a mostly Puerto Rican and African American street gang founded in the South Bronx, New York City in 1970 by Benjamin Melendez. Active primarily in the 1970s, the gang engaged in small crimes and battles with rival gangs such as the Seven Immortals and Savage Skulls. After a period of dormancy, the gang resurfaced in 2017 with homicides linked to them.

org:10th-street-gang.description: A street gang based in Buffalo, New York, formed in the 1980s in the Lower West Side. The gang is multicultural with a Hispanic majority and operates without a traditional leader. It gained notoriety for a violent rivalry with the 7th Street Gang, resulting in 38 homicides between 2009 and 2012. In 2011, 27 members were arrested on RICO charges.

org:supreme-team.description: Organized crime syndicate based in South Jamaica, Queens, New York City, founded in 1981 by Kenneth 'Supreme' McGriff and his nephew Gerald 'Prince' Miller. Specialized in crack cocaine distribution, generating over $200,000 daily at its 1987 peak. Known for extreme violence and murder to control drug trade. Leaders were convicted and sentenced to life in prison.

org:decepticons-gang.description: The Decepticons were a Brooklyn street gang active from the mid-1980s into the early 1990s. Named after the antagonists of the Transformers franchise, they were known for flash-mob style attacks and muggings. The gang originated in Brooklyn and expanded across New York City, with offshoots in Queens and the Bronx. Some former members later became notable in hip-hop music.

org:born-to-kill-gang.description: Born to Kill was a New York City-based street gang composed of first-generation Vietnamese immigrants, founded in 1988 by Tho Hoang "David" Thai. Active primarily in the 1980s and early 1990s, the gang engaged in drug trafficking, counterfeiting, extortion, and murder. Its main base of operations was Canal Street in Manhattan's Chinatown. The gang was largely dismantled by 1992 following arrests and prosecutions of its members.

org:tanglewood-boys.description: The Tanglewood Boys was an Italian-American recruitment gang or farm team for the Lucchese crime family, operating from the Tanglewood Shopping Center in Yonkers, New York. Active from the 1990s to the 2000s, the gang was involved in murders, assaults, armed robbery, arsons, and bookmaking in Westchester County, the Bronx, and the Upper West Side. Many members were sons of made men.

org:albanian-boys.description: The Albanian Boys were a gang of Albanians operating in the Bronx, New York, founded in 1991. Initially a youth ethnic street gang, they evolved into subcontractors for organized crime, engaging in extortion, intimidation, protection money, and debt collection. The gang was active through the 1990s.

org:dominicans-don-t-play.description: Dominicans Don't Play (DDP) is a Dominican-American street gang formed in Manhattan, New York, in 1991. Known for using machetes and knives, they are involved in drug trafficking, robbery, extortion, and murder. The gang operates across New York City, New Jersey, and internationally, with ties to Colombian cartels for cocaine distribution.

org:nine-trey-gangsters.description: The Nine Trey Gangster Bloods (NTG) are a set of the United Blood Nation formed in 1993 at Rikers Island, operating on the East Coast. They initially sold crack, heroin, and PCP in Harlem from abandoned buildings on Lenox Avenue. Gained notoriety through the 2018 Tekashi 6ix9ine RICO case where the rapper turned state's evidence. They maintain an alliance with the Lucchese crime family for smuggling drugs into East Jersey State Prison. Multiple federal operations (Operation Nine Connect, 83-member indictment) have targeted the gang.

sources.title: Lucchese crime family New Jersey faction — Wikipedia

org:rudaj-organization.description: The Rudaj Organization was an Albanian-American gang active in the New York City metro area, founded in 1993 by Alex Rudaj and Nardino Colotti. It engaged in extortion, illegal gambling, and murder, operating in Queens, The Bronx, and Westchester County. The organization was dismantled after a 2004 federal indictment that charged 22 members.

org:trinitarios.description: Dominican American criminal organization founded in 1993 on Rikers Island, New York City, by Leonides Sierra and Julio Marine. Initially formed to protect Dominican and Hispanic inmates, it expanded into drug trafficking, murder, and robbery across the northeastern US and internationally. Despite major prosecutions in 2009-2014, the group experienced a resurgence with internal factional violence.

org:sex-money-murder.description: Sex Money Murder (SMM) is a Blood set and one of the original subgroups of the United Blood Nation, formed on September 27, 1995 in the Soundview Houses in the Bronx by Peter 'Pistol Pete' Rollock. Rollock was sentenced to life at ADX Florence in 2000 for seven murders, held under special administrative measures due to fears his orders would be carried out by members. SMM has spread to Brooklyn, Long Island, Charlotte, Raleigh, and Georgia. They gained independence from UBN after the 'Wet T-Shirt War' and operate as a fully independent Blood set.

org:31-gang.description: A street gang based in East Buffalo, New York, founded in the early 2000s and named after the ID number of the Harriet Ross Tubman School. The gang operates in the Willert Park and Broadway-Fillmore areas, primarily involved in drug trafficking, including cocaine and crack cocaine. Members have faced federal charges, and the gang has been active in conflicts with law enforcement.

org:7th-street-gang.description: The 7th Street Gang was a Hispanic street gang based in Buffalo, New York, founded in 2000 by Efrain "Cheko" Hidalgo. It engaged in drug trafficking of cocaine, heroin, and other narcotics, and was involved in violent conflicts with rival gangs, particularly the 10th Street Gang. The gang was active from 2000 to 2017, when many members were arrested.

org:lrgp.description: LRGP (Lester, Ralph, George, Playter) was a street gang based in the Broadway-Fillmore area of Buffalo, New York, founded in the 2000s. Primarily involved in drug trafficking including marijuana and crack cocaine, they engaged in violent conflict with the rival Bailey Boys. The gang was heavily targeted by law enforcement, leading to multiple arrests and its eventual disbandment by 2016.

org:schuele-boys.description: Street gang based in Buffalo, New York, founded in 2000 on Schuele Avenue. Engages in drug trafficking, robbery, extortion, contract killing, arms trafficking, and selling stolen property. Known for producing drill rap music under the label 'Gone Entertainment'. In 2017, 28 members were arrested for narcotics distribution and homicides.

org:cblbfl.description: American street gang based in Buffalo, New York, founded in 2009 in the Towne Gardens Housing Complex. Engages in drug trafficking (heroin, fentanyl, cocaine, crack, marijuana) and weapons racketeering. Involved in turf wars with rival gangs, including the Ronnie Gang. Members have been convicted under RICO statutes.

org:bailey-boys.description: Street gang based in Buffalo, New York, founded around 2011 and named after Bailey Avenue. Known for murder, robbery, drug trafficking, and racketeering. The gang gained notoriety after a 2012 shooting in Martin Luther King Jr. Park. Its leader Kenneth Pettway Jr. was arrested in 2018 on federal drug and RICO charges.

org:black-tuna-gang.description: The Black Tuna Gang was a Miami-based drug smuggling organization in the 1970s, led by Robert Platshorn and Robert Meinster. They imported an estimated 500 tons of marijuana into the United States over 16 months. The gang was known for sophisticated methods, including radio code words and surveillance countermeasures. They were dismantled by a joint FBI-DEA operation, resulting in convictions of eight members.

org:texas-syndicate.description: The Texas Syndicate is a criminal prison gang that originated in Folsom State Prison, California in the early 1970s. Predominantly Hispanic, it was formed to protect Texas-born inmates from other prison gangs.

org:hermandad-de-pistoleros-latinos.description: Mexican American prison gang founded by Chino Avitia in Texas during the early 1980s. It operates in Texas prisons and communities, particularly Laredo, and is active in Mexico with a large contingent in Nuevo Laredo. The gang has over 1,000 members and is involved in trafficking cocaine and marijuana from Mexico into the United States.

org:miami-boys.description: The Miami Boys was a drug trafficking organization based in Liberty City, Miami, Florida, founded in 1982 by Issac Hicks and James Sawyer. Structured like organized crime with ranks and territories, it expanded into multiple cities across the South and Midwest. The gang was largely eliminated in Atlanta in 1996 and the last dealer in Jacksonville was arrested in 1999.

org:metz.description: The Metz Gang was a drug ring founded by Glenn Metz and his brother Cordell Metz, active from 1985 to 1992 in New Orleans. It distributed approximately 1,000 kilograms of cocaine in the New Orleans metropolitan area and committed murders, attempted murders, and other violent crimes. The gang was dismantled after a 1993 conviction of its leaders.

org:barrio-azteca.description: Barrio Azteca is a Mexican-American criminal organization originating in El Paso, Texas, founded in 1986 as a prison gang. It has grown into a multinational criminal enterprise with thousands of members in the US and Mexico, primarily involved in drug trafficking, murder, and other organized crime.

sources.title: DOJ - Barrio Azteca Leader Pleads Guilty

org:puro-tango-blast.description: Puro Tango Blast is a loose affiliation of regionally based street and prison gangs of primarily Hispanic men from major Texas cities. Formed in the late 1980s within the Texas Department of Criminal Justice, it lacks a strict hierarchy and is classified as a security threat group. Members engage in drug trafficking, robbery, and other crimes.

org:zoe-pound.description: Haitian-American street gang founded in 1990 in Miami, Florida, initially as a vigilante self-defense group. Engages in drug trafficking, robbery, murder, prostitution, and racketeering. Expanded across Florida and other states, with territory reaching Virginia by 2006. A faction called ZMF was involved in the 2018 murder of XXXTentacion.

sources.title: No Limit Soldiers %28organized crime group%29 — Wikipedia

org:hidden-valley-kings.description: Neighborhood-based gang in Charlotte, North Carolina, formed in the late 1990s to early 2000s as a branch of the Queen City Kings. Engages in drug trafficking, extortion, racketeering, robbery, murder, and other criminal activities. The gang is still active.

sources.title: Taking Down Hidden Valley Kings — WBTV

org:d-block-boys.description: American criminal street gang based in Algiers, New Orleans, Louisiana, formed by teenagers from the DeGaulle Manor Public Housing Complex and Fischer Projects. Active from 2004 to 2017, involved in drug trafficking, multiple murders, and violent crimes. The gang was tracked by NOPD since the mid-2000s and listed as a threat by the FBI in 2011.

org:byrd.description: The Byrd Gang is a criminal organization based in New Orleans, Louisiana, originating from the Magnolia Projects. Active since 2006, it is involved in drug trafficking, murder, theft, and kidnapping. The gang has been described as one of the most murderous in the city and has been the subject of federal RICO indictments.

org:110ers.description: The 110ers are a violent criminal organization based in New Orleans, Louisiana, operating primarily in the 10th and 11th Wards. Founded in the 2000s, the gang is involved in drug trafficking, murder, theft, and kidnapping. They have been under federal investigation since 2010 and were indicted in 2013 for multiple killings, including the death of a child.

org:90s-westcoast-crips-in-south-los-ang.description: Westcoast Orchard Blocc Crip are a predominately African-American street gang from South Los Angeles, California. Their neighborhood is located between 91st and 95th Streets from Vermont Avenue to Hoover.

org:east-side-hustler-crips-from-south-l.description: East Side Hustler Crips are a predominately African-American street gang located on the eastside of South LA occupying the neighborhood around 104th Street and 108th Streets between Avalon (east) and Main Street (west).

org:venice-shoreline-crips.description: The Venice Shoreline Crips (VSLC) are a Crip set based in Venice's Oakwood neighborhood — originally a 'servant's zone' and one of the few places near California's coastline where blacks could own property. Founded in the late 1960s-early 1970s alongside the original Crip gangs. Warred with Culver City 13 and Venice 13 over the drug trade in the 1990s. In 1980, a member murdered Senator Abraham Ribicoff's niece.

org:90-neighborhood-crips.description: The 90 Neighborhood Crips are a predominantly African-American Crip set operating around 90th Street in South Los Angeles under the Neighborhood Crips card.

org:115-neighborhood-crips.description: 115 Neighborhood Crips are a predominately African-American street gang partially located in the City of Hawthorne and Unincorporated Los Angeles County. Although small in geographic size, it’s turf falls into two jurisdictions just west of Los Angeles Southwest College and adjacent to the 111 Neighborhood Crips and the Imperial Village Crips.

org:111-neighbor-hood-crips-in-south-los.description: The 111 Neighbor Hood Crips are a predominately African-American street gang located in South Los Angeles west of Western Avenue off 111th Street. They were founded around 1973 or 1974 and are among one of the oldest Crip gangs on the Westside of Los Angeles.

org:112-neighborhood-crips.description: The 112 Neighborhood Crips are an African-American Crip set based in the West Athens area of South Los Angeles, with territory centered around 112th Street between Western and Van Ness Avenues.

org:357-crips.description: Seven are primarily an African-American criminal street gang located in the Valwood neighborhood, on the West Side of Pomona, California.

org:67-neighborhood-crips.description: 67 NeighborHood Crips are a predominately African-American Street gang in South Los Angeles located on 67th Street between Normandie and Vermont Avenue on the northeast side of Eight Tray Gangster Crip’s turf.

org:dorner-blocc-criminal-gang.description: The Dorner Blocc Crips (DBC) also known as D-Blocc, are primarily, but not exclusively, an African-American street gang located on the East Side in the Edgemont neighborhood of Moreno Valley, region of Riverside County, California.

org:garden-blocc-crips.description: The Garden Blocc Crips (GBC) are primarily, but not exclusively, an African-American street gang located in the Meadowview, neighborhood of Sacramento, California.

org:wdcc.description: The Neighborhood Don't Care Crips are an African-American Crip set in South Los Angeles, part of the Neighborhood Crips alliance which is historically allied with the East Coast Crips.

org:rollin-40s-neighborhood-crips.description: Rollin 40s Neighborhood Crips are a predominately African-American street gang located in South Los Angeles from the community of Leimert Park (west) to the 110 Freeway (east) between Martin Luther King Jr. Blvd (north) and Vernon Avenue (south).

org:rollin-60s-neighborhood-crips.description: The Rollin' 60s Neighborhood Crips are one of the largest Crip sets with ~1,600 members, formed in 1976 in Hyde Park, Los Angeles from the Westside Crips. Named after West 60th Street between Slauson and Florence Avenues. Described in 2004 as 'the largest black criminal street gang in the City of Los Angeles.' Members use Seattle Mariners and Chicago White Sox logos. Nipsey Hussle was a member (killed by a fellow member in 2019). In 2025, leader Eugene 'Big U' Henley Jr. was arrested for running a 'mafia-like' organization including kidnapping and murder. Rivals include Eight Tray Gangster Crips (feud started in 1979 over a girl) and Hoover Criminals.

edges.evidence: Known for conflicts with Rollin' 60s Neighborhood Crips and a strong CK/GDK posture.

7 Mile Playboy Gangster Crips vs. 7 Mile Rollin 60s — One of Detroit's most documented Crip-on-Crip feuds. Resulted in multiple shootings and large federal cases: 16 PBGC members convicted, 12 R60s members convicted
The Rollin 60s Crips in Detroit are documented as a predominantly African American, local chapter of the nationwide Crip alliance

The organization became the subject of a major federal investigation in the late 2010s, centered on an extended violent conflict with the 7 Mile Playboy Gangster Crips, a feud that resulted in multiple deaths, including bystanders.
Reported rivalries include: Neighborhood Crip sets, primarily the Rollin 60s
Longstanding conflicts have been documented between Crip—affiliated sets and major local criminal street gang organizations such as the Rollin 60s Neighborhood Crips
PBF, has been identified in community and public reporting as Crip-aligned through association with the Rollin 60s Neighborhood Crips

The organization became the subject of a major federal investigation in the late 2010s, centered on an extended violent conflict with the 7 Mile Playboy Gangster Crips, a feud that resulted in multiple deaths, including bystanders.
Historical tension with Rollin 60s Neighborhood Crips and Rollin Neighborhood Crips (2X/Rollin)-aligned crews

org:school-yard-crips.description: School Yard Crips are a predominately African-American neighborhood located in the Mid-Town section of Los Angeles located between Venice and Washington. In the 1970s there was a short lived Blood gang located in this community called Mid Town Family. During the late 1970s, when dancer crews were extremely popular, the Sharp Dancers formed in this community, then eventually some of these dancers transformed into the School Yard Crips.

org:lynwood-neighborhood-crips.description: The Lynwood Neighborhood Crips (LNHC) also known as the Lynwood N-Hood, are primarily, but not exclusively, an African-American street gang located on the East Side of Lynwood, California.

org:rollin-90s-neighborhood-crips.description: The Rollin' 90s Neighborhood Crips are a Crip set on the west side of South Los Angeles. They either branched off from or allied with the Rollin' 60s Crips (the '0' alliance formed at a 1983 party). Their rivalry with the Eight Tray Gangster Crips (shared with the 60s) defines much of their conflicts. They expanded to Nashville in the mid-1990s (East Nashville Crips, charged with 7 murders). In 2017, three members murdered Justin Holmes (mistaken for Eight Tray), drawing attention because defendant Cameron Terrell was a wealthy white teen. Have also expanded to Montreal, Canada.

org:ghost-town-crips.description: The Ghost Town Crips (GTC) are based in northern Pomona, California, originating in the mid-1980s as a spinoff of the 357 Sin Town Crips in an area of boarded-up shops and abandoned buildings — a 'ghost town' of what was once a thriving downtown.

org:neighborhood-crips-nhc.description: The Neighborhood Crips are originated from the Rollin 60s Neighborhood Crips and are one of the fastest growing organisation in the history of gangs.

org:perris-neighborhood-crips.description: The Perris Neighborhood Crips are a Crips-affiliated street gang based in Perris, Riverside County, part of the expansion of LA-based Crips into the Inland Empire during the late 1980s.

org:rollin-50s-crips.description: Crips are a predominately African-American street gang from the westside of South Los Angeles, California. They are located between Vermont (east) and Western Avenues (west) from Slauson Avenue (south) and 54th Street (north). This set is a result of three different independent sets, 55, 57 and 58 unifying during the late 1990s becoming the Rollin 50s Neighborhood Crips.

org:rollin-40s-crips.description: Ride” (album) is a member of the 47th Street Rollin 40’s Neighborhood Crips, in San Diego, California.

edges.evidence: Paybacc Crips vs. Rollin 40s (7-Tel RFC) — Publicly reported mainly as intermittent street disputes, locals cite street fights and verbal online banter.

org:46-neighborhood-crips.description: 46 NeighborHood Crips are a predominately African-American street gang located in the area of 46th Street & Western Avenue. In 2010 the Los Angeles Unified School District built a new middle school, Barack Obama Global Preparation Academy right in the central location of 46 NHC’s territory, between Western Avenue and St. Andrews Place along 46th Street.

org:west-covina-neighborhood-crips.description: The West Covina Crips are a well-known criminal street gang based in West Covina, California. They falls under the Neighborhorhood Crips 2x (Deuces) umbrella, with several subsets in different parts of the country in states such as Oregon, Washington, Nebraska, and Ohio, California.

org:harvard-gangster-crips.description: The Harvard Park Gangster Crips are an African-American Crip set based in the Harvard Park neighborhood of South Los Angeles, operating under the Gangster Crips umbrella.

org:menlo-gangster-crips.description: The Menlo Gangster Crips are an African-American Crip set based around Menlo Avenue in South Los Angeles, operating under the Gangster Crips umbrella.

org:gangster-crips.description: The Gangster Crips are a collection of African-American Crip sets in South Los Angeles distinguished from Neighborhood Crips by their use of the 'Gangster' moniker. They are rivals of all Neighborhood Crip sets and allies of other Gangster Crip sets.

edges.evidence: Primary Rivalries: Rollin/Neighborhood (2X) Crips, Blood sets, Vice Lords

7 Mile Playboy Gangster Crips vs. 7 Mile Rollin 60s — One of Detroit's most documented Crip-on-Crip feuds. Resulted in multiple shootings and large federal cases: 16 PBGC members convicted, 12 R60s members convicted
Paybacc Crips vs. Rollin 40s (7-Tel RFC) — Publicly reported mainly as intermittent street disputes, locals cite street fights and verbal online banter.
Available sources and graffiti documentation indicate long-standing alliances and associations with local Gangster Disciples–aligned groups, including BMG

The organization became the subject of a major federal investigation in the late 2010s, centered on an extended violent conflict with the 7 Mile Playboy Gangster Crips, a feud that resulted in multiple deaths, including bystanders.
The organization is active within the broader 5Point Nation (5PN) and Movin Gang (3X) alliance structure
W7M ETGC is generally understood to be a localized adaptation of the Eight Trey ('8Tray') Gangster Crips, a well-known Gangster Crip identity that originated in Los Angeles
The W/S Paybacc Crips (PBC) are a Gangster Crip–aligned street gang originating from Gardena, California, where they are more formally known as the Gardena Paybacc Crips (GPBC)
Detroit's Gangster Crip (3X / Movin Gang) sets traditionally maintain opposition toward Rollin/Neighborhood (2X) Crips sets
Detroit's Gangster Crip ("3X / Movin Gang") sets traditionally maintain opposition toward Rollin/Neighborhood ("2X") Crips sets and various Blood-affiliated crews.
Available sources and graffiti documentation indicate long-standing alliances and associations with local Gangster Disciples–aligned groups, including BMG
Detroit's Rollin/Neighborhood (2X) Crips traditionally maintain opposition toward Gangster Crip (3X / Movin Gang) sets

org:88agc.description: Eastside Avalon Gardens Crips are a predominately African-American street gang located in the Avalon Gardens Housing Projects built and administered by the City of Los Angeles.

org:rollin-40s-avalon-gangster-crips.description: Rollin 40s Avalon Gangster Crips Skip to content Menu Home Hoods Asian Gangs Barrios Los Angeles Barrios Compton Varrios LA Varrios Long Beach Varrios Orange County Gangs Costa Mesa Santa Ana Gangs Bloods Pirus Carson Bloods Compton Bloods Hawthorne Bloods Inglewood Bloods L.

org:118th-gangster-crips.description: The 118 Gangster Crips are an African-American Crip set under the Gangster Crips card, based in Hawthorne, California. Formed in the 1980s, they rival the Hawthorne Piru and Acacia Blocc Hustlers.

sources.title: Team 118th Gangster Crips 118th Gangster Crips — StreetGangs

org:73-gangster-crips.description: 73 Gangster Crips are a predominately African-American street gang located in South Los Angeles in the area from the Broadway (west) to Avalon (east) between Florence (north) and 75th Street (south).

org:112bgc.description: Broadway Gangster Crips are a predominately African-American street gang located near Broadway and 112th Street in South Los Angeles with a close relationship to the 52 Broadway Gangster Crips.

org:116-avalon-gangster-crips.description: Avalon Gangster Crips Skip to content Menu Home Hoods Asian Gangs Barrios Los Angeles Barrios Compton Varrios LA Varrios Long Beach Varrios Orange County Gangs Costa Mesa Santa Ana Gangs Bloods Pirus Carson Bloods Compton Bloods Hawthorne Bloods Inglewood Bloods L.

org:4-tray-gangster-crips.description: Tray Gangster Crips are an active African-American street gang located on the East Side of South Los Angeles, California. The 4-Tray Gangster Crips were established by former member’s of the East Side Crips, and originated from the Eight Tray Gangster Crips in the 1970’s.

org:43gcss.description: African-American Gangster Crip set located on the eastside of South Los Angeles in an area known as the Low Bottoms. Also known as Four Tray Gangster Crips Southside.

org:47-gangster-crips.description: The 47 Gangster Crips fall under the East Side Crips/Gangster Crips card and are allies of most gangs who claim the same.

org:52-broadway-gangster-crips-los-angel.description: Cs is a predominately Black American street gang that formed near 52nd Street and Broadway in South LA during the mid 1970s.

org:71-gangster-crips.description: Gangster Crips Skip to content Menu Home Hoods Asian Gangs Barrios Los Angeles Barrios Compton Varrios LA Varrios Long Beach Varrios Orange County Gangs Costa Mesa Santa Ana Gangs Bloods Pirus Carson Bloods Compton Bloods Hawthorne Bloods Inglewood Bloods L.

org:broadway-gangster-crips.description: The Broadway Gangster Crips consist of two sub-sets the 52 Broadway Gangster Crips and the 112 Broadway Gangster Crips.

org:42gc.description: Gangster Crips, 42(es) are a predominately African-American street gang located on the Eastside of Los Angeles in the historic jazz community on Central Avenue.

sources.title: Team Eastside Four Deuce Gangster Crips — StreetGangs

org:four-tray-gangster-crips-43-gc-los-a.description: Eastside Four Tray Gangster Crips are an African-American street gang located on the Eastside of South Los Angeles in the old historic Central Avenue community where the Westcoast Jazz movement flourished during the 1940s and 1950s.

sources.title: Eastside Four Tray Gangster Crips — StreetGangs

org:mad-ass-gangster-crips.description: Mad Ass Gangster Crips (MAGC) are an African-American street gang located on the West Side of Inglewood, California.

org:marvin-gangster-crips.description: The Marvin Gangster Crips fall under the 3X Gangster Crips (GC) umbrella. During the 1980s, the Marvin Gangsters wore three golf balls emblems o.

org:most-valuable-pimp-gangster-crips-lo.description: An African-American Gangster Crip set based in South Los Angeles, operating under the Gangster Crips umbrella.

sources.title: Most Valuable Pimp Gangster Crips — StreetGangs

org:north-highlands-gangster-crips.description: The North Highlands Gangster Crips are an African-American Crip set based in North Highlands, north of Sacramento, California. They are allied with the Valley Hi Gangster Crips and rival the Beast Mob Bloods and Del Paso Heights Bloods.

org:palm-oak-gangster-crips.description: Oak Gangster Crips (POGC) are predominantly an African-American street gang situated on the east side of Lynwood, California.

org:pbgc.description: West Side Play Boy Gangster Crips are a predominately African-American street gang located in a West LA neighborhood west of La Cienaga Boulevard along Corning Avenue.

org:project-gangster-crips-riverside.description: The Rubidoux Project Crips (PJC) also known as the Project Gangster Crips (PJGC) and the West Side Rubidoux Project Crips or the P-Jays, are primarily, but not exclusively, an African-American multigenerational street gang located in the neighborhood of Rubidoux, on the West Side of Riverside, California.

org:trouble-gangster-crips.description: Nothing But Trouble (NBT), are an African-American street gang located on the West Side of South Los Angeles, California.

org:valley-hi-gangster-crips.description: Valley Hi Crips (VHC) are a criminal street gang named after the “Valley Hi” Community located in the Southern region of Sacramento, California.

org:87-gangster-crips.description: Gangster Crips are a predominately Black American gang located on the Eastside of South Los Angeles in the area of Manchester and Main Street.

org:118-eucalyptus-mob-gangster-crips.description: The 118 Eucalyptus Mob Gangster Crips (118EMGC) are a predominantly African-American Crip set based around 118th Street in South Los Angeles, also known as the 118 Gangster Crips after merging with a neighboring set.

org:96-gangster-crips.description: Gangster Crips (or ES 96GC) are an African American street gang formed in the early 1980’s in Los Angeles, California claiming the Gangster Crips and East Side Crips card. Their rivals include the Be-Bopp Watts Bishops, 92 Elm Street Pirus and the 99 Watts Mafia Crips. They are allies of all Gangster Crips.

org:98-gangster-crips.description: Gangster Crips are an African American street gang in Los Angeles, California founded in the 1980’s under the Gangster Crips, East Side Crip card. They are considered rivals of the Denver Lane Bloods, Hoover Criminals (in particular the 92 and 94 set), 98 Main Street Mafia Crips and the Eight Tray Gangster Crips.

org:mansfield-gangster-crips-los-angeles.description: African-American Gangster Crip set based along Mansfield Avenue between Venice and Olympic Boulevards on the Westside of Los Angeles.

org:by-yourself-gangster-crips-west-la-l.description: By Yourself Hustler Crips (BYHC) are an African-American Gangster Crip set based on the West Side of Los Angeles, California, active in the Faircrest Heights, Miracle Mile, and Palms areas.

sources.title: Team By Yourself Gangster Crips — StreetGangs

org:51-trouble-gangster-crips.description: 51 Trouble Gangster Crips are a predominately African-American street gang and one of the few Crips gangs that previously existed during the early 1990s as a non-Crip and non-gang-related street organization known as.

org:naughty-nasty-gangster-crips.description: The Naughty Nasty Gangster Crips are an African-American Crip set in South Los Angeles notable for adopting brown as their primary color rather than the traditional Crip blue.

org:avalon-40s-crips.description: Avalon 40s Crips is a predominately African-American street gang on the eastside of South Los Angeles near Avalon & Martin Luther King Blvd.

org:avalon-ganster-crips-los-angeles.description: The 116 Avalon Gangster Crips are a predominantly African-American Crip set based on the East Side of South Los Angeles around 116th Street and Avalon Boulevard, under the Gangster Crips umbrella.

org:back-street-crips.description: African-American Crip set based in South Los Angeles, emerged during the late 1970s–early 1980s as one of many smaller Crip sets in South Central.

org:centinela-park-compton-crips.description: Willowbrook is a predominately African-American gang based just north of the city of Compton on the border in Willowbrook. They were formed around 1970 as the Carver Park Boys going back to the days when they functioned with the youth in the Nickerson Gardens.

org:fudge-town-mafia-crips.description: An African-American Crip set based in the Watts area of South Los Angeles, controlling territory from Wilmington to Weigand Avenues between 102nd and 108th Streets. Part of the broader Mafia Crips alliance.

org:grandee-compton-crips.description: Grandee Compton Crips were a predominately African-American street gang located in Compton, California that started during the early 1970s right off Grandee Street by Arthur “Big Hauncho” Day and Mack Thomas. They were one of the first Crip street gangs in Compton creating their own specific identity by taking on the name Grandee, after a street in southwest Compton. Both Day and Thomas lived in Watts before their families relocated to Compton in the early 1970s.

org:hat-watts-crips.description: Hat Gang Watts Crips are a predominately African-American street gang located in the unincorporated area of Los Angeles County near Watts. HGW started in the early 1970s by Killer Hank, Mr Rome, Dre (Sistrunk), L.A. Slim (Havest), Hat Gang Wayne, Big Blue, Mookie, Savage, and Wizard.

org:original-front-hood-compton-crips.description: Original Front Hood Compton Crips are a predominately Black gang located in the city of Compton founded in the early 1970s. This set started off as the Original Front Brothers.

sources.title: Original Front Hood Compton Crip — StreetGangs

org:ten-line-gangster-crips.description: African-American Gangster Crip set based on the West Side of South Los Angeles, emerged in the late 1970s–early 1980s as the Gangster Crip faction grew as a counterpart to the Neighborhood Crips.

org:front-street-watts-crips.description: Eastside Front Street Watts Crips are a predominately African-American street gang in the Watts section of south Los Angeles.

org:grape-watts-crips.description: The Grape Street Watts Crips are a Crip set based in the Jordan Downs housing project in Watts, formed in the 1970s. Uniquely identified by wearing purple rather than traditional Crip blue. Their rivalry with the Bounty Hunter Bloods (Nickerson Gardens) has been called 'the most violent and long lasting feud between two gangs in the Watts area.' In 2008, the murder of veteran member Brandon 'BL' Bullard by East Coast Crips sparked a war resulting in 9 dead and 26 wounded in six weeks. In 2008, a federal indictment charged members with distributing PCP. Active in California, Tennessee, and New Jersey.

org:pj-watts-crips.description: The PJ Watts Crips (Project Boys) are a predominantly African-American Crip set based in the Imperial Courts housing project in Watts, South Los Angeles. Founded in the early 1970s, they were one of the three gangs central to the historic 1992 Watts Truce alongside the Grape Street Crips (Jordan Downs) and Bounty Hunter Bloods (Nickerson Gardens). They are allied with the Bounty Hunters against their mutual rival the Grape Street Crips.

org:watts-franklin-square-crips.description: Watts Franklin Square Crips (WFC) are a small gang on the east side of Watts Ca, located on 101st and 102nd st, between Success Ave and Compton Ave.

org:117-street-watts-crips.description: Street Watts, are primarily an active African-American street gang located on the East Side in the Willowbrook/Watts, section of Los Angeles, California.

org:anzac-grape-compton-crips.description: The Anzac Grape Compton Crips (AGCC) are a small African-American street gang, located on the North Side of Compton (Willowbrook district), California.

org:chester-street-compton-crips.description: Chester Blocc Compton Crips (CBCC) are a small African-American street gang that originated on the east side of Compton, California.

org:neighborhood-compton-crips.description: The Neighborhood Compton Crips (NHCC) are an African American street gang located on the East Side, South of Compton, California.

org:palm-and-blocc-compton-crips.description: Palmer Blocc Compton Crips are a predominately African-American street gang located on the eastside of Compton and they are among one of the oldest Crip gangs in Compton going back to the mid 1970s.

sources.title: Palm and Blocc Compton Crips — StreetGangs

org:palmer-blocc-compton-crips.description: The Palmer Blocc Compton Crips (PBCC) are primarily an African-American street gang, located on the West Side of Compton, California.

org:pvcc.description: Wilmington Arms Apartments Park Village Compton Crips are a predominately African-American and Samoan street gang located on the westside of Compton.

org:raymond-street-hustler-compton-crips.description: Raymond Street Hustlers (RSH) are a small street gang located on the west side of Compton, California.

sources.title: Raymond Street Hustler Compton Crips — UnitedGangs

org:south-side-compton-crips-sscc.description: The South Side Compton Crips (SSCC) formed in the 1970s in the area west of Long Beach Boulevard in Compton — the only unclaimed territory at the time. They are notorious for the 1996 murder of Tupac Shakur: member Orlando Anderson shot Tupac after being beaten by the Death Row Records entourage at the MGM Grand. Anderson was killed in 1998 in a gunfight with Corner Poccet Crips. In 2023, Duane 'Keefe D' Davis was arrested for the Tupac murder. In 2003, SSCC members killed Yetunde Price, half-sister of Venus and Serena Williams, while defending a crack house.

sources.title: Team South Side Compton Crips — StreetGangs

org:tpcc.description: Westside Tragniew Park Compton Crips is a predominately African-American street gang located on the westside of Compton.

org:harbor-city-crips.description: Harbor City Crips (HCC) are primarily, but not exclusively, an African-American street gang located on the West Side of Harbor City, California. Their neighborhood stretches from Vermont Ave to Western Ave, between Lomita Blvd and Anaheim Street.

org:wattsmafiacrip.description: Eastside 99 Watts Mafia Crips are a predominately African American street gang located in the area of Watts / Florence in South Los Angeles.

org:blue-gate-mafia-crips.description: The Blue Gate Mafia Crips (BGMC) are predominantly an African-American street gang located on the East Side in the Watts district of South Los Angeles, California.

org:original-swamp-compton-crips.description: Original Swamp Compton Crips are a predominately African-American street gang in West Rancho Dominguez (formerly West Compton, aka Rosewood), an unincorporated section of Los Angeles County.

org:nutty-blocc-compton-crips.description: The Nutty Blocc Compton Crips are a set of the Crips gang alliance based in Compton, California. They are one of the larger Crip sets in Compton and have been involved in gang wars, notably with the ATF alliance in 1995. Several influential rappers have been associated with the gang.

org:farm-dog-compton-crips.description: Farm Dog Compton Crips are a predominately Black street gang in the City of Compton. Their territory is in an agricultural section of Compton where it is not uncommon to see many farm animals in the yards of the homeowners including many horses.

org:santana-blocc-compton-crips.description: Eastside Santana Blocc Compton Crips is a predominately African-American street gang located on the Eastside of the City of Compton. Prior to the formation of Santana Blocc Crip, there were two other neighborhoods that existed, Oak Parks Business Men (Oaks Park Boys) and Mid Town which were active during the early 1970s. Circa 1978 the two neighborhoods merged to form into the Santana Blocc Crips. Ken “Yogi” Tate may have initially had the vision to form Santana Blocc Crips, but it wasn’t until after his death the merger was solidified.

org:atlantic-blocc-compton-crips.description: Acacia Block Compton Crips are a predominately African-American street gang in the City of Compton in Los Angeles County.

org:atlantic-drive-compton-crips.description: The Atlantic Drive Compton Crips are a Crip set based in Compton named after Atlantic Drive. They have been at war with the Santana Blocc Compton Crips since the 1980s after a drug deal went bad, a feud that led to multiple drive-by shootings and death penalty convictions.

org:atlantic-gardens-compton-crips.description: Anzac Grape Compton Crips are a predominately African-American street gang located in the norther part of the City of Compton.
