Being Crips, they are rivals with all the Blood and Piru neighborhoods in the area, and their main Crip rivals are the Original Front Hood Compton Crips.

org:ducky-hood-compton-crips.description: African-American Crip set based in Compton, California, emerged during the 1970s–1980s as the Crip alliance expanded across the city. Part of the network including Kelly Park and Neighborhood Compton Crips.

org:hickery-watts-crip-los-angeles.description: Hickery Street Watts Crip (HSWC) is a predominately Black American street gang from the Watts section on the eastside of South Los Angeles. It is the first Crip gang ever established in Watts founded by Tyrone Tate.

org:lantana-blocc-compton-crips.description: Westside Lantana Blocc Compton Crips are a predominately African-American street gang located on the westside of Compton.

org:macon-macc-compton-crips.description: African-American Crip set based in Compton, California, named after Macon Street. Emerged during the late 1970s–1980s in the dense network of Compton Crip gangs.

org:mona-parcc-compton-crips-in-willowbr.description: Mona Parcc Compton Crips are a predominately African-American street gang located in the unincorporated section of Los Angeles County known as Willowbrook which is just north of the City of Compton. They are named for the large County Park, Mona Park, located at 2291 East 121st Street between Mona Boulevard and Willowbrook Avenue.

org:nestor-ave-compton-crips.description: African-American Crip set based along Nestor Avenue on the west side of Compton, California, formed during the late 1970s–1980s Crip expansion.

org:six-hood-compton-crips-in-willowbroo.description: Six Hood Compton Crips In Willowbrook are a predominately Black gang located in the unincorporated section of Willowbrook, just north of Compton.

org:twilight-zone-compton-crips-in-rosew.description: The Twilight Zone Compton Crips are a predominantly African-American Crip set based in the Rosewood Park area of Compton, California.

org:ward-lane-compton-crips.description: African-American Crip set on the East Side of Compton, California, with cliques including the 400, 500, and 800 Blocc. Part of the network of Compton Crip gangs formed during the 1970s–1980s.

org:kelly-park-compton-crips.description: The Kelly Park Compton Crips (KPCC), formerly the Kelly Park Hustler Crips, originated in Kelly Park on the East Side of Compton. Connected to N.W.A — Eazy-E and MC Ren were both members. In 1986, after a cycle of retaliatory murders with the Lueders Park Piru, a task force of 43 police officers raided Kelly Park. A 1986 police substation in Kelly Park that banned gang colors (blue, red, and green for Lime Hood Piru) significantly reduced crime.

org:1st-east-coast-crips.description: East Coast Crips, 1st Street was a predominately African-American street gang located in the Aliso Village housing projects during the 1970s where there was a significant Black population in the projects.

sources.title: Team 1st Street East Coast Crips — StreetGangs

org:69-east-coast-crips.description: Neighborhood East Coast Crips are a predominately African-American street gang located on the Eastside of South Los Angeles. They are among one of the oldest Crip gangs, being founded during the early 1970s when they were simply known as Shack Boys.

org:del-amo-blocc-190-east-coast-crips.description: The Del Amo Blocc 190 East Coast Crips are a predominantly African-American Crip set based in the city of Carson, California, part of the East Coast Crips alliance operating around 190th Street.

sources.title: Del Amo Blocc 190 East Coast Crips — StreetGangs

org:118-blocc-east-coast-crips.description: East Coast Blocc Crips (118ECBC) are an African-American street gang located on the East Side of South Los Angeles, California.

org:118-east-coast-crips.description: East Coast Blocc Crips, are a predominately African-American street gang located in South Los Angeles and part of the East Coast Crips alliance.

org:62-east-coast-crips.description: Deuce East Coast Crips, are primarily an African-American street gang located on the East Side of South Los Angeles, California.

org:89-east-coast-crips.description: The 89 East Coast Crips are a predominantly African-American Crip set operating around 89th Street on the East Side of South Los Angeles, part of the East Coast Crips alliance.

org:68ecc.description: East Coast Crips are a predominately African-American street gang from South Los Angeles, that are part of the “Six Pacc” alliance of East Coast Crips sets including the 62, 66 and 69 ECCs.

org:89ecc.description: Neighborhood East Coast Crips are a predominately African-American street gang on the eastside of South Los Angeles.

org:190-east-coast-blocc-crips.description: The 190 East Coast Blocc Crips are a subset of the East Coast Crips based in the Del Amo area of Carson, California. In 2016, the final defendant in a RICO indictment targeting the East Coast Crips pleaded guilty to federal racketeering involving violent crimes, home invasion robberies, and crack cocaine distribution.

org:1200-east-coast-blocc-crips-riversid.description: The 1200 East Coast Blocc Crips are an African-American Crip subset based on the east side of Riverside, California, with approximately 147 members involved in violent assaults, drug trafficking, and robberies.

sources.title: 1200 East Coast Blocc Crips Riverside — UnitedGangs

org:59-east-coast-crips.description: 59 East Coast Crips alliance. Due to demographic changes in this part of South Los Angeles, which saw the population go from majority Black to majority Latino, the presence of this gang has reduced significantly between 1995 and 2010. According the the 2010 Census, Blacks represented only 11 percent of the residents in zip code 90001, while Latinos represented 88 percent.

org:76ecc.description: Neighborhood East Coast Crips are a predominately African-American street gangs located on 76th Street between Central and Compton Ave on the eastside of South Los Angeles.

org:q102-east-coast-crips.description: The Q102 East Coast Crips originated around 102nd Street, between San Pedro Ave and Avalon Blvd. They falls under the East Coast Crips umbrella and are affiliated with the Neighborhood Crips (2xDeuce) card.

org:taper.description: Compton Taper Street Pirus were a predominately African-American street gang on the westside of Compton during the early 1970s.

org:west-park-pirus.description: West Park Pirus were a predominately African-American street gang located in Compton during the early 1970s.

org:east-side-pirus-compton.description: East Side Pirus are the first Pirus on the eastside of Compton and were formed after the original members of the Westside Pirus, which included Puddin and Bobalouie visited the eastside around 1971.

org:butler-block-pirus.description: Small Piru Blood gang that operated on the west side of Compton, California during the 1970s. Named after Butler Street, they were among the early Piru sets but became defunct by the early 1980s.

org:elm-street-piru.description: The Elm Street Piru is a Blood/Piru set that started in Compton, California and has since spread to Brooklyn, Atlanta, Virginia, Newark, St. Louis, and other cities. In 2009, a father-daughter pair was charged with recruiting high school students across Northern Virginia. In 2020, an Elm Street Piru member shot and killed 8-year-old Secoreia Turner during the Rayshard Brooks protests in Atlanta when her mother's car failed to stop at a gang-erected barricade.

org:fruit-town-piru.description: The Fruit Town Piru (Neighborhood Piru) is a Blood/Piru set formed in the 1970s on the east side of Compton, California, named after fruit-themed streets (Plum, Cherry, Apple, Peach). Active in Fort Worth, Houston, St. Louis, Newark, Norfolk, and Birmingham. They developed a rivalry with Mob Piru in the late 1990s connected to Death Row Records disputes, resulting in several murders. Rappers Chris Brown and Soulja Boy have claimed affiliation.

edges.evidence: BandGang/ShredGang (BGSG), which has been described as maintaining loose affiliations with West Coast–based Blood–affiliated sets such as the Inglewood Family Gangster Bloods (IFGB), Neighborhood Pirus (NHP), and the Centinela Park Family Gangster Bloods (CPFGB)

org:mulberry.description: African-American Piru Blood gang named after Mulberry Street in Compton, part of the Tree Top Piru lineage — Compton Piru sets named after tree-themed streets — emerged in the late 1970s–early 1980s.

org:west-side-pirus-compton.description: West Side Pirus are a predominately African-American street gang located on the westside of Compton, CA. They were originally known as the Piru Street Boys founded around 1969 by Sylvester “Puddin” Scott, Vincent Owens, Lorenzo “LB” Benton, Larry “Tam” Watts and A.C. Moses and others making them one of the oldest Black gangs in LA County. The founders named their gang, Piru, after the street they lived on in the western section of Compton.

org:mob-piru.description: The Mob Piru (East Side Mob Piru) is a Piru/Blood set based on the east side of Compton, California. Suge Knight, co-founder of Death Row Records, was an affiliate and hired many Mob Piru members as label security. Tupac Shakur also became affiliated when he joined Death Row in 1995. The gang split from the Lueders Park Piru and the two developed a rivalry after a murder in 2009. Several Mob Piru members connected to Death Row were murdered in the early 2000s, including Alton 'Buntry' McDonald and Wardell 'Poochie' Fouse (implicated in the murder of Notorious B.I.G.).

org:145-neighborhood-piru.description: The 145 Neighborhood Pirus are primarily an African-American Piru street gang, located on the north side of Compton, California.

org:135-pirus.description: 135 Pirus are a predominately African-American street gang near 135th between Main Street and Avalon. This neighborhood was formed around 1975 in unincorporated Los Angeles County in a small community between Compton to the east and Gardena to the west.

org:east-compton-piru.description: A Blood/Piru gang based on the East Side of Compton, California. They are part of the network of Piru sets in the Compton area.

org:hollyhood-pirus.description: Hollyhood Piru is a predominately Black street gang located on the Eastside of Compton that historically falls under the Lueders Park Pirus like the rest of the Pirus on the eastside. They are named after the street, Holly Avenue.

org:cedar-block-piru.description: The Cedar Block Piru is a Blood/Piru set formed around 1980 on the west side of Compton, named after Cedar Street. Active in St. Louis, Newark, Norfolk, and Richmond. Connected to rapper The Game, who joined in high school despite his parents being Crips. In December 1992, Cedar Block Piru members aided by two Fort Lewis soldiers murdered Allen King and his three children, believing King had reported their drug house.

org:cross-atlantic-pirus.description: The Cross Atlantic Pirus are a Blood gang set based in Compton, California, part of the Piru alliance that formed when young African Americans refused to submit to Crip dominance in the early 1970s, becoming the foundation of the Bloods movement.

org:tree-top-piru.description: The Tree Top Piru (Bompton Tree Top Piru) is a Blood/Piru set formed in the 1970s on the west side of Compton, named after tree-themed streets (Acacia, Poplar, Maple, Spruce, Cedar, Elm). Has spread to Tennessee, Texas, Maryland, and Baltimore. Connected to rappers DJ Quik and YG. In 2007, Baltimore TTP leader Michelle Hebron was sentenced to 30 years for racketeering and murder. In Texas, member Javan Williams received life for two 2017 murders.

org:village-town-piru.description: Ujima Village Piru is a predominately African-American street gang located in the public housing projects in Willowbrook, unincorporated Los Angeles County. Willowbrook is located adjacent to the City of Compton on the northwest side and the Ujima Village homes are located next to Magic Johnson Park.

org:clanton-14th-street-1st-hood-westsid.description: The oldest clique of Clanton 14th Street, one of the oldest Hispanic street gangs in Los Angeles, founded in 1921. Clanton 14 are Sureño-affiliated and were among the founders of the Mexican Mafia. The 18th Street Gang originally formed from members rejected by Clanton 14.

org:clanton-14th-street-c14-3rd-hood-hol.description: The Hollywood Westside Clanton 14 Street is their 3rd of a total of five Clanton barrios in Los Angeles. The original Eastside Clanton neighborhood was founded in the early 1920s and by the 1970s, they formed five solid neighborhoods throughout Los Angeles.

sources.title: Clanton 14th Street (C14)  3rd Hood, Hollywood, CA — StreetGangs

org:clanton-14th-street-4th-hood.description: Approximately in 1975 Clanton started a fifth neighborhood in the North Hollywood area of Los Angeles just west of Burbank. Clanton was one of the first non-Valley neighborhoods to start a neighborhood in the Valley and two brothers from the 2nd Hood, Ernie "Big Cuate" and Ricky were the founders of the North Hollywood varrio.

org:toonerville.description: Toonerville Rifa 13 (TVR) is a Chicano street gang based in Atwater Village, Los Angeles, with approximately 450 members. Named after the area's resemblance to the 'Toonerville Folks' cartoon due to the red line trolley. Mexican Mafia-affiliated Sureño gang. Member Timothy Joseph McGhee was sentenced to death in 2009 for three homicides and suspected in up to twelve murders. Rivals with Armenian Power, The Avenues, Vineland Boys, and MS-13. Has expanded to Glendale and Gwinnett County, Georgia. In 2008, LA and Glendale sought a gang injunction against them.

org:temple-street-tst-in-central-los-ang.description: Varrio Westside Temple Street is a predominately Hispanic gang located in the central area of Los Angeles west of downtown. Founded in the early 1920s, TST is among the oldest street gang in Los Angeles named after one of the oldest streets in Los Angeles. They have a unique history of being a racially diverse gang, especially during the 1970s and 1980s, when they had black, Filipino and White members.

sources.title: Temple Street (TST) in Central Los Angeles — StreetGangs

org:east-side-clover-in-lincoln-heights.description: East Side Clover is a predominately Hispanic Street gang located in the Lincoln Heights community just east of the Los Angeles River. The gang formed on the 600 block of Clover during the early 1900s when Lincoln Heights was a destination locations for Italians, Irish, Russian, Mexicans, Germans, and Japanese immigrants.

sources.title: East Side Clover in Lincoln Heights — StreetGangs

org:lincoln-heights-lhts-gang-in-northea.description: Lincoln Heights (LHTS) is a predominately Mexican-American street gang located in the neighborhood with the same name in northeast Los Angeles, California. The Lincoln Heights neighborhood is among one of Los Angeles’ oldest communities, dating back to the early 1800s. The neighborhood was formed off the banks of the Los Angeles River and grew to the east. The street gang, LHTS, formed between 1910 and 1920 making it one of the oldest active gangs in Los Angeles.

sources.title: Lincoln Heights (LHTS) gang in Northeast Los Angeles, California — StreetGangs

org:rose-hill.description: Rose Hill is a Latino street gang based in the El Sereno/Rose Hills neighborhood of Northeast Los Angeles, near the Rose Hill Courts public housing complex built in the 1940s.

org:playboys-surenos-13.description: South Side Playboys, are a predominantly Mexican-American Sureño street gang that originated in the mid-1950s at the corner of Pico Boulevard and Fedora Street in Los Angeles. Originally a car club called the "Southern California Latin Playboys Car Club."

org:varrio-st-andrews-sta-in-hollywood-a.description: Varrio St. Andrews (STA) is a predominantly Latino street gang located on North St. Andrews Place between Melrose and Beverly in the Hollywood area of Los Angeles. A relatively new varrio, not connected to the defunct neighborhood of the same name in South Central.

sources.title: Varrio St. Andrews (STA) in Hollywood area — StreetGangs

org:varrio-vista-rifa.description: Varrio Vista Rifa is a Latino Sureño-affiliated street gang based in the Vista area of Los Angeles, part of the network of barrio gangs in Southern California.

org:42nd-street-locos.description: A Sureño-affiliated Latino street gang based around 42nd Street in South Los Angeles, one of numerous numbered-street Latino varrios in the South Central corridor.

org:alley-locos.description: Small Latino street gang in the Los Angeles area, part of the broader network of Sureño-affiliated 'Locos' cliques active across LA County since the 1980s.

org:astoria-garden-locos.description: Isai Barrera from Astoria Gardens told Police Jokey from Pacoima Flats had a gun and was with Teodoro Elenes a member of the Project Boys the day Javier Garcia from Brownstones was ked.

org:big-top-locos.description: Big Top Locos is a Latino Sureño-affiliated street gang based in the Los Angeles area, part of the network of 'Locos' cliques operating under Mexican Mafia authority since the 1980s.

org:chevy-chase-locos.description: The chevy chase park area is alot calmer now and beatiful No and the people that live there are new to the area. Most of the gang members are in jail or living regular lives. The few remaining gang members are old and care less about that lifestyle. Basically all the crazy people are gone.

org:haskel-street-locos.description: Latino street gang named after Haskel Street in the West San Fernando Valley, documented among active SFV Hispanic gangs since at least the late 1990s.

org:la-mirada-locos-lml.description: Sureño-affiliated Latino street gang documented by LAPD as active in drug trafficking in the Hollywood and Los Feliz neighborhoods of Los Angeles.

org:locos-park.description: A Latino Sureño-affiliated street gang based in Los Angeles, claiming territory around a neighborhood park area.

org:tokers.description: A Latino Sureño-affiliated street gang based in the East Los Angeles area, one of the older Chicano gangs with roots dating back to the 1950s-1960s.

org:valerio-street-locos.description: A Latino Sureño-affiliated street gang based near Valerio Street in the San Fernando Valley, Los Angeles.

org:washington-locos-aka-washington-boys.description: The Washington Locos (Washington Boys) are a Latino Sureño-affiliated street gang based near Washington Boulevard in the Mid-City/West Adams area of Los Angeles.

org:18th-street-gang.description: Multi-ethnic street gang from Los Angeles, California, founded in the 1960s by Mexican-American and other immigrant youths. One of the largest street gangs in Los Angeles with 30,000–50,000 members across the US, Mexico, and Central America. Engages in drug trafficking, prostitution, extortion, kidnapping, robbery, assault, and murder.

org:42nd-street-lil-criminals.description: Latino street gang based around 42nd Street in South Los Angeles, operating in an area contested by numerous Sureño-affiliated gangs under Mexican Mafia influence.

org:alley-tiny-criminals.description: The Alley Tiny Criminals (ATC) are a Latino street gang and tagging crew originating from the Exposition Park area of South Central Los Angeles, aligned with the Sureño (13) structure.

org:barrio-evil.description: A Latino Sureño-affiliated street gang based in Los Angeles, claiming allegiance to the Mexican Mafia.

org:barrio-mojados.description: They are a Sureño-affiliated barrio gang, with 'Mojados' being a Spanish term referencing immigrants.

org:bronson-bse-from-mid-city-washington.description: Bronson (BSE) are a predominately Mexican-American street gang located in the Mid City / Washington Heights area of Central Los Angeles, California. They are named for the street, Bronson Ave, that runs north and south just east of Crenshaw Blvd.

org:brown-pride-raza.description: Brown Pride Raza is a Sureño-affiliated Latino street gang based in the San Fernando Valley area of Los Angeles, part of the broader Sureño movement aligned with the Mexican Mafia.

org:crazys-cys.description: Meco Poo buhts have no hands,only thing they do have is a big trout mouth for all that meco guzzling.

org:criminals-cls-gang-in-south-west-los.description: They are a relatively new gang compared to other Los Angeles gangs and they are located in an area with low gang activity.

org:eastside-18th-street-northeast-los-a.description: Eastside 18th Street is predominately a Latino street gang located in the northeast section of Los Angeles.

org:eighteen-street-hollywood-gangsters-.description: The Hollywood gangsters (HGS) are a click of Eighteen Street operating within their own territory in Hollywood. They occupy an area along Yucca Street north of Hollywood Boulevard.

org:elmwood-street.description: Elmwood Rifa 13 is a Latino Sureño gang based in the San Fernando Valley. In 2025, five members and associates were arrested on federal charges alleging a murder-for-hire plot.

org:fickette.description: Fickette (Fickett Street) is a Latino street gang based along Fickett Street in the Boyle Heights neighborhood of East Los Angeles, documented by LAPD's Hollenbeck Division as one of its most dangerous corridors.

org:head-hunters.description: Varrio Head Hunters (HHS-13) are a Latino Sureño street gang based in Los Angeles, subject to gang injunctions and documented by law enforcement including U.S. Customs and Border Protection.

org:indiana-dukes.description: Himachal easily surpass all other mound stations in India when it comes to quality holiday time owing to variety of tourism activities.

org:intruders.description: Latino street gang in the greater Los Angeles area, emerging during the 1980s–90s era of gang proliferation in Southern California.

org:lennox-street.description: A Latino Sureño-affiliated street gang based in the Lennox area of unincorporated Los Angeles County, near Inglewood.

org:loas-lost-angeles.description: Latino street gang in Los Angeles whose name references the city, part of the broader network of Sureño-aligned cliques.

org:michigan-chicano-force.description: A Chicano prison gang and street gang based in Michigan, part of the Sureño-affiliated network that expanded from California to the Midwest.

org:montecito-park.description: A Latino street gang based near Montecito Heights in Northeast Los Angeles, a Sureño-affiliated barrio gang.

org:ochentas-80s.description: The Ochentas (Spanish for 'Eighties') are a Latino Sureño street gang based in Los Angeles, operating under the Mexican Mafia-affiliated gang structure in Southern California.

org:opal-street.description: Predominantly Mexican-American street gang named after Opal Street in the Boyle Heights neighborhood of East Los Angeles, also known as Opal Street Locos (OSL), active since the 1980s.

org:orphans.description: A Latino street gang based in the Pico-Union/Westlake area of Los Angeles, near 8th and Union Streets.

org:park-western-lomas.description: Park Western Lomas (PWL 13) was a Latino Sureño-affiliated street gang based in the Park Western area of Los Angeles, operating near public housing projects.

org:poserz-prz.description: Small Los Angeles-area street gang that emerged during the late 1980s–early 1990s party crew era, when numerous social groups across LA transitioned into criminal street gangs.

org:primera-flats-23rd-street-in-south-l.description: Primera Flats is one of the oldest Latino neighborhood gangs in South Central Los Angeles, based around 23rd Street near Santee High School. They were one of the first three gangs targeted by an LA gang injunction in 1982 alongside Dogtown and the 62nd East Coast Crips.

sources.title: Primera Flats 23rd Street in South LA — StreetGangs

org:radford-street.description: A Latino street gang based near Radford Street in the North Hollywood/San Fernando Valley area of Los Angeles.

org:sentinel-boys.description: A street gang based in the Los Angeles area, part of the numerous smaller sets that emerged during the 1980s–1990s gang proliferation era in Southern California.

org:sol-valle-diablos.description: A Latino Sureño-affiliated street gang based in the Sun Valley area of the San Fernando Valley, Los Angeles.

org:soto-street.description: Latino street gang in the Boyle Heights neighborhood of East Los Angeles, named after Soto Street — a major corridor through one of LA's most historically gang-impacted barrios with gangs dating back to the 1940s.

org:state-street.description: A Latino street gang based near State Street in South Los Angeles, part of the network of Sureño-affiliated gangs in the area.

org:street-criminals.description: Latino street gang in Los Angeles, part of the wave of 'criminal'-named Sureño cliques that proliferated in the 1980s–90s.

org:suicidals.description: Gang that originated from the fanbase of Venice Beach crossover thrash band Suicidal Tendencies in the early 1980s, becoming a recognized street gang with multiple affiliate cliques known for violence at punk shows and in their Venice neighborhood.

org:the-magician-club.description: Small Los Angeles street gang that emerged from the party crew scene of the late 1980s–early 1990s, when numerous social crews evolved into criminal street gangs across LA County.

org:the-mob-crew.description: Los Angeles-area street gang that emerged from the party crew scene of the late 1980s–early 1990s, part of the wave of social crews that transitioned into criminal street gangs.

org:tiny-insane-kriminals.description: The Tiny Insane Kriminals (TIK) are a Latino Sureño-affiliated street gang in the Los Angeles area, using the stylized 'K' spelling common among smaller cliques in the Sureño network.

org:unos-sin-verguenza.description: Latino street gang in Los Angeles whose Spanish name translates to 'Shameless Ones,' a Sureño-affiliated clique.

org:vicky-s-town.description: Small Latino street gang in Los Angeles, named in the barrio tradition of claiming a specific neighborhood.

org:vincent-town.description: K vida de este barrio los k fuimos solo nos acordamos los k vivimos en este barrio sabemos lo k hay en las calles de north hills k anos los 1999 al 2001 verdad cuando asias filas para comprar comidad jajajajaja.

org:wabash.description: A Latino street gang based in the Wabash Avenue area of Los Angeles, part of the network of Sureño-affiliated barrio gangs.

org:wanderers.description: The Wanderers 13 are a Latino Sureño-affiliated street gang based on the West Side of Los Angeles, operating in the Pico-Union/Westlake corridor since at least the 1980s.

org:watts-village-boys.description: Street gang based in the Gonzaque Village public housing project in Watts, South Los Angeles, where they and rival Hacienda Village Bloods divide territory along 106th Street.

org:brown-pride-surenos.description: Sureño-affiliated Latino street gang based in the San Fernando Valley, active since at least the late 1990s.

org:ninos-surenos.description: A Latino street gang affiliated with the Sureños in Los Angeles. The name translates to 'Southern Children,' indicating allegiance to the Mexican Mafia.

org:tepatitlan-tepa-in-lennox-ca.description: Tepatitlan (Tepa) is a predominately Latino street gang in the unincorporated area of Lennox in the South Bay portion of Los Angeles County. The varrio was formed on 106th Street and Freeman Avenue during the late 1970s.

org:rebels.description: A Latino Sureño-affiliated street gang with cliques in multiple cities including Omaha and Los Angeles.

org:satanas.description: Filipino American street gang based in Southern California, founded in 1972. Believed to be the oldest Filipino-American street gang in Los Angeles. Involved in gang violence, murder, drug trafficking, and theft. Active across multiple California counties and the Philippines.

org:judas-13.description: Salutations! I would like to join your gang, i feel like i would be a great addition to your Asian family. My mother makes great sushi and i know some Tea Kwan Do.

org:ms-13.description: International criminal gang originating in Los Angeles, California in the 1980s, initially formed to protect Salvadoran immigrants. It expanded through deportations to Central America and is active in the US, Canada, Mexico, and Central America. Engages in drug trafficking, murder, extortion, and other crimes. Designated as a terrorist organization by the US, Canada, and El Salvador.

org:armenian-power.description: Armenian Power is a US-based criminal organization of Armenian origin in Los Angeles County, California. Founded in 1989 by Armen Petrosyan, it is involved in drug trafficking, murder, assault, fraud, identity theft, illegal gambling, kidnapping, extortion, and robbery. The group has strong ties with Russian organized crime and has around 250-750 members. It remains active as of the present.

org:down-insane-malditos-dim.description: Down Insane Malditos (DIM) – Are a predominately Latino street gang located in the West LA section of Los Angeles. Their turf is known as the Cadillac-Corning area that is known for the Play Boy Gangster Crips, a predominately African-American gangs. DIM are relatively new, having been formed around 2005.

org:santa-monica-13.description: Santa Monica 13 (SMG) is one of the oldest gangs on LA's Westside, originating in the late 1920s as the 'Santa Monica Tomato Patch Gang' in the Pico neighborhood. Sureño gang affiliated with the Mexican Mafia. Despite being Sureño, they wear traditional black bandanas. The SM17 (17th Street) clique is their primary subset. Have had historical rivalries with Venice 13, Culver City Boys, 18th Street, and Venice Shoreline Crips, though a truce with Venice 13 was later established. Considered the first gang to originate on the Westside of Los Angeles.

sources.title: StreetGangs - Pico Youth and Family Center

org:diamond-street-near-downtown-los-ang.description: Westside Varrio Diamond Street is a predominately Hispanic street gang located in the Westlake area of Los Angeles just west of Downtown LA. They are one of the few Chicano gangs that continues to sign their graffiti with the phrase "C/S" or "Con Safos," a Chicano phrase that was extremely popular from the 1940s through the 1970s, but began loosing popularity in the 1980s and 1990s.

sources.title: Diamond Street near Downtown Los Angeles — StreetGangs

org:playboys-13.description: The Playboys 13 (PBS13) is a large Sureño street gang with approximately 6,500 members, founded in the mid-1950s as the 'Southern California Latin Playboys Car Club' at Pico Boulevard and Fedora Street in West Los Angeles. Mexican Mafia-affiliated. Has spread to Orange County, Washington, Oregon, Texas, Utah, Pennsylvania, and Mexico. Multiple factions: Westside (original, Pico/Fedora), Eastside (49th Street, South Central), and Southside (Bell Gardens/South Gate). Each clique operates autonomously with no central leadership. Known for their Playboy bunny tattoo/graffiti. Rivals with 18th Street, Florencia 13, and most other Sureño gangs despite shared La Eme affiliation.

org:drifters.description: Central Los Angeles Varrio Drifters (DFS) are a predominately Latino street gang located in the Pico-Union section of Central Los Angeles off Pico & Fedora.

org:burlington-street-locos.description: A Latino Sureño-affiliated street gang based near Burlington Avenue in the Westlake/Pico-Union district of Los Angeles.

org:culver-city-boys.description: Mexican-American street gang from Mar Vista, Los Angeles, California, founded in the 1960s. Engages in murder, drug trafficking, extortion, assault, auto theft, and robbery. Under a civil injunction enforced by the LAPD, but remains active.

org:barrio-st-andrews-bsa.description: A Latino Sureño-affiliated street gang based near St. Andrews Place in the Mid-City/Koreatown area of Los Angeles.

org:harpys-5th-avenue-los-angeles.description: Harpys 5th Avenue is a predominately Hispanic gang neighborhood located between Pico Blvd and Washington Ave just east of Crenshaw Blvd in the Mid City section of Los Angeles. They are located in the Wilshire Division of the LAPD and their closest rival are the Mara Salvatrucha gang located on Bronson & Country Club Avenue just east of Crenshaw Blvd.

org:verdugo-gangsters-san-bernardino.description: The Verdugo Gangsters consists of three Latino subsets Called the WestSide Verdugo, EastSide Verdugo, and the Southside Verdugo, operating in different regions of San Bernardino, California. They made headlines after two of their leaders (brothers) were killed by childhood friends in a quadruple homicide case dubbed “Dead Presidents” by police.

org:aztlan.description: California Varrio Aztlan (AZT) are a predominately Hispanic street gang in the Mid Wilshire area of Los Angeles, California.

org:verdugo-gangsters.description: Side Verdugo (WSV), combined are the Largest Latino gang in the San Bernardino Valley with origins dating back to the 1950s.

org:westside-18th-street-west-adams.description: A clique of the 18th Street Gang operating in the West Adams neighborhood of Los Angeles. The 18th Street Gang is one of the largest transnational criminal organizations with origins in LA's Rampart area in the 1960s.

org:witmer.description: Central Los Angeles Witmer Street (WST) is a predominately Hispanic street gang located in the downtown Los Angeles area just west of the 110 Freeway.

org:sotel.description: West Los Angeles Sotel 13 is a predominately Latino street gang located near Stoner Park and named after the street “Sawtelle” in West Los Angeles.

org:venice.description: Venice 13 (V13) is a Mexican-American street gang based in the Oakwood neighborhood of Venice, Los Angeles, founded in the 1960s after lower-class Mexican-Americans were displaced by freeway construction. They have a long-standing rivalry with the Venice Shoreline Crips.

org:easy-riders-gang.description: Riders are a Mexican-American Sureño criminal street gang established in the 1970s, with geographical cliques located throughout the city of Los Angeles, California.

org:18thstreet-picounion.description: The Pico-Union clique of the 18th Street Gang operates near where the Santa Monica and Harbor freeways intersect around 18th Street and Union Avenue. The 18th Street Gang formed in the 1960s when youths excluded from Clanton 14 created their own organization, growing into one of the largest transnational gangs with 30,000-50,000 members across the US, Mexico, and Central America.

org:18th-street-rimpau-blvd.description: 18th Street – Rimpau Blvd is a predominately Latino street gang located in the Mid Town section of Los Angeles near 18th Street and South Rimpau between Venice and Washington Avenue.

org:18thstreet-westlake.description: The Westlake clique of the 18th Street Gang operates in the MacArthur Park and Westlake district of Los Angeles, west of downtown. In 2026, federal prosecutors charged seven alleged members with racketeering, accusing them of taking orders from a Mexican Mafia member held in state prison.

org:clanton-14th-2nd-hood-los-angeles.description: Hood is predominately Hispanic street gang located in the Mid City area of Los Angeles on the verge of extinction during the early 1990s. This neighborhood is a spinoff of the original Eastside Clanton neighborhood that was formed on Clanton Street during the early 1920s.

org:rockwood.description: Central Los Angeles Rockwood Street Locos (RWST, RSL, RSLCS) are a predominately Hispanic street gang located in the Westlake area of Central Los Angeles west of Downtown Los Angeles.

org:5th-and-hill-los-angeles.description: During the 1970s and 1980s they had a strong presence but as the city continued to develop the downtown section of LA, the gang’s presence has declined as housing values have increase.

org:mexican-mafia.description: The Mexican Mafia (La eMe) is a predominantly Mexican-American prison gang founded in 1957 by Luis 'Huero Buff' Flores at Deuel Vocational Institution in Tracy, California. Despite its name, it has no origins in Mexico. With 250-300 'made' members and 50,000+ loyal street soldiers, it is considered 'among the most powerful, dangerous and feared criminal organizations in the world' by the US Government. La eMe controls all Sureño street gangs in Southern California through a tribute/'tax' system — including MS-13 and 18th Street. Allied with the Aryan Brotherhood, Sinaloa Cartel, and Mongols MC. The 'Shoe War' of 1968 at San Quentin established Nuestra Familia as their primary rival. Their symbol is a black hand (referencing early 20th century Black Hand extortion). Blood-in, blood-out membership policy. In 1992, Joe 'Pegleg' Morgan's no-drive-by edict stopped all drive-by shootings in East LA for 5 months — demonstrating their total control.

org:almighty-vice-lord-nation.description: The Almighty Vice Lord Nation (AVLN) is an African-American People Nation street gang and one of the largest gangs in the United States with 25,000+ members in chapters in every state. Founded in 1958 by Edwin Marion Perry, Toehold, Bobby Bonds, Ernest Wren, and Leonard Calloway at the Illinois State Training School for Boys in St. Charles. Headquartered in North Lawndale, Chicago. In the late 1960s, CVL Inc. became a non-profit that opened businesses and job training programs funded by government grants and the Rockefeller Foundation. The positive era collapsed when federal investigations shut them down in the early 1970s. Has 25+ factions including Conservative, Traveling, Renegade, Insane, Mafia, Imperial Insane, and Unknown Vice Lords — each operating independently. Symbols: Playboy Bunny, cane, top hat, martini glass, pyramid.

sources.title: Almighty Black P. Stone Nation — Wikipedia

org:aryan-brotherhood.description: The Aryan Brotherhood is a white supremacist prison gang and organized crime syndicate in the United States, founded in 1964 at San Quentin State Prison. With an estimated 20,000 members inside and outside prison, it is responsible for up to 25% of murders in the federal prison system despite comprising less than 3% of the inmate population.

org:comando-vermelho-united-states.description: Comando Vermelho (Red Command) is one of the oldest and most influential criminal organizations in Brazil, founded in 1979 in the Cândido Mendes prison in Rio de Janeiro. It is primarily involved in drug trafficking, arms dealing, and territorial control of favelas.

sources.title: List of criminal gangs in Brazil — Wikipedia

org:black-guerrilla.description: The Black Guerrilla Family (BGF) is an African-American Marxist-Leninist prison gang, street gang, and political organization founded in 1966 by George Jackson, George 'Big Jake' Lewis, and W.L. Nolen at San Quentin State Prison. Founded during the Black Power movement, its stated goals include promoting black power, maintaining dignity in prison, and overthrowing the US government. The BGF has 100-300 members with thousands of associates, active primarily in California and Maryland. A BGF member murdered Black Panther co-founder Huey P. Newton in 1989.

org:black-gangster-disciples-nation.description: The Gangster Disciples (GDN) are an African-American street and prison gang founded in 1966 in Chicago's Englewood neighborhood. Originally formed as the Supreme Gangsters by Larry Hoover, they merged with David Barksdale's Black Disciples in 1969 to create the Black Gangster Disciple Nation. After Barksdale's death in 1974 and the 1989 split with the Black Disciples, the GDs became the largest gang in Chicago with 50,000-90,000 members across 100+ cities and 50 states. Hoover ran the gang from prison until his 1995 federal arrest (17-year investigation) and is now at ADX Florence serving life. Symbols include the six-pointed Star of David (for co-founder 'King David' Barksdale) and the trident (Folk Nation). In 2016, 32 members were RICO-indicted including a former police officer who served as a hitman. In 2022, Hoover renounced his gang ties.

sources.title: Almighty Black P. Stone Nation — Wikipedia
Spanish Gangster Disciples - Chicago Gang History

org:the-brotherhood-of-eternal-love.description: Eternal Love was an organization of drug users and distributors operating from the mid-1960s through the late 1970s in Orange County, California. Founded by John Griggs, it produced and distributed LSD and hashish with the goal of sparking a 'psychedelic revolution' in the United States. The group was known for the 'Orange Sunshine' LSD tablets and assisted Timothy Leary's escape from prison.

sources.title: The Brotherhood of Eternal Love — Wikipedia

org:nuestra-familia.description: Nuestra Familia ('Our Family') is a Mexican-American prison gang founded in 1968 at Deuel Vocational Institute in Tracy, California, in response to violence from the Mexican Mafia. They represent Northern California (Norteño) inmates, with the dividing line from their rivals being roughly at Bakersfield. Their street army operates through affiliated Norteño gangs controlling drug trafficking and extortion throughout Northern California.

org:dc-blacks.description: The D.C. Blacks is an African-American prison gang in the United States whose members are from Washington D.C. They are allied with the Black Guerrilla Family and rival the Aryan Brotherhood. Formed due to the concentration of D.C. inmates in federal facilities, they are one of the largest prison gangs within penitentiaries but smaller than gangs like the Bloods and Crips.

org:nazi-lowriders.description: A white supremacist prison gang active in Southern California, especially Cypress, with small factions in Chicago and other states. Originated in the mid-1970s, recognized by California prison authorities in 1999. Known for extreme violence and a three-tier hierarchy. Allies include the Aryan Brotherhood and Public Enemy No. 1; rivals include Bloods, Crips, and Black Guerrilla Family.

sources.title: Hells Angels MC criminal allegations and incidents — Wikipedia

org:the-family.description: The Family was an African-American organized crime group based in Newark, New Jersey, founded by Akbar Pray in the early 1970s. It was primarily involved in drug trafficking, particularly cocaine, and at its height had over 300 members. The group collapsed after Pray was sentenced to life in prison in 1990, becoming fragmented.

org:black-mafia-family.description: The Black Mafia Family (BMF) is a drug trafficking and money laundering organization founded in 1985 in Detroit, Michigan by brothers Demetrius and Terry Flenory. It distributed cocaine across the United States with ties to Mexican cartels and used a hip-hop record label as a front. The organization was dismantled by federal indictments in 2005, with the Flenory brothers sentenced to 30 years in prison.

org:kumi-415.description: Predominantly African-American prison gang formed in Folsom State Prison in the mid-1980s by inmates from the San Francisco Bay Area. The name combines the Swahili word for ten (kumi) with San Francisco's area code 415. Members often incorporate African symbols and Swahili language into their culture and tattoos. The gang operates in several California prisons and selected areas.

org:primeiro-comando-da-capital-united-s.description: Primeiro Comando da Capital (PCC) is a Brazilian prison gang founded in 1993 in the Taubaté prison in São Paulo. It is the largest criminal organization in Brazil with tens of thousands of members, controlling drug trafficking routes and prison populations across the country.

org:united-blood-nation.description: The United Blood Nation (East Coast Bloods) is a street and prison gang formed on July 16, 1993 at Rikers Island by Leonard 'OG Dead Eye' McKenzie, who allied gangs to protect against dominant Hispanic gangs. The East Coast faction of the California Bloods, UBN has 7,000-15,000 members and became the dominant East Coast gang in the 1990s. Co-founder Omar Portee was sentenced to 50 years at ADX Florence. Subgroups include Nine Trey Gangsters and Sex Money Murder. The 6ix9ine/Nine Trey RICO case in 2018 drew widespread media attention.

org:211-crew.description: American white supremacist prison gang formed in 1995 at Denver County Jail by Benjamin Davis. Active both in and out of prison, it is involved in murder, weapons and drug trafficking, kidnapping, and robbery. The gang has been linked to high-profile murders including that of Colorado Department of Corrections head Tom Clements. Its membership is estimated at 1,000.

org:bro-draskapet-united-states.description: Brödraskapet ('The Brotherhood') is a Swedish prison gang founded in 1995 by inmates at the maximum security prison in Kumla, Sweden. It is one of Sweden's most notorious criminal organizations.

org:dead-man-incorporated.description: Dead Man Incorporated (DMI) is a predominantly white organized crime enterprise founded in the late 1990s in the Maryland Department of Corrections by Perry Roark, James Sweeney, and Brian Jordan. Initially allied with the Black Guerrilla Family, the gang expanded into drug trafficking, murder, and other crimes.

org:florencia.description: Florencia 13 (F13/South Side Florencia 13) is one of the largest Sureño gangs in South Los Angeles with 2,000-3,000+ members, named after the Florence area of LA County. Controlled by the Mexican Mafia. Notorious for racially motivated attacks on African-Americans — in 2007, 102 members were indicted in 'Operation Joker's Wild' (the biggest gang raid in US history at the time), with court testimony confirming race-based targeting. They've expanded to Oregon, Colorado, Florida, Utah, Arizona, Kansas, Mexico, Canada, Brazil, and Germany. In 2022, three members pled guilty to RICO charges for the murder of off-duty LAPD Officer Fernando Arroyos. Allied with Pueblo Bishop Bloods and Harvard Park Brims despite being Sureño.

org:22street.description: Varrio 22nd Street is a predominately Hispanic gang on the eastside of South Los Angeles located near 22nd Street and Central Avenue.

org:28thstreet.description: Street is a predominately Latino street gang on the eastside of South Los Angeles in a section known as the Low Bottoms.

org:29thstreet.description: Varrio 29th Street is a Hispanic Sureño-affiliated street gang based on the east side of South Los Angeles, one of the many long-standing Latino varrios with roots in early 20th century barrio culture.

org:36thstreet.description: Street is a predominately Hispanic street gang located on the eastside of South Los Angeles in an around the area of 36th Street east of Main Street and north of Martin Luther King Jr.

org:40thstreet.description: Varrio 40th Street was a predomintely Latino street gang that existed on the eastside of South Los Angeles during the 1970s.

org:varrio-43rd-street.description: Varrio 43rd Street is a predominantly Latino Sureño-affiliated street gang based around 43rd Street in South Los Angeles, part of the dense network of numbered-street varrios in the South Central corridor.

org:eastsidelocos.description: Locos Varrio East Side Trece are a predominately Hispanic street gang located on the eastside of South Los Angeles between Adams Blvd & 29th Street from Griffith (west) to Hooper Avenue (east).

org:varrio-hawthorne-lil-watts-lws.description: The most asked question about this barrio is “why do they call themselves Lil Watts when they are in Hawthorne, several miles southwest from Watts?” The answer to that question is related to when they actually formed. During the late 1960s, several guys from Hawthorne began a barrio in the most run down section of Hawthorne and it was Watts that had a reputation of being an area of Los Angeles that was most feared.

org:varrio-junior-mafia-near-imperial-hi.description: Varrio Junior Mafia is predominately Hispanic-American street gang located near Imperial Highway in South Los Angeles. They also have another gang located near 107th Street between Vermont Normandie Avenues.

sources.title: Varrio Junior Mafia  near Imperial Highway — StreetGangs

org:varrio-lennox.description: Gang, is a predominantly Hispanic street gang located in the Lennox area of unincorporated Los Angeles County, California.

org:watts-13-gang.description: South Side Watts 13 are an active Mexican-American street gang, located in the Watts neighborhood of South Los Angeles, California.

sources.title: Watts 13 Gang also known as South Side Watts 13 — UnitedGangs

org:street-saints-sts-99th-street.description: The Street Saints are an African-American street gang based around 99th Street in South Los Angeles, operating in the Watts-adjacent area between Century Boulevard and Imperial Highway.

org:harpys-deadend-los-angeles.description: Varrio Harpys are a predominately Latino Street gang located in the West Adams section in South Los Angeles. The gang formed in the 1960s in the area north of the University of Southern California near downtown Los Angeles. The gang is divided into two main identities; the Dead End Harpys and the Wild Bunch (WB). Under those two identities exist several cliques of the gang.

org:king-boulevard-stoners.description: A Latino Sureño-affiliated street gang based near Martin Luther King Jr. Boulevard in South Los Angeles.

org:temple-street-52nd-tokers-south-los-.description: Temple Street is one of the oldest street gangs in Los Angeles, founded by Filipino and Mexican youths in the 1920s–1930s in the downtown LA area. They have been involved in murders, assaults, burglaries, and drug trafficking throughout their long history.

sources.title: Temple Street  52nd Tokers  South Los Angeles — StreetGangs

org:35th-street-eastside.description: Varrio Eastside 35th Street is a predominately Hispanic street gang located on the Eastside of South Los Angeles near 35th Street.

org:36th-gangsters.description: Seis) are a Mexican-American street gang located in South Central, recently changed to South Los Angeles, California. Their territory stretches from Jefferson Blvd to Martin Luther King Jr. Boulevard between Main Street and San Pedro Avenue.

org:55-bunch.description: An African-American street gang based around 55th Street in South Central Los Angeles, part of the dense network of gangs in the mid-South LA area between Slauson and Florence Avenues.

org:ghettoboyz.description: Eastside Ghetto Boyz (GBZ) are a predominately Latino street gang located on the eastside of South Los Angeles off Adams Blvd.

org:elm-watts.description: The Elm Street Watts, 13 are a well-known Mexican-American street gang located in the Watts district of South Los Angeles, California. The Watts Elm Street have been active since the 1960s and has been around for over forty years or longer. They are named after a residential street called “Elm Street” between Bandera Street and Fir Ave, from Firestone Blvd to 92nd Street.

org:hang-out-boys-hob-in-south-los-angel.description: Hang Out Boys (HOB) are a predominately Latino street gang located in South Los Angeles off Broadway and Martin Luther King Blvd. They formed during the mid-1980s.

org:moon-light-cats.description: Moon Light Cats is a small multi-ethnic gang in South Los Angeles with cliques on 67th and 69th Streets, allied with the Criminals (CLS) gang.

org:south-central-assasins.description: Latino street gang based in the South Central Los Angeles area, operating in a region dominated by both Black and Hispanic gang activity since the 1970s.

org:south-side-play-boys-pbs-in-south-lo.description: East Side Play Boys are a predominately Hispanic street gang located on the eastside of South Los Angeles. The PBS from this neighborhood started during the mid 1970s and have the following clicks; 35th, 42nd Cyclones, 43rd, 46th Locos, 49th, 51st Locos, 56th Locos, 64th Street Chicos Locos. The PlayBoys are among one of the first Latino gangs to form in this area of Los Angeles.

sources.title: South Side Play Boys (PBS) in South Los Angeles — StreetGangs

org:street-saints.description: Street Saints are a predominately Hispanic street gang located on the eastside of South Los Angeles between Central Avenues and San Pedro Street.

org:street-villains-gang.description: Calle Villanos, are a Mexican-American street gang located in South Los Angeles, California (formerly known as South Central).

org:watts-grape-los-angeles.description: Watts Varrio Grape Street 13 is a long-standing Mexican-American street gang in the Watts neighborhood near the Jordan Downs Housing Projects. Originally part of a multiracial gang that included Black members, the Latino faction split off and aligned with the Sureños by the 1970s.

org:watts-jardin-garden-in-watts.description: Watts Jardin (Garden) was a predominately Latino street gang in the Watts section of South Los Angeles. It occupied the area of where the Imperial Courts stand which are occupied by the P Jay Watts Crips, but they faded from the streets during the 1950s. They were one of the first Latino gang in Watts.

org:weigand.description: Weigand Colonia Watts Southside Weigand Colonia Watts is a predominately Latino street gang in the Watts section of South Los Angeles.

org:southlos.description: Varrio South Los 13 is a predominantly Hispanic Sureño gang in South Los Angeles whose turf extends from Central Avenue on the east side to approximately Vermont Avenue on the west side.

org:vernon-hood-south-los-angeles-los-an.description: Street gang claiming territory in the Vernon Avenue corridor of South Los Angeles, an area contested between numerous Black and Latino gangs.

org:38thstreet.description: South Los Angeles Varrio South Side 38th street is a predominately Hispanic Street gang on the eastside of South Los Angeles.

org:errol-flynns.description: The Errol Flynns were a street gang founded on the lower east side of Detroit, Michigan, United States during the 1970s. They were known for their flamboyant dress and use of hand signs, and engaged in extortion, robbery, and drug trafficking. The gang collapsed in the 1980s due to the rise of crack cocaine and prosecution of leaders.

edges.evidence: The gang's conflicts with the Errol Flynns defined much of the era's gang landscape.

org:young-boys-inc.description: Young Boys Inc. was a major African American drug organization based in Detroit, Michigan, founded in 1976 by Dwayne Davis, Bernard Boykins, Reginald Chestnut, and Charles Lindsey. It pioneered inner-city street-corner heroin sales using minors as couriers to avoid severe penalties.

org:east-warren-posse.description: The East Warren Posse (EWP), also known as East Warren Crips (EWC), is a predominantly African American, Crip-affiliated street gang historically active on Detroit's east side. The gang is centered around the East Warren Avenue corridor and identifies with the color blue consistent with Crip affilia

org:detroit-gangster-disciples.description: The Gangster Disciples are a highly organized African-American street and prison gang founded in Chicago in 1966 by Larry Hoover, active in more than 35 states including Michigan. The Detroit chapter operates as part of the national Gangster Disciples structure, which has an estimated 30,000-40,000 members nationwide. The organization uses the "Growth and Development" motto and a six-pointed star as its symbol. Members in Detroit are involved in drug trafficking, extortion, and territorial control across multiple neighborhoods.

sources.title: Gangster Disciples: A Gang Profile — DOJ

org:tck-crew.description: TCK Crew is a tagger crew historically identified by Detroit Police for widespread tagging across commercial and public structures. Multiple members were arrested in 2014 during coordinated anti-graffiti operations. The crew is tracked alongside numerous Detroit street gangs and sets.

org:w7m-evergreen-telegraph-gangster-cri.description: The W7M Evergreen Telegraph Gangster Crips (W7M ETGC), commonly referred to as W7M ScoreGang (SCRG), are a predominantly African American, Detroit-based Crip-affiliated street gang operating on Detroit's far northwest side. They are a prominent faction within the 5Point Nation alliance and reflect a

edges.evidence: the group has been identified as a 'subset of the Crips' active within the Five Points neighborhood of Detroit's 48219 ZIP code
The set reflects a fusion of local Detroit gang structures with elements of Gangster Crip (3X/Movin Gang) self-identification.
Within Detroit's west-side gang landscape, W7M ScoreGang is commonly described as a prominent faction within the broader 5Point Nation (5PN) alliance

Early roots trace to the tagger crew B.S.S. (Bloc Side Soldiers), which was founded by Zahid Sekou (a/k/a 'Tiny Joker') and later affiliated with smaller Five Points–area cliques as portions of the neighborhood adopted 5Point Nation (5PN) symbolism and terminology beginning around 2015.

W7M ETGC is widely understood to be influenced by the broader Eight Trey ('8Tray') Gangster Crip tradition... the group is generally viewed as a localized offshoot or adaptation of the Eight Trey tradition

The phrasing also serves as a nod to the historical operating area of the 7 Mile Playboy Gangster Crips, who—according to the United States Department of Justice—were active along West Seven Mile Road between Lahser and Evergreen. The addition of 'Telegraph' by newer generations of Gangster Crips (3X / Movin' Gang) signals a westward expansion of that identity

org:chambers-brothers.description: The Chambers Brothers were a criminal organization based in Detroit, Michigan, that dominated crack cocaine distribution in the city during the mid-1980s. Founded by four brothers from Marianna, Arkansas, the gang controlled over 50% of Detroit's crack trade, operating around 700 crack houses and generating $3 million daily.

org:dwbi.description: DWBI ('Don't Worry Bout It') is a citywide street gang alliance in Metro Detroit comprising multiple independent cliques that broadly identify under the local Blood umbrella. The network began consolidating around 2014 and operates through numerous affiliated sub-groups spread across Detroit's east

edges.evidence: DWBI ('Don't Worry Bout It') is a citywide street gang alliance comprising multiple independent cliques that broadly identify under Detroit's local Blood umbrella
