org:dead-rabbits.description: Irish American criminal street gang active in Lower Manhattan from the 1830s to 1860s. They were founded by disgruntled members of the Roach Guards and were known for their rivalry with the Bowery Boys. The gang engaged in street fighting, assault, murder, and rioting, and supported Tammany Hall politicians. Their symbol was a dead rabbit on a pike.

org:bing-kong-tong.description: The Bing Kong Tong was one of the most powerful Tongs in San Francisco's Chinatown during the early 20th century. It was involved in violent Tong wars, particularly against the Hop Sing and Suey Sing Tongs. The organization was weakened by a decade-long war and state authorities, eventually emerging as a trade union, though suspected of remaining ties to organized crime.

org:barrow.description: The Barrow Gang was an American criminal group active from 1932 to 1934, led by Clyde Barrow and including Bonnie Parker. They robbed banks, stores, and gas stations across the Central United States during the Great Depression, killing at least nine police officers. The gang gained national notoriety during the 'public enemy era'.

org:norwalk.description: The Varrio Norwalk stretches north from 166th Street to Hopland Street, between Pioneer Boulevard to Norwalk Boulevard.

org:colacurcio-organization.description: The Colacurcio Organization was a criminal group based in Seattle, Washington, founded in the 1950s by Frank Colacurcio. It operated strip clubs and vending machines as fronts for racketeering, prostitution, and money laundering. The organization was dismantled after a series of indictments in 2009-2010, culminating in Colacurcio Sr.'s death in 2010 and his son's conviction.

org:state-line-mob.description: The State Line Mob was an association of criminal elements operating in the 1950s and 1960s along the Mississippi–Tennessee state line. Involved in bootlegging, gambling, prostitution, robbery, and murder, it owned motels, restaurants, and clubs that served as centers for vice. The group gained national attention for its feud with Sheriff Buford Pusser, which inspired the film Walking Tall.

org:manson-family.description: The Manson Family was a commune, gang, and cult led by Charles Manson, active in California from the late 1960s to early 1970s. The group consisted of approximately 100 followers who lived an unconventional lifestyle, using psychoactive drugs and radicalized by Manson's teachings. They committed at least nine murders, including the Tate–LaBianca murders, and other crimes. The group dissolved after Manson and other members were arrested.

org:dinner-set-gang.description: A gang of thieves active in the late 1960s and 1970s, known for burglarizing wealthy Americans' homes while the victims were at dinner. Based in the United States, the gang was led by brothers-in-law Peter Salerno and Dominick Latella. They stole millions in jewels, targeting families like the DuPonts and Flaglers. The gang avoided violence and carried no weapons.

org:suren-os-united-states.description: The Sureños are a large network of loosely affiliated Mexican-American and Central American street gangs in Southern California that pay tribute to the Mexican Mafia (La Eme) prison gang. The Sureño identity spread from California prisons in the 1960s–70s and now encompasses thousands of cliques across the US.

edges.evidence: in Southwest Detroit various GD-aligned sets (particularly predominately Latino sets) have mutual relations with Sureños (SUR-13) gangs
this Latin Counts set feuded with Sur 13 and the Gangster Disciples.

org:the-company.description: Organized crime syndicate based in Hawaii, founded in 1969 by Wilford Pulawa. Controlled criminal activities in Hawaii from the late 1960s to the mid-1990s, involved in illegal gambling, prostitution, racketeering, extortion, and drug trafficking. Extended influence to mainland US states including California and Nevada.

org:weather-underground.description: American Marxist militant organization active from 1969 to 1977, originally a faction of Students for a Democratic Society. It sought to overthrow the U.S. government through a bombing campaign targeting government buildings and banks, with no fatalities from its bombings. The group was designated a domestic terrorist organization by the FBI and dissolved after the Vietnam War peace accord.

sources.title: The Brotherhood of Eternal Love — Wikipedia

org:the-covenant-the-sword-and-the-arm-o.description: Far-right survivalist anti-government militia advocating Christian Identity, active in the United States during the 1970s and early 1980s. Developed from a Baptist congregation founded in 1971 in Pontiac, Missouri, and later operated a compound in northern Arkansas. Dissolved after a 1985 siege and arrest of its leaders.

sources.title: The Covenant%2C the Sword%2C and the Arm of the Lord — Wikipedia

org:deuces-and-trays.description: Deuce” or 2x card is an alliance reference to various street gangs that are affiated with the NeighborHood Crips as well as Rollin 0’s card. The majority of the sets affiated with the Deuce (2x) card are produced by the West Side Crips gang. A number of Deuces (2x) cliques are also allied with the Blocc Crips, which is an totally a separate Crip card.

org:fff-gang.description: FFF was a gang centered in the San Fernando Valley during the 1980s, unique for its membership of White Americans from middle and upper middle class backgrounds. It was founded by members of a punk rock band of the same name. The gang's activities largely ended after prominent member Mark Miller was fatally shot in 1985.

org:tiny-rascal-gang.description: The Tiny Rascal Gang (TRG) is a predominantly Cambodian-American gang founded in 1981 in Long Beach, California by Cambodian refugees who escaped the Khmer Rouge genocide. With 5,000-10,000 members and associates in 16 US states (FBI 2009), they are one of the largest Asian gangs in America. A branch split off to form the Asian Boyz. They have alliances with the Wah Ching and 14K triad. The Mexican Mafia 'green-lighted' TRG in the 1990s, leading to war with Sureños. Colors are gray or blue. Members have killed a Sacramento police officer (2007) and committed a mass shooting killing 3 in 2019. In 2023, 1,000 Cambodian-Americans have been deported partly due to TRG activity.

sources.title: DOJ - Tiny Rascal Gang RICO Charges

org:friends-stand-united.description: Anti-fascist, anti-racist, and anti-drug hardcore crew founded in the late 1980s by Elgin James in Boston, Massachusetts. Originally fought Neo-Nazis and racist groups in the punk scene, but later accused of unprovoked violence and intimidation. Classified by the FBI as a street gang.

org:los-solidos.description: Los Solidos is a street gang founded in the early 1990s by Lawrence Beauvais in Hartford, Connecticut. The gang formed from the merger of two other gangs to defend against larger rivals like the Latin Kings. It operates in Hartford, Springfield, Massachusetts, and other northeastern cities, involved in drug trafficking, weapons sales, and violent crimes.

org:nuestro-general.description: Nuestro General (NG) is the supreme leadership position within the Mexican Mafia (La Eme) prison gang, representing the highest authority in the organization that controls all Sureño street gangs in Southern California.

org:volksfront.description: American white separatist organization founded in 1994 by Randal Lee Krager and Richard Arden while incarcerated in Oregon. The group aimed to create an all-white homeland in the Pacific Northwest and had chapters in the US and several other countries. It was considered a neo-Nazi skinhead group by watchdogs. The US branch dissolved in 2012.

org:shadowcrew.description: ShadowCrew was a cybercrime forum operating from 2002 to 2004, founded by Brett Johnson, Seth Sanders, and Kim Marvin Taylor. It facilitated credit card fraud, hacking, and other illegal activities, with around 4,000 members. The site was infiltrated by the US Secret Service through informant Albert Gonzalez, leading to its shutdown.

org:311-boyz.description: The 311 Boyz was a teen gang in Las Vegas, Nevada, estimated at 140 members, active in 2003. The group consisted mostly of young males who attended Centennial High School and lived in the northwest area of Las Vegas. They were involved in violent acts, including an attack on a pickup truck that led to multiple criminal charges.

org:felony-lane-gang.description: Organized crime gang based in Fort Lauderdale, Florida, known for smash-and-grab thefts from parked cars to steal identification and cash fraudulent checks. They recruit drug addicts and target mothers at daycares, fitness centers, and outdoor areas. The gang operates nationwide in the United States.

org:rod-gang.description: A small murderous drug ring that operated in New Orleans, primarily in the St. Roch neighborhood. Formed among teens in trailers after Hurricane Katrina, the gang sold crack, marijuana, and heroin from 2007 to 2013. Twelve members were indicted on murder and racketeering charges in 2013.

org:39ers-gang.description: Hybrid street gang formed in 2010 from the merger of the G-Strip gang (Gallier Street) and 3-N-G gang (Third and Galvez) in New Orleans. Primarily African American, involved in drug trafficking, murder, and racketeering. Linked to over 45 murders, including the 2010 killing of rapper Magnolia Shorty. Ten members were indicted and convicted in 2017.

org:scattered-spider.description: Scattered Spider is a hacking group mostly composed of teens and young adults from the United States and the United Kingdom, believed to be affiliated with the cybercriminal network 'The Com'. Founded around May 2022, the group gained notoriety for hacking and extorting Caesars Entertainment and MGM Resorts International in 2023.

org:bahala-na-nalang-gang.description: The Bahala Na Gang (BNG) is a Filipino street gang founded in 1940 in Sampaloc, Manila, Philippines, by Divino Talastas. The name means 'come what may' in Filipino. It spread to Filipino immigrant communities in California — San Diego, Los Angeles, San Jose, San Francisco, Stockton, Sacramento — and Las Vegas. Members are involved in drug trafficking, weapons trafficking, murder, robbery, and kidnapping. In 1990, a gang fight at a Stockton taco stand involving a dozen BNG members killed two and injured five, resulting in 348 criminal counts.

org:tokyo-boyz.description: Japanese American gang founded in 1942 at Manzanar concentration camp during World War II. Initially formed by rebellious youths due to harsh internment conditions, the gang engaged in petty crimes like stealing rations. After release, members continued criminal activities in California, with membership growing post-internment. The gang was active until 1996.

org:4-seas.description: The Four Seas Gang (Sì Hǎi Bāng) is a Taiwanese triad society founded in 1954, the second largest criminal organization in Taiwan with an estimated 10,000 members. Has maintained presence in Southern California through Taiwanese immigrant communities.

org:four-seas-mafia.description: The Four Seas Gang (四海幫) is a Taiwanese triad organization founded in 1954, the second largest criminal organization in Taiwan with over 700 members and 46 branches. It has maintained an international presence including in Southern California.

org:united-bamboo.description: The United Bamboo Gang (竹聯幫) is one of the largest Taiwanese triads, founded in 1956 in Taipei by a group of high school students from military families who fled mainland China. It has an estimated 10,000 members worldwide and has been involved in drug trafficking, extortion, and political activities.

org:continentals.description: The Continentals were a Chinese American street gang prominent in New York City's Chinatown in the early 1960s. Founded in 1961 by American-born Chinese high school students to protect Chinese students from attacks by other ethnic groups. The gang engaged primarily in non-violent crimes such as vandalism and had up to 100 members.

org:wah-ching.description: Chinese American criminal organization and street gang founded in San Francisco, California in 1964. Involved in narcotics, racketeering, gambling, and other illegal activities. Known for the 1977 Golden Dragon Massacre and conflicts with rival gangs. Active primarily in the San Francisco Bay Area and Greater Los Angeles.

org:ken-side-wah-ching.description: A subset of the Wah Ching, a Chinese-American criminal organization founded in San Francisco in 1964. The Ken Side clique operates in the Los Angeles area.

org:monterey-park-wah-ching.description: A local clique of the Wah Ching operating in Monterey Park, a San Gabriel Valley city with a large Chinese American population. The Wah Ching was founded in San Francisco in 1964 and expanded to Southern California, becoming involved in narcotics, racketeering, and extortion.

org:paul-side-wah-ching.description: A subset of the Wah Ching, a Chinese-American criminal organization, operating in the San Gabriel Valley area of Los Angeles.

org:sonny-side-wah-ching.description: A subset of the Wah Ching Chinese-American criminal organization operating in the Alhambra/San Gabriel Valley area of Los Angeles. The Wah Ching was founded in San Francisco in 1964 and expanded throughout Southern California.

org:taiwanese-side-wah-ching.description: It’s exhausting to seek out educated folks on this matter, but you sound like you recognize what you’re speaking about! Thanks.

org:wah-ching-ken-side.description: The Ken Side clique of the Wah Ching, a Chinese-American criminal organization operating in the Los Angeles/San Gabriel Valley area.

org:wah-ching-t-side.description: The Taiwanese Side clique of the Wah Ching, a Chinese-American criminal organization operating in the Los Angeles/San Gabriel Valley area.

org:joe-boys.description: The Joe Boys were a Chinese American youth gang founded in the 1960s in San Francisco's Chinatown by Joe Fong, a former Wah Ching member. They were involved in drug trafficking, extortion, robbery, and murder. The gang was active from 1969 to 1977 and had conflicts with the Wah Ching and Hop Sing Tong.

org:6-shots-pinoy-real.description: A Filipino-American street gang based in Los Angeles, affiliated with the Pinoy Real network of Filipino sets active in the LA area.

org:akro-pinoy-akp.description: A Filipino-American street gang based in the Carson area of Los Angeles, connected to the Akrho fraternity originating from the Philippines.

org:atwater-villa-pinoy-real.description: A Filipino-American street gang based in the Atwater Village neighborhood of Los Angeles, a clique of the broader Pinoy Real network.

org:granada-hills-pinoy-real.description: A Filipino-American street gang based in the Granada Hills area of the San Fernando Valley, a clique of the broader Pinoy Real network active in LA.

org:north-side-bahala-na-gang.description: A Filipino-American street gang affiliated with the Bahala Na Gang network, based in the northern areas of Los Angeles.

sources.title: North Side Bahala Na Gang — StreetGangs

org:ping-on.description: Boston-based Chinese criminal organization founded in the 1970s by Stephen 'Sky Dragon' Tse, who had ties to the 14K Triad. It engaged in drug trafficking, arms trafficking, fraud, prostitution, human trafficking, illegal gambling, money laundering, extortion, murder, illegal immigration, and racketeering.

org:pinoy-boys-gang.description: A Filipino-American street gang based in the Los Angeles area, part of the network of Filipino gangs that emerged in Southern California during the 1980s–1990s.

org:pinoy-real.description: A Filipino-American street gang network based in Los Angeles with multiple cliques across different neighborhoods. 'Pinoy' is a colloquial term for Filipino.

org:real-pinoy-brothers.description: Real Pinoy Brothers (RPB) is a Filipino-American street gang based in the Los Angeles area, part of the network of Filipino gangs in Southern California.

org:scout-royal-brotherhood.description: A Filipino organization that originated as a fraternity in the Philippines. In California, some members have been involved in gang activity, with the Carson chapter linked to Blood affiliation.

org:tau-gamma-pinoy.description: Tau Gamma Phi still active in the san fernando valley? What city? They are still the biggest in the PH.

org:west-co-bahalana-gang.description: A Filipino-American street gang in West Covina, California, connected to the Bahala Na Gang network that originated in Philippine prisons.

org:west-covina-pinoy-real.description: Filipino-American street gang based in West Covina in the San Gabriel Valley, part of the Filipino gang presence documented since the early 1990s.

org:ghost-shadows.description: The Ghost Shadows was a Chinese American street gang prominent in New York City's Chinatown from the early 1970s to the mid 1990s. Formed in 1971 by immigrants from Taiwan and Hong Kong, it was affiliated with the On Leong Tong. The gang engaged in extortion, kidnapping, murder, racketeering, drug trafficking, and illegal gambling. It became defunct due to federal RICO crackdowns in the 1990s.

sources.title: On Leong Chinese Merchants Association — Wikipedia

org:north-side-satanas.description: The North Side Satanas are a Filipino-American street gang based in the San Gabriel Valley area of Los Angeles, named after the Satanas gang network founded in 1972 which is one of the oldest documented Filipino gangs in Southern California.

org:sarzana-in-cerritos.description: Sarzana is a Filipino American street gang based in the Cerritos/Artesia area of Southern California, part of the Filipino gang scene that emerged alongside groups like Satanas (founded 1972).

org:la-oriental-boys.description: The LA Oriental Boys are an Asian American street gang operating in the Los Angeles area, part of a network of Asian gangs documented by law enforcement since the 1980s.

org:oriental-boys.description: An Asian-American street gang based in Chinatown and Central Los Angeles, composed primarily of Chinese and Southeast Asian youth.

org:oriental-lazy-boys-in-chinatown-cent.description: West Side Oriental Lazy Boyz are a predominately Cambodian street gang located in the Chinatown community near downtown Los Angeles near Alpine Park. They are one of the first gangs among the Oriental Family and among the very few Asian gangs with a physical territory in the city of Los Angeles. They also were among the only Asian gangs to have ties with a Blood set.

sources.title: Oriental Lazy Boys in Chinatown, Central Los Angeles — StreetGangs

org:alhambra-hell-side.description: An Asian-American street gang based in Alhambra, California, composed primarily of Southeast Asian and Chinese-American youth involved in territorial disputes with other Asian gangs in the San Gabriel Valley.

org:avenue-oxford-boys.description: A street gang based in the Mid-City area of Los Angeles near Oxford Avenue, operating in the Koreatown/Mid-Wilshire corridor.

org:black-dragons.description: Chinese-American criminal organization and street gang formed in 1980 by Chinese immigrants in Lincoln Heights, Los Angeles. Operated in Los Angeles and San Gabriel Valley, involved in both organized crime (extortion, prostitution, money laundering, narcotics, counterfeiting) and street-level crime (murder, robbery, home invasion). At its peak had over 100 core members.

org:china-town-boyz.description: The Chinatown Boyz (CTB) are a Chinese American street gang based in the Chinatown neighborhood of Los Angeles, emerging from the Chinese gang scene in LA's Chinatown that developed in the 1960s–1970s alongside groups like Wah Ching and the Black Dragons.

org:easy-boys-in-long-beach-ca.description: An Asian-American street gang based in Long Beach, California, part of the network of Southeast Asian gangs in the Long Beach area.

sources.title: Easy Boys in Long Beach, CA — StreetGangs

org:flipside-trece.description: Flipside Trece is a Filipino/Latino street gang in Los Angeles affiliated with the Sureño (13) movement, part of a broader network of Filipino American gangs in Southern California.

org:fliptown-mob.description: Fliptown Mob is a Filipino-American street gang based in the Los Angeles area, part of the network of Filipino gangs that emerged in the 1980s–1990s. The 'Flip' in their name references 'Filipino.'

org:garden-grove-town-mob.description: Garden Grove Town Mob (GGTM) is an Asian street gang based in Garden Grove, Orange County, California. They were the target of a 2011 law enforcement raid that yielded firearms, ecstasy, and cash connected to gang-related homicides.

org:hell-side-gang.description: An Asian-American street gang based in Alhambra and the San Gabriel Valley, composed primarily of Chinese and Southeast Asian youth.

org:jefrox.description: A Filipino-American street gang based in Los Angeles, part of the network of Filipino gangs that emerged in LA during the 1980s and 1990s.

org:korat-boys.description: An Asian-American street gang based in Long Beach, California, named after the Khorat region of Thailand. Composed primarily of Thai and Southeast Asian youth.

org:local-boys.description: An Asian-American street gang based in Long Beach, California, composed primarily of Cambodian and Vietnamese Americans who emerged during the 1980s refugee resettlement period.

org:lost-boys.description: An Asian/Pacific Islander street gang based in the Long Beach/Los Angeles area, composed primarily of Cambodian and Vietnamese youth who emerged during the 1980s Southeast Asian refugee resettlement era.

org:luzon-visaya-mobsters.description: The Luzon Visaya Mobsters (LVM) are a Filipino American street gang based in Los Angeles. Their name references the two main island groups of the Philippines (Luzon and Visayas).

org:maplewood-jefrox.description: Filipino-American street gang based in the Maplewood area of Los Angeles, active since the 1980s. Part of the wave of Filipino gangs alongside groups like Satanas (1972) and Asian Boyz.

org:mental-boyz.description: An Asian-American street gang based in the Los Angeles area, part of the network of Southeast Asian youth gangs that formed during the 1980s–1990s refugee and immigrant era in Southern California.

org:original-asian-gangsters.description: The Original Asian Gangsters (OAG) are an Asian American street gang in Los Angeles, part of a documented network of over 100 Asian gangs in LA County.

org:rebel-boys.description: The Rebel Boys are a Latino Sureño-affiliated street gang operating on the West Side of Los Angeles.

org:red-door.description: An Asian-American criminal organization based in Diamond Bar, California. In 2010, 18 members were arrested on federal drug trafficking and marriage fraud charges.

org:ruthless-asian-criminals.description: An Asian-American street gang active in Los Angeles, composed of Southeast Asian youth involved in robbery, extortion, and violent crime.

org:silly-boys.description: An Asian-American street gang active in Los Angeles, composed primarily of Southeast Asian youth from the Cambodian and Vietnamese communities that settled in LA during the 1980s refugee era.

org:suicidal-town-long-beach-california.description: Suicidal Town (STC) is a predominantly Cambodian-American street gang based in Long Beach, California, emerging in the 1980s among Khmer Rouge genocide refugees. They are part of the Southeast Asian gang landscape in Long Beach alongside the Tiny Rascal Gang and Asian Boyz.

sources.title: Suicidal Town, Long Beach, California — StreetGangs

org:tres-cantos.description: An Asian-American street gang active in the Cerritos/LA County area, composed primarily of Filipino youth.

org:triad-boyz.description: A Chinese/Asian-American street gang active in the Los Angeles area, named after the Chinese Triad criminal organizations but operating as a local street gang.

org:true-brown-style.description: True Brown Style (TBS) is a Filipino gang originating in the Philippines with some presence in Filipino-American communities in Southern California.

org:united-brotherhood.description: Multi-ethnic Asian-American street gang active in the Los Angeles area, part of the wave of Asian gang formation in Southern California during the 1980s–90s.

org:valley-side-jefrox.description: A Filipino-American street gang based in the San Fernando Valley area of Los Angeles, affiliated with the Jefrox gang network.

org:viet-boyz.description: A Vietnamese-American street gang based in the Alhambra/San Gabriel Valley area of Los Angeles, part of the network of Vietnamese gangs in Southern California.

org:westside-islanders.description: An Asian/Pacific Islander street gang based in Long Beach, California, composed primarily of Samoan and Tongan youth.

org:white-dragons.description: An Asian-American street gang based in the Los Angeles area, composed of Chinese and Taiwanese-American youth active since the 1980s.

org:white-tigers.description: The White Tigers were a Chinese American street gang based in New York City's Flushing, Queens, and northern Chinatown. Formed in 1980 by Cantonese and Taiwanese immigrants and American-born Chinese, they engaged in extortion, kidnapping, drug trafficking, and murder. Active from the early 1980s to the mid-1990s, they fought turf wars with rival gangs like the Green Dragons.

org:korea-town-gangsters.description: A Korean-American street gang based in the Koreatown neighborhood of central Los Angeles, emerged during the 1980s-1990s immigration wave.

org:korea-town-mobsters.description: Ktown mob los Angeles affiliated or not Koreans look out for each othe during these times man w covid kicks and county handin out deals les jus rob the ish outta errbbody.

org:korean-mobsters.description: A Korean-American street gang active in Los Angeles, engaged in extortion, robbery, and home invasions targeting the Korean-American community.

org:korean-play-boys.description: A Korean-American street gang based in the Los Angeles area, emerged during the late 1980s-1990s within the Korean-American community.

org:seoul-boyz.description: A Korean-American street gang based in the Los Angeles area, named after Seoul, South Korea. Part of the Korean gang network that emerged in LA during the 1980s-1990s.

org:tiny-raskal-gang.description: A predominantly Cambodian-American street gang that originated among refugees in Long Beach, California in the 1980s following the Khmer Rouge genocide. Classified by the FBI as one of the largest Asian gangs in the United States.

org:menace-of-destruction.description: Menace of Destruction (MOD) is a Hmong street gang founded in 1988 in Fresno, California. Originally called Masters of Destruction, it is one of the oldest active Asian street gangs in Northern California and the largest Hmong gang in the United States. The gang is involved in murders, shootings, drug trafficking (marijuana and methamphetamine), and weapon trafficking.

org:asian-boyz.description: The Asian Boyz (ABZ) are a Crip-affiliated street gang founded in the late 1980s in Long Beach, California to protect Cambodian refugees from the East Side Longos. They split from the Tiny Rascal Gang after an internal dispute. With 2,000-5,000 members in 28 cities across 14 states (FBI 2009), they are one of the largest Asian gangs in the US. Colors are blue/navy (West Coast) and green/black/white (Midwest/East Coast). Founder Marvin Mercado received 218 years for four murders. They have a long-running feud with the Wah Ching ('Summer Madness' shootouts in 1993). Some members serve in the US military and traffic drugs using that position.

org:central-side-asian-boyz.description: The Asian Boyz are predominantly Cambodian, Vietnamese, and Filipino, with approximately 2,000 members across 14 states.

org:west-side-asian-boyz.description: The West Side clique of the Asian Boyz, a predominantly Cambodian-American street gang. The ABZ were founded around 1989 and are classified by the FBI as one of the largest Asian gangs in the US.

org:jackson-boys.description: Asian American street gang based in San Francisco's Chinatown, founded in the 1990s by the Tsan brothers (Bobby, Johnny, Tommy) as successors to the Wo Hop To triad. Engaged in extortion, drug trafficking, arson, and illegal gambling. Noted for a 1995 shooting on Stockton Street that wounded seven bystanders. Still active as of 2000.

org:lgkk.description: The Last Generation Korean Killers (LGKK) are a Korean-American street gang in the Los Angeles area, identified by LAPD as responsible for armed robbery sprees. Their case was notable as one of the first to utilize an extradition treaty with South Korea.

org:fullerton-boys.description: A Korean-American street gang based in Fullerton, California, founded in 1996. Members were originally from other gangs such as LVM, SZA, and Wah Ching. The gang emerged as a collective identity after other gangs disbanded in Fullerton.

org:black-p-stones.description: The Black P Stones (BPS) are a well-known active African-American street gang located on the East Side of South Los Angeles, California.

edges.evidence: Leonard 'Cal' Calloway was one of the first seven Vice Lords that helped put it all together and before he was a Vice Lord, he was an Imperial Chaplain. Cal was related to fifteen-year-old Eugene 'Lil Bruce' Hairston.
Not only did each of their gangs fight the Egyptian Cobras and the Devil's Disciples north and west of them, they also fought each other tooth and nail
There was also a cry out for help from the community after a very nasty 1964 war between Rangers and Disciples as both gangs were growing
Prior to this merger, the Gangsters didn't have many problems with the Stones but once the BGDN was established the Stones targeted Gangsters which is why GDs and Stones have been at war since 1969.
Jeff Fort officially disbanded the Main 21 and declared the Black P Stone name defunct and was to be replaced by the El Rukn name.
the Cobrastones still left the Black P Stone Nation and became known as the 'Mickey Cobras' that year.

Jeff Fort stood as the front runner for organizing an alliance in opposition to the Folks as he gathered several El Rukns, Black P Stones, Vice Lords and Latin Kings to The Fort on one fateful day in 1981. Together, these organizations drafted the People alliance
This alliance drew the Black P Stones very close to the Latin Kings and even helped relations with Vice Lords, Mickey Cobras and Four Corner Hustlers.
The Gaylords also tangled badly with Black P Stones of West Englewood resulting in many shootings, stabbings and violent rumbles happened between the two gangs.
The Latin Stones became slightly allied with Latin Counts in the area but mostly allied with Black P Stones.
Even though the Stones are a fellow People alliance group, the Players could only exist on these streets by going to war with the Stones which is exactly what happened.
May 7, 1971 article about the Black Souls at Madison and Damen in the Near West Side neighborhood getting ready to use an antique tripod machine gun on Vice Lords and Black P Stones.

According to many old school Black P Stones and Vice Lords that settled in Austin in 1968, the Four Corner Hustlers were aggressive about recruitment so much that Fours started a war with Black P Stones and Apache (Insane) Vice Lords.
Although the Party People were on Maplewood and 56th and Campbell was not official territory Party People still heavily cherished this corner and resented the Stone's arrival causing war to erupt.

Black P Stones began to have a growing presence in the Lathrop projects at Leavitt & Clybourn in the very early 90s as Deuces were fading out. The Deuces did not approve of this Black P Stone advancement and war kicked off.
Two Two Boys and SDs came to the aid of the Latin Souls during their war with Latin Kings, Saints and Black P Stones
Familia Stones have run out of People nation allies as they even have engaged in wars with Black P Stone groups in parts of the city.
The Latin Souls immediately tangled with Bishops, Latin Kings and Black P Stones.
Cobras are heavily invested in Woodlawn and have shared these streets close by the Black P Stones with on and off violent rivalries which goes back to 1958.
In April, 1978 when the People Nation alliance was created the P.R. Stones were invited to join which brought them back into an alliance with Black P Stones.
The Black P Stones were the main African American gang in the 80s and 90s but the Stones are with the People alliance.

On April 15, 1972 the Black P Stones attacked Bob's Restaurant and smashed all the windows, the TJOs went on the defense and attacked the Stones which resulted in a massive gang brawl as bottles and chains were being used.
Eventually CVLs and Black P Stones would go to war over territory now that both gangs were big in certain south side areas.

most Crip-identified sets historically maintain adversarial relationships with several Blood-affiliated groups. Longstanding conflicts have been documented between Crip sets and organizations such as the Black P. Stones
Blood-affiliated gangs, mainly Black P. Stones / Jungle Stone Bloods
Primary Rivalries: Blood sets ( 55 SMB/RedZone , Black P. Stones , Bounty Hunters, Head Bangers)

org:bounty-hunter-watts-bloods.description: The Bounty Hunter Watts Bloods are a Blood set based in the Nickerson Gardens public housing projects in Watts, the largest government housing complex west of the Mississippi. Originally known as the Green Jackets, founded in 1969 by Gary Barker and Bobby Jack. With over 2,000 documented members subdivided into cliques (Lot Boys, Block Boys, Bell Haven, Ace Line, Duece Line, Tray Line, Four Line, Five Line), they control the largest territory of any African-American street gang in Watts. Their longstanding feud with the Grape Street Watts Crips has been described as 'the most violent and long lasting feud between two gangs in the Watts area.' They notably allied with the rival PJ Watts Crips against the Grape Street Crips.

edges.evidence: Longstanding conflicts have been documented between Crip sets and organizations such as the Black P. Stones, Bounty Hunter Bloods
Primary Rivalries: Blood sets ( 55 SMB/RedZone , Black P. Stones , Bounty Hunters, Head Bangers)

org:doty-block-bloods.description: African-American Blood gang based in the Inglewood/South Bay area near Doty Avenue, emerged during the late 1970s as Blood sets expanded across the South Bay.

org:eastside-be-bopp-watts-bloods-los-an.description: Bopp Watts Bloods Be Bopp Watts Bloods are a predominately Black street organization located in the Watts section of South Los Angeles. They have 2 main clicks or subgroups, the 92nd Street and 93rd Street clicks are are located from 92nd Street (north) to 98th Street (south) along Central Avenue.

org:hacienda-village-bloods.description: The Hacienda Village Bloods (HVB) are a African-American street gang located on the East Side in Watts neighborhood of South Central, California.

org:hoover-park-family-bloods.description: Hoover Park Family was a predominately African-American Blood gang located in the area of Hoover Park on Adams Blvd and Hoover during the 1970s.

org:pacoima-piru-bloods.description: Pacoima Piru Bloods are a predominately African-America street gang located in the community of Pacoima in the San Fernando Valley section of Los Angeles. They are among the only African-American gang in the Pacoima community which is dominated by several Latino gangs, and one of the few non-Compton Pirus in Los Angeles.

org:queen-street-bloods.description: Westside Queen Street Blood are a predominately African-American street gang located in, CA on the northwest side of Inglewood.

org:rollin-20s-outlaw-bloods.description: Outlaw Bloods are a predominately African-American street gang located on the eastside of South Los Angeles near Adams Blvd and Central Avenues.

org:west-downtown-inglewood-bloods.description: African-American Blood gang based in the downtown/western district of Inglewood, California. Part of Inglewood's extensive Blood network that includes the Crenshaw Mafia and Queen Street Bloods.

org:pueblobloods-los-angeles.description: East Side Pueblo Bishop Bloods are a predominately African-American street gang based in the Pueblo Del Rio housing projects in South Los Angeles in a community known as the Low Bottoms. They have been active in that area since the early 1970s before the Blood identity was even forged.

org:center-park-bloods.description: The Center Park Gangster Bloods (CPB) are an African-American Blood gang based in Inglewood, California, one of several Blood sets operating alongside the Crenshaw Mafia and Inglewood Family Gang.

org:denver-lane-bloods.description: The Denver Lane Bloods are one of the original founding sets of the Bloods alliance, established in South Los Angeles in the early 1970s alongside the Pirus, Brims, and Bounty Hunters to oppose Crip dominance. In 2025, a multi-agency operation targeted the Pasadena Denver Lane Bloods for gun trafficking.

sources.title: DOJ - Denver Lane Bloods Narcotics Arrests

org:fruit-town-brims.description: The Fruit Town Brims (FTB) are a predominantly African-American Blood gang located in South Los Angeles. They are one of the most well-known Blood sets in the city.

org:harvard-park-brims-los-angeles.description: Harvard Park Brims are a predominately African-American street gang located in South LA between Gage (s) & Slauson (n), from Western (w) to Vermont (e). They have three clicks, 61, 62, & 64 and throughout the 1980s they were primarily known as the 62 Harvard Park Brims.

org:parkside-piru-san-diego.description: African-American Piru Blood gang with presence in multiple cities, operating under the Piru/Bloods alliance since the late 1970s–1980s, including San Diego's Paradise Hills neighborhood.

org:rollin-50s-brim.description: African-American Blood gang under the Brim alliance, based around 50th Street in South Los Angeles near Van Ness Avenue. Part of the Brim faction that allied with the Pirus to form the Bloods in the early 1970s.

org:76qsb.description: Bixx Block) were a predominately African-American street gang that was a spin off of the original Queen Street Blood gang in Inglewood, California.

org:92bishop.description: Bishop Bloods are a predominately African-American street gang that was founded during the early 1970s on Bandera Street in the Watts section of South Los Angeles.

org:athens-park-bloods.description: The Athens Park Bloods (APB) are primarily a black criminal street gang located on the west side of Rosewood (also known as Athens), around the Rosedale community, region of Los Angeles, Ca. Their neighborhood stretches from Figueroa Ave to Avalon Blvd. They’ve several cliques, but their main cliques are 124th Street and 127th Street.

org:emerald-hills-gangster-bloods.description: Emerald Hills Uptown Bloods, are an African-American street gang located in the Emerald Hills neighborhood of Southeast San Diego, California.

org:family-swan-bloods.description: The Family Swan Bloods (FSB) is an alliance between two street gangs the Mad Swan Bloods and the Neighborhood Family Bloods, both gangs are located on the East Side of South Los Angeles (previously known as South Central, LA), California.

org:neighborhood-family-bloods.description: The Neighborhood Family Bloods are an African-American street gang on the East Side of South Los Angeles, California.

org:oak-park-bloods.description: The Oak Park Bloods (OPB), are an African-American street gang located in the Oak Park neighborhood of Sacramento, California.

org:pasadena-squiggly-lane-gangster-bloo.description: Predominantly African-American Blood set based in Pasadena and Altadena, California with approximately 80 members. One of several Blood gangs operating in the Pasadena area.

sources.title: Pasadena Squiggly Lane Gangster Bloods — UnitedGangs

org:centinela-park-family.description: Centinela Park Family is a predominately Black America street gang in the City of Inglewood, California adjacent to the City of Los Angeles.

org:mad-swan-bloods.description: Eastside Mad Swan Bloods are 77, 79, 80 and 84 Mad Swan Bloods. They are closely aligned with the Neighborhood Family Swan Bloods adjacently located.

org:orchard-brim.description: African-American Blood gang operating under the Brim alliance in the South Bay area of Los Angeles County, emerged in the late 1970s–early 1980s as part of the Brim faction that formed the core of the Bloods alliance.

org:van-ness-gangsters.description: African-American Blood gang under the Brim alliance, also known as the Van Ness Gangster Brims, based on the West Side of South Los Angeles near 54th Street and Van Ness Avenue. Allied with Fruit Town Brims and Harvard Park Brims.

org:avenue-piru-gang.description: Avenue Piru Gang is a predominately African-American street gang in the City of Inglewood and among one of the few Blood gangs that claim Piru that are located outside of the city of Compton, considered the birth place of the Pirus. The APGs main click are the 104th Streets, but there are members who claim 108th & 109th Streets.

org:crenshaw-mafia-gang.description: Crenshaw Mafia Gang are a predominately African-American street gang located in the City of Inglewood in Los Angeles County. The original founders started this gang as a spin-off of the Inglewood Family Gang in 1981. Their turf is located on the westside of Crenshaw Blvd off Century in a neighborhood called “The Bottoms,” and 104th Street is their core location.

sources.title: Crenshaw Mafia Blood Guilty Pleas — DOJ

org:rollin-20s-bloods.description: The Rollin 20s Neighborhood Bloods (R20NHB), are primarily, but not exclusively, an African-American street gang located in the West Adams district on the West Side of South Los Angeles, California.

org:40s-piru-los-angeles.description: East Side 40s Piru were a predominately African-American Blood street gang located on the eastside of South Los Angeles. A newer generation of Bloods from the same neighborhood started claiming All For Crime Bloods in the early 2000s, and the 40s Piru identity has slowly disappeared.

org:east-side-pain-gang.description: The East Side Pain gang was the target of a July 2007 multi-agency takedown involving more than 500 law enforcement officers which included 55 warrants executed simultaneously leading to multiple federal indictments, five federal forfeitures, more than 15 state violation arrests as well as five property nuisance abatement actions taken by the City Attorney’s Safe Neighborhoods Division.

org:hawthorne-piru.description: The Hawthorne Piru is a Blood gang set based in Hawthorne, California, part of the Piru alliance tracing its origins to the Piru Street Boys founded in 1969 in Compton. They are one of the Piru sets that spread from Compton to surrounding South Bay cities during the 1970s–1980s.

org:black-p-stones-jungles.description: The Black P. Stones Jungles are a Blood-affiliated gang based in Baldwin Village ('The Jungles') on the West Side of South Central Los Angeles, one of the largest gangs in the city with 500-900 members. Originally consisted of City Stone Bloods (Mid-City) and Jungle Stone Bloods (Baldwin Village). They were targeted by 'Operation Stone Cold' (2005, 18 arrested) and 'Operation Red Dawn' (2011, 75 indicted with 900+ officers). They have a rivalry with the 18th Street Gang and a loose alliance with the Playboy Gangster Crips.

sources.title: Black P Stones - Chicago Gang History

edges.evidence: Blood-affiliated sets on the Westside (notably 'Tag Wars' against Jungle Stones)

org:avenue-bloods-los-angeles.description: A Blood gang set operating in the Inglewood/South Bay area of Los Angeles, part of the network of Blood sets in the Westside.

org:blood-stone-villians-eastside-los-an.description: Eastside Blood Stone Villians are a predominately African-American street gang located on the eastside of South Los Angeles in an area commonly referred to as the bottoms. They have two main clicks, 52nd & 56th Streets, but there are some newer clicks that have emerged in recent years. This neighborhood was founded by Michael “Icey Jack” Bowie.

org:dalton-gangster-bloods-los-angeles.description: Dalton Gangster Bloods were on Dalton Avenue south of Martin Luther King Jr. Blvd, but the neighborhood is not active and faded out in the mid 1980s.

org:miller-gangster-bloods-los-angeles.description: Miller Gangster Bloods are a predominately African-American street located in South Los Angeles. The gang was started by an OG Athens Park Blood members on 120th Street in the Dorie Miller Manor Apartments between Central and Avalon.

org:neighborhood-swan-bloods-los-angeles.description: Eastside Neighborhood Family Swan Bloods are a predominately African-American street gang on the eastside of South Los Angeles. Located south of Manchester to 92nd Street (s) between Avalon (w) and Central Avenues. The northwest section of this area is controlled by the 88 Avalon Garden Crips.

org:456-island-piru-pomona.description: Island Gang are primarily an African-American street gang founded in the 1970s, on the North Side of Pomona, California. They are called the “Islands” due to their area being isolated and surrounded by crips as well as being the only subset of the Bloods, located in the city of Pomona.

org:5-9-brims-san-diego.description: The 5-9 Brims (South Side Brims) are a Blood-affiliated gang based in the Lincoln Park area of San Diego, California. They are also known as the Central City Gangsters and are one of several Blood sets operating in the San Diego area.

org:rollin-30-s-bloodstone-piru.description: Blood Stone Pirus) are an African-American street gang located on the East Side of South Los Angeles, California. The BSP originated in the 1970’s around Martin Luther King Jr Blvd, between Avalon Blvd to Central Ave. The BloodStone Pirus have been acted for over thirty year, and falls under the Piru umbrella.

org:unton-untouchable-brims-los-angeles.description: The Untouchable Brims are an African-American Blood gang under the Brim alliance, operating in the Los Angeles area. The Brims are one of the founding factions of the Bloods alongside the Pirus, formed in the early 1970s.

org:brown-side-piru-los-angeles.description: The Brown Side Piru is a Blood gang set on the East Side of South Los Angeles under the Piru alliance. Like other Piru sets, they trace their lineage to the original Piru Street Boys who broke from the Crip alliance in the early 1970s.

org:deep-valley-bloods.description: The Deep Valley Bloods are an African-American Blood gang based in the San Fernando Valley area of Los Angeles, on law enforcement radar since the early 1980s.

org:delmann-heights-bloods.description: The Delmann Heights Bloods (DHB) are a multi-ethnic street gang located on the West Side in the Delmann Heights community of San Bernardino, California. According to the FBI and DOJ, they play a major role in narcotics distribution in the San Bernardino area.

org:lincoln-park-bloods.description: The Lincoln Park Bloods was originally known as “Paul Lowe’s Control”, throughout the 1970s. However, this eventually changed to “Lincoln Park Piru/ Syndo Mob ” in the late 1970s.

org:neighborhood-piru-inglewood.description: Neighborhood Piru (NHP), are an active African-American street gang located on the West Side of Inglewood, California. Their neighborhood is centered around Rogers Park, located on Beach Ave, between Hyde Park Blvd and Ivy Blvd. The Neighborhood Pirus and the Avenue Piru Gang, are the only two gangs in Inglewood, to represent Piru.

org:rollin-20s-neighborhood-bloods.description: The Rollin 20s Neighborhood Bloods stretches from Vermont Ave to Arlington Ave, between Jefferson Blvd to Pico.

org:skyline-piru-san-diego.description: The Skyline Pirus are a multi-ethnic Blood/Piru gang based in the Skyline neighborhood of southeastern San Diego, one of the larger Piru sets in San Diego County.

org:ftb-los-angeles.description: Westside Fruit Town Brims are a predominately African-American street gang aligned with the Bloods located in the Exposition Park area of South Los Angeles.

org:circle-city-pirus-in-watts-los-angel.description: Circle City Piru is a predominately black street gang based in the Watts section of South Los Angeles. There are located between Central Avenue (west) and Success (east) between 103rd (north) and 107th Street. The CCP are among several gangs adjacent to Ted Watkins Park (formerly known as Will Rogers Park). In 1995 the park was renamed to honor Watkins, who was the founder of the Watts Labor Community Action Committee (WLCAC).

org:pasadena-park-nine.description: A Blood gang operating in the Pasadena area of Los Angeles County, one of the few Blood sets in the Pasadena/Altadena corridor.

org:little-africa-piru-san-diego.description: The Little Africa Pirus are a Blood/Piru gang established in the 1980s in the Lincoln Park neighborhood of San Diego, named after the historically African-American community in that area.

sources.title: Little Africa Piru San Diego — UnitedGangs
Little Africa Piru San Diego — StreetGangs

org:mid-town-los-angeles.description: Mid Town Family was a predominately African-America Blood street gang in the Mid-Town section of Central Los Angeles during the 1970s.

org:original-genoside-bloods.description: The Original Genocide Bloods are an African-American Blood gang set active in the Los Angeles area, one of the smaller Blood sets that emerged during the 1980s expansion of the Bloods alliance.

org:liggett-bloods.description: Liggett Street Gangsters are a blood street gang located in Panorama City, in the San Fernando Valley of Los Angeles, California. They originated in the late 1980s or early 1990s, as a neutral street gang with no affiliation with Bloods or Crips.

org:krook-city-bloods-san-diego.description: Valley Krook City Bloods (MVKCB) are primarily an Samoan-American street gang that function in the Tri-City area of Oceanside, in the North County (San Diego area) of California.

sources.title: Krook City Bloods San Diego — UnitedGangs

org:del-paso-heights-bloods.description: The Del Paso Heights Bloods are based in the Del Paso Heights neighborhood of North Sacramento, an area devastated by the crack epidemic of the 1980s. The neighborhood was cut off by highway construction and military base closure, leading to economic decline and gang proliferation.

org:allforcrime-los-angeles.description: All For Crime Bloods are a predominately African-American street gang, that is part of the Bloods alliance on the eastside of South Los Angeles. This street gang formed around 2003 and they have an established territory near 42nd Street & Ascott in South Los Angeles that once belonged to the Rollin 40s Pirus, another Blood gang. In recent years the 40s Piru identity has been over shadowed by AFCB. There are members of the Rollin 40s Pirus still in the area, but the younger generation of gang members have decided to represent AFCB.

org:east-coast-crips-los-angeles.description: The East Coast Crips are a large African-American Crip alliance originating in the early 1970s on the East Side of South Los Angeles, with numerous subsets identified by street numbers (62, 66, 68, 69, 76, 89, 97, 118, 190).

org:dodge-city-crips-los-angeles.description: The Dodge City Crips are a predominantly African-American Crip gang based in the Harbor area of Los Angeles, near the Harbor City and Lomita neighborhoods.

org:eastside-116-kitchen-crips-los-angel.description: A Kitchen Crip set operating around 116th Street on the East Side of South Los Angeles. The Kitchen Crips are named after their origin near a community kitchen.

org:87-kitchen-crip-in-south-los-angeles.description: Eastside 87 Kitchen Crip Gang is a predominately African-American street gang located in Florence, a section of unincorporated Los Angeles County adjacent to South Los Angeles. Their territory is located from Central (west) to Mary (east) from Manchester (north) to Florence (south) on the eastside of South Los Angeles. Prior to the formation of this gang, the Avenues, led by Craig Monson were active in this community from 1966 to 1971.

org:four-corner-blocc-crips-4corner.description: Four Corner Blocc Crips (4CBC) is an African-American Crip set based in South Los Angeles, California. Part of the broader network of Crip sets in the South Central area.

org:four-line-drive-crips-los-angeles.description: African-American Crip set in South Los Angeles, part of the network of smaller Crip sets formed during the 1970s–1980s expansion.

org:geer-crips-los-angeles.description: The Geer Gang Crips are an African-American Crip set based on the West Side of South Los Angeles, subject to a gang injunction by the Los Angeles City Attorney alongside the School Yard Crips.

org:holmes-town-crips-los-angeles.description: The Holmes Town Crips are an African-American Crip set based in the Holmes Avenue corridor of Watts/South Los Angeles, operating near the Jordan Downs and Nickerson Gardens housing projects.

org:kitchen-crips-los-angeles.description: The Kitchen Crips originated in the 1970’s, with three sub-sets the 116 Kitchen Crips and 95 Kitchen Crips as well as the 87 Kitchen Crips.

org:main-street-crips.description: Main Street Mafia Crips are a predominately African-American street gang, located between 79th and Manchester on the eastside of South Los Angeles. There is another faction of Main Street Mafia Crips about 0.5 miles south which include the 98th and 104th Street clicks.

org:main-mafia-crips-msmc-los-angeles.description: The Main Street Mafia Crips are a predominately African-American street gang located on the eastside of South Los Angeles. This particular faction includes the 98 and 104th Street Main Street Crip. There is another faction of Main Street Crips, located 0.5 miles north of this section.

org:sacremento-ave-crips.description: African-American Crip set based in the Altadena area of Los Angeles County, named after Sacramento Avenue. Part of the Crip expansion into surrounding communities during the late 1970s–1980s.

org:westside-original-blocc-crips.description: The Original Blocc Crips are a predominately African-American street gang located in the unincorporated section of Los Angeles County known as Athens. Their turf lays between Normandie (east) and Western Avenues (west) from 107th Street (north) to Imperial Highway (south). This section of South Los Angeles is policed by the Lennox Department of the Los Angeles County Sheriff’s Department, but it is adjacent to the City of Los Angeles to the north.

org:twilight-zone-crips-110-los-angeles.description: Twilight Zone Crips are a predominately Black street gang located in the Southern section of Los Angeles between Broadway & Avalon. There is another separate Twilight Zone set from Compton.

org:west-blvd-crips-los-angeles.description: The West Blvd Crips are a predominately African-American street gang located in the West Adams section of South Los Angeles.

org:6-pacc-east-coast-crips.description: The 6-Pacc East Coast Crips who originated on the East Side of South Los Angeles, California. Their neighborhood stretches from Avalon Blvd to the 110 Freeway (Harbor Fwy), between 60th Street to 69th Street.

org:west-coast-crips-san-diego.description: The West Coast Crips originated in 1972, as the West Coast Mafia Crips. Like many black gangs in Southeast San Diego, they also have roots in South Los Angeles, California.

sources.title: West Coast Crips San Diego — UnitedGangs

org:front-hood-compton-crips.description: Front Hood Compton Crips The Original Front Hood Compton Crips (OFHCC) are an African-American street gang on the W est Side of Compton. They are west of the Poccet Hood Compton Crips, and East of the West Side Piru. Their neighborhood spread from W 130th St to 138th Street. Wilmington Ave to the Compton Creek acts as their borderline.

sources.title: Original Front Hood Compton Crip — StreetGangs

org:mona-park-compton-crips.description: The Mona Park Compton Crips (MPCC) are primarily an African-American street gang located on the East Side in the unincorporated community of Willowbrook and Compton, California. Their neighborhood runs from 118th Street to 125th Street, between Mona Blvd and Wilmington Ave. Which, cover both sides of the Compton (Blue Line) Train Tracks, on Willowbrook Ave.

org:colton-city-crips.description: The Colton City Crips (CCC) are primarily an African-American street criminal gang located in the city o f Colton, California.

org:criminal-class-locastas.description: The Criminal Class Locastas are a Hispanic Sureño-affiliated street gang based in South Los Angeles, operating under Mexican Mafia authority.

org:del-monte-crips.description: The Del Monte Blocc Crips (DBC) or Del Monte Blocc Gangster Crips (DBGC), due to their affiliation with Gangster Crips 3x. They are mostly an African-American criminal street gang located on the West Side in Anaheim, California. The Del Monte Blocc Crips are boundaries are Lincoln and Orange Avenues between Western Ave and Beach Blvd.

org:down-ass-pimp.description: Down Ass Pimp Gang (DAP) was a predominantly African-American street gang located in South Los Angeles.

org:duroc-crips.description: The Low-end clique share their neighborhood with the Duarte East Side, a known Hispanic street gang.

org:five-time-hometown-crips.description: Street Crips are an African-American criminal street gang based on the West Side of San Bernardino, California.

org:gundry-blocc-crips.description: The Gundry Blocc Crips (GBC) are a predominantly African-American Crip set based near Gundry Avenue in the Compton/North Long Beach area of Los Angeles.

org:holmes-crips.description: Holmes Street Watts (HSW) are an African-American street gang located on the East Side in the Willowbrook / Watts community of South Los Angeles, California. Their neighborhood is West of the Kenneth Hahn Plaza (Shopping Center) from 117th Street to 118th Street, between Wilmington Ave and Compton Ave. This gang as been active since the 1980’s.

org:south-side-village-crips.description: The South Side Village Crips (SSVC), also known as the Hate Gang are primarily, but not exclusively, an African-American criminal street gang operating in the Southern region of Pomona (P-Town), California.

org:twilightzone.description: Street Twilight Zone Crips are a predominately Black street gang located in the Southern section of Los Angeles between Broadway & Avalon.

org:walnut-blocc-crips-lynwood.description: The Walnut Blocc Crips (WBC) are primarily an African-American street gang located on the East Side of Lynwood, California.

org:by-yourself-hustler-crips.description: By Yourself Hustler Crips (BYHC) are an African-American Gangster Crip set based on the West Side of Los Angeles, California, active in the Faircrest Heights, Miracle Mile, and Palms areas.

org:mafia-crips-los-angeles.description: The Mafia Crips are a large African-American Crip alliance consisting of several street gang sets that originated throughout South Los Angeles, California.

org:shot-gun-crips-in-gardena.description: Shot Gun Crips are a predominately African-American street gang in the South Bay city of Gardena that formed around 1975.

org:tiny-oriental-crips-san-diego.description: The Tiny Oriental Crips (TOC) are an Asian/Laotian-American street gang based on the West Side of Linda Vista and parts of the Mira Mesa, in the Central neighborhood of San Diego, California. The Tiny Oriental Crips are primarily consist of Laotians, but also include Hmong and Vietnamese as well as descendants of Khmer.

sources.title: Tiny Oriental Crips San Diego — UnitedGangs

org:pope-crips.description: The Pope Street Hustlers are primarily, but not exclusively, an African-American Street gangs they originated on the East Side of Lynwood, California. Their name is derived from a residential Street known as “Pope Avenue” a street which runs from Lavinia Avenue to Los Flores Boulevard.

org:donna-crips.description: Qs are one of the oldest African-American street gangs located in North Las Vegas, Nevada. Their name is derived from Donna street which is the heart of their gang. They were established in 1977, originally known as the GQs by original gangsters such as Ramont “Paps” Williams, after moving to Las Vegas from Compton, California.

org:blackmafiacripdog.description: Los Angeles Black Mafia Crip Dog was a predominately Black street gang located on the eastside of South Los Angeles during the 1970s approximately where the Main Street Crips formed.

org:altadena-blocc-crip-abc-in-altadena.description: Altadena Blocc Crip are a predominately African-American street gang located in the city of Altadena, just north of Pasadena. They have been active since about 1979/1980. ABCs Resting in Peace Tiny Ace Roc Baldie C-Neil Daniel “C-Roc” Fuller, 30 (March 6, 1969 – October 31, 2009) Lil C-Roc Casper Loc Criptonite Lil Cuz Flem Flam Goddy Guydie Ice Man L-Bone Lil Joker Baby Killa Rob Lil Loco Monk Ne.

org:97-gangster-crips.description: Gangster Crips are an African American street gang founded in the early 1980’s in Los Angeles, California. Their territory is concentrated in the 97th street area, east of the 110 Freeway and west of Avalon Blvd.

org:beach-town-mafia-crips-los-angeles.description: Beach Town Mafia Crip are an African American street gang located in the Watts section of South LA on the eastside of the city.

org:burnside-avenue-crip-los-angeles.description: Burnside Avenue Crip Gang are a predominately African-American street gang located in the West LA section of Los Angeles within the territory of Mansfield Gangster Crips.

org:east-side-players-los-angeles.description: African-American street gang based on the East Side of South Los Angeles, emerged during the 1970s–1980s gang proliferation era in South Central.

org:nogales-gangster-crips.description: The Nogales Gangster Crips (NGC), also known as the Nogales Street Gangster Crips (NSGC) or simply Nogales Crips (NC) are primarily, but not exclusively, an African-American street gang named after a residential street (Nogales St.) between Rio Linda Blvd and Marysville Blvd located in North Sacramento, California.

org:original-valley-gangster-crip-los-an.description: One of the first African-American Crip gangs in the San Fernando Valley, founded in the early 1970s. Widely regarded as the first Crip set in the Valley, contemporaries of the Pacoima Pirus.

org:pimp-player-hustler-gangster-crips.description: Crips, are an African-American street gang operating on the East Side of San Bernardino, California. They originated in the mid-1980’s, during the crack epidemic era. The PPHG Crips are also associated with the number 4x (4’s), similar to gangs under the 2x (Deuces)

sources.title: Pimp Player Hustler Gangster Crips — UnitedGangs

org:rollin-30s-tre-tre-crips.description: Crips are an African-American street gang located in the neighborhood of Northeast Denver, Colorado. They were founded in the mid-1980’s by a group of friends, Michael Asberry (Cyco Mike) and Philip Jefferson and Albert Jones, three teenagers with family ties to the Watts area in South Los Angeles, California.

org:65-menlo-crips-in-south-los-angeles-.description: The 65 Menlo Crips are a predominately African American street gang located in South Los Angeles off 65th Street & Menlo Avenue. They started off as the Menlo Boys and during the early 1980s and they had a positive relationship with the 52 Hoover Gangster Crips and 59 Hoovers, the dominant gangs in the area.

org:too-many-hoes-tmhg-los-angeles.description: An African-American Crip set based in South Los Angeles, part of the network of smaller Crip sets in the South Central area.

org:whitsett-avenue-crips.description: The Whitsett Avenue Crips (WAC) is predominantly a black street gang, located on the East Side of North Hollywood, San Fernando Valley, California. They originated during the late 1980s, named after Whitsett Ave, a local street which runs through the heart of their neighborhood, between Oxnard Street and Sherman Way.

org:81st-east-side-hustler-crips.description: The 81st Street Crips are primarily an African-American Street gang operating in Colorado Springs, Colorado. Their history dates back to the early 1990s, named after a street in South Los Angeles (81st Street), which is home to other gangs under the Hustler Crips umbrella such as the HardTime Hustler Crips as well as the East Side Hustler Cri.

sources.title: 81St Street East Side Hustler Crips — UnitedGangs

org:insane-crips-long-beach.description: The Insane Crips Gang (ICG) are the largest African-American criminal street gang located on the East Side of Long Beach, California. They are primarily located in Central Long Beach, originating on 21st Street and Lewis Ave, spreading to surrounding blocks.

org:linda-vista-crips-san-diego.description: The Linda Vista Crips (LVC), also known as the Linda Vista Rollin 60s Crips, are a multi-ethnic street gang operating on the north side of the Linda Vista community in San Diego, California.

sources.title: Linda Vista Crips San Diego — UnitedGangs

org:nip-crips.description: Nip Crips were a short lived predominately southeast Asian street gang in Long Beach, California. It members included Cambodian, Vietnamese, and Laotian members that were located near the intersection of Dawson & Anaheim. They became defunct during the early 1990s.

org:east-dago-mob-crips.description: The East Dago Mob Crips (EDMC) are a violent street gang residing on the East Side in the City Heights neighborhood in San Diego, California. They were originally known as “Playboy International” (PBI), but changed it to East Dago Mob Crips in 1985. The main cliques of the East Dago Mob Crips are 46th Street and 46th Street (Lil Neighbors).

org:westmob.description: Westmob is a primarily African-American street gang based in San Francisco, California, active since at least 1999. It claims territory in the Hunters Point projects and is known for a long-running conflict with the rival Big Block gang. The gang has been linked to rap music and drug trafficking, and its rivalry was featured in the 2002 documentary Straight Outta Hunters Point.

org:graveyard-crips.description: African-American Crip set based in the Pico neighborhood of Santa Monica, California, named for their proximity to Woodlawn Cemetery. Founded in the 1970s under the Gangster Crips umbrella, with long-running rivalries with Santa Monica 13 and Venice Shoreline Crips.

org:bedrocc.description: Bedrocc Gangster Crips was a predominately African-American street gang located in the West LA section of Los Angeles in the same area of the PlayBoy Gangster Crips.

org:brik-boy-crips-bbc-in-northside-long.description: The Brik Boy Crips (NSBBC) are an African-American Crip set based in North Long Beach, California. In 2016, they were indicted as part of a $14.3 million identity theft and tax fraud scheme.

org:hard-time-hustler-crips-los-angeles.description: The Hard Time Hustler Crips are a predominantly African-American Crip set operating in the Watts and South Los Angeles area, with multiple subsets along different streets including 78th, 88th, 93rd, and 103rd-104th.

org:73hustlercrip.description: Eastside 73 Hustler Crips are a predominately African-American street gang located on 73rd Street between Avalon and Wadsworth.

org:hustler-crips.description: The Hustler Crips is a large umbrella consisting of several individual African-American street gangs located throughout South Los Angeles, California.

org:hard-time-hustler-crips.description: Hard Time Hustler Crips are the only sub-set under the Hard Time Hustler Crips to reside on the East Side of South Los Angeles, California.

sources.title: The 88 Hard Time Hustler Crips — UnitedGangs

org:homes-side-hustler-los-angeles.description: The Homes Side Hustler Crips are a subset of the Hustler Crips alliance operating in the South Los Angeles area, part of the network of Hustler Crip sets that emerged in the mid-1970s.

org:latin-kings-chicago.description: The Almighty Latin King and Queen Nation (ALKQN) was founded in 1954 in Chicago's Humboldt Park by Ramon Santos as the Imperials, a Puerto Rican progress movement. They merged with other gangs facing violence from Italian and Greek greasers. Now one of the largest Hispanic gangs worldwide with 20,000-35,000 in the KMC faction (31 states) and 2,000-7,500 in the NYC Bloodline faction (5 states). Colors are black and gold. Members of the People Nation alliance. Their symbols include the five-point sacred crown and the Lion. They maintain links with Mexican cartels (Sinaloa) and American Mafia families. Notable prosecutions include 'King Blood' Luis Felipe (250 years) and 'Corona' Augustin Zambrano (60 years).

org:mickey-cobras-chicago.description: The Mickey Cobras are a Chicago gang affiliated with the People Nation, originally founded in 1954 as the Egyptian King Cobras on Chicago's West Side. Named after leader Henry 'Mickey' Cogwell who was assassinated by Jeff Fort in 1977 for refusing to join the El Rukns. Their colors are green, black, and red. Allied with the Chicago Outfit (organized crime link documented since 1970). They operated a large-scale heroin ring netting over $100,000/week until the 2001 federal prosecution of 15 members. Described by US Attorney Patrick Fitzgerald as one of Chicago's 'super-gangs.'

sources.title: Almighty Black P. Stone Nation — Wikipedia

edges.evidence: Cobras and Vice Lords would have a tight alliance in these apartments and this remains a Cobra stronghold currently.

In order to earn their keeps the Cobras had to fight a violent gang war against the Black Gangster Disciples that ruled most of the buildings. Cobras even boldly sold crack cocaine in these buildings directly competing with the Black Gangster Disciples.
The HGs were close allies with the Black Gangster Disciples, therefore, they only had to fight it out with Mickey Cobras and the Vice Lords
Spade Town Insane Deuces were heavy at war with Mickey Cobras from Fuller Park.
Emerald Knights and Latin Souls were allied with the Cobrastones (Mickey Cobras) from 53rd and Shields in Fuller Park.
By 1962, Mickey Cogwell arranged an alliace/merger with the Blackstone Rangers. Now that the Cobras were Blackstone Rangers they would go by the name 'Cobrastones.'
Once the Cobras separated from the Stones they retained peace with the EL Rukns and joined the People alliance on the streets.

Jerome Cogwell was the younger brother of Mickey Cogwell and was about 20 years old when he was killed. The murder was done by the Devil's Disciples which was the main gang allied with the Disciple alliance.

In 1985, Jeff Fort ordered the murders of several King Cobras in April of 1985. According to court documents in the case of United States vs. Andrews, Jeff Fort wanted complete control of the heroin trade at 54th and Bishop in the Back of the Yards neighborhoods and 67th and Stoney Island in the Woodlawn neighborhood.

Within weeks of the formation of the King Cobras in the early months of 1960, the Egyptian Cobras and King Cobras came to an agreement to come together as one fighting force that would be somewhat combined as the 'Egyptian King Cobras.'
Cobras are heavily invested in Woodlawn and have shared these streets close by the Black P Stones with on and off violent rivalries which goes back to 1958.

org:latin-counts-chicago.description: The Latin Counts is an Indigenous Latino American street gang based in Chicago, Illinois, with a significant branch in Detroit, Michigan. Founded in 1955 by Tejano youth in the Pilsen neighborhood, it is a member of the People Nation alliance. The gang is involved in drug trafficking, racketeering, and other criminal activities.

edges.evidence: After the killings of 'Mando' 'Snake' in 1984 and 'Micobrio' in 1984, who was killed by Racine Street Latin Counts, hardened La Raza more into a hard core gangbanging crew
On Tuesday August 21, 1979, Reno 'Maniac Mouse' Enriquez of the Latin Counts from address 1619 S. Loomis (17th and Loomis) shot Louie Limas to death

The Bishops became very close allies with the Latin Counts. Bishops and Counts had close family ties and perhaps the Counts wanted the Bishops to join but the Bishops wanted to go their own way but would vow to fight alongside the Counts in the wars against Ambrose and the Satan Disciples.

In January of 1996 Bishops and Counts got into an argument at a party... Later on that night a Latin Count named 'Rhino' gunned down a Bishop named 'Chuco' to avenge the death of the Counts' girlfriend. This started a vicious war between Bishops and Counts that would live on until present day.

The Pilsen area Brazers still were not fond of using firearms and now fought impressive battles against Latin Kings, Latin Counts, Bishops, Halsted Boys, Morgan Deuces, Villa Lobos and Latin Brothers in the area.
The newly arrived Gents found conflict with the Latin Kings as the Gents befriended the Latin Counts from 25th and California.
The counts also had rivalry with the Latin Kings after they moved to Coulter and Damen in 1966.

The Latin Brother originals were closely related to the Latin Counts and were in consideration for joining the Counts but for some reason it was decided for these guys to not be part of the Counts, therefore, these guys started the Latin Brothers, a decision that was honored by both the Counts and Brothers.
Bishops and Counts hung out at 19th and Racine and this made Racine Boys, Bishops and Counts allies and good friends.
The Counts were invited to join the Nation of the Peoples alliance it formed in 1981.
This alliance drew up a truce with long time enemies the Latin Kings

In the year 1992, Don Juan 'Benny' Rios moved to Cicero at the age of 14 and got acquainted with the Twelfth Street Players at Roosevelt and Austin. Rios was the founder of Cicero Latin Counts. The Twelfth Street Players then offered up territory by Parkholm Park by 18th and 51st
the guns were blazing as the Players were taking on the whole BCN in a deadly gang war... The Player and Count war has never been forgiven
The Counts out here hung out with the Latin Dragons during these years.
The Latin Stones became slightly allied with Latin Counts in the area but mostly allied with Black P Stones.
A war erupted in the mid-2000s with the Latin Counts which was costly and at this time the Latin Stones began to shrink but have remained active as a low-key group.
The Almighty Popes were immediately aligned tightly with Latin Kings, Twelfth Street Players and Latin Counts
by 1981 drama did pop off and the Party People could no longer function as just a party crew and were forced to gangbang against the Latin Counts and Ambrose who gave them problems.

Pee Wee Cullerton Deuces now identified with the People alliance on the streets as they now had a tight relationship with Latin Kings, Stone Kents, Villa Lobos, Party Gents and Latin Counts in the area

King Cobras had a very nasty war with Latin Counts of 108th and Hoxie. The Latin Counts killed one of King Cobra top gunner "Lil Rico" in the 1990s. In revenge the King Cobras killed the leader of the 108th and Hoxie section known as "Maniac."
The Bishops retaliated and killed a Racine Boy which started the war between Racine Boys against Bishops and Counts.

The Cobras in Cicero made lots of noise but had a unlikely friendship with Cicero Latin Counts until a fight broke out at a party at 18th and 47th after a Cobra smashed a beer bottle into a Latin Count's teeth. After this incident the war with the Counts was costly and Cicero police were cracking down on the gangbanging heading into the 2000s, this Cobra section had to close.
There were a few People alliance mobs that touched down in Cicero in the 1993-1994 years which included Bishops and Latin Counts.
In the year 1995, the Dragons began to have friction with the Latin Counts. During this friction, war began which led to the death of Henry Garcia.
The Sin City Boys are a Folk Nation gang at least since 1981 that has engaged in wars with People Nation gangs around them like Latin Kings , Bishops , Latin Angels and Latin Counts .

When the Royal Popes formed in the year 1973 they immediately established a tight alliance with the Latin Counts from 18th & Loomis and the two gangs have been tight ever since. This unity was called the 'P.C.N' or 'Pope Count Nation.'
Latin Count and Spanish Vice Lords mural showing unity in the 1980s.
Rivals referenced in reporting or public records have included: Latin Counts, Vice Lords, People Nation (5X), Bloods
the MadDawg Bloods were sometimes called the Black Counts due to close ties with the Latin Counts.
sporadic conflicts with groups like the Latin Counts (5600 TMC)

org:chiwest.description: Chi-West is a People Nation street gang founded 1958 in Chicago.

edges.evidence: Chi-West became even less visible by 1989 as many thought the organization had gone extinct but Chi-West was still active and even drew up a reluctant alliance with the Damen and Crystal Latin Kings that brought Chi-West into the People alliance in the early 1990s.

At Maplewood and Charleston Chi-West became close allies with a Puerto Rican gang the Latin Lovers. These two groups hung out all the time and had serious wars with Imperial Gangsters and the Paulina Barry Community.
