In 1964 the Latin Kings formed which became the ultimate nemesis for white gangs but Chi-West kept the Latin Kings completely out of Ukrainian Village, they never claimed a single piece of turf here.

Chi-West became even less visible by 1989 as many thought the organization had gone extinct but Chi-West was still active and even drew up a reluctant alliance with the Damen and Crystal Latin Kings that brought Chi-West into the People alliance in the early 1990s.
In the year 1966, the Maniac Latin Disciples formed and one of the primary reasons they formed was because of Chi-West.
In 1970 the Insane Dragons formed and of course, one of the main reasons they formed was because of Chi-West.
Cortland and Harding only lasted until 1978 because Chi-West killed the north side leader of the Bishops "Cougar."
instead they fought white greaser clubs like C-Notes , Chi-West , Taylor Jousters , Gaylords and the Simon City gang
The Warlord's worst enemies were Taylor Jousters , Chi-West , Gaylords and PVR/PVPs which were white greaser clubs that wanted to flush out groups of Puerto Rican youths.

org:conservative-vice-lords.description: The Conservative Vice Lords (CVL) are the oldest and original faction of the Almighty Vice Lord Nation, founded in 1958 in North Lawndale, Chicago. In the late 1960s, CVL Inc. became a legitimate non-profit opening businesses and job training programs. The positive era collapsed in the early 1970s when federal investigations shut them down.

sources.title: Conservative Vice Lords - Chicago Gang History

org:playboys-ventures-pulaski-park-rice-.description: Austin neighborhood Established 1971-mid-1980s Sections of Austin Division & Waller Established 1971 as Playboys (headquarters PVR) Menard & Thomas Established as Rice Boys (PVR) Central Ave & Division (Austin Bowl headquarters, PVP) Crystal to Rice, Long to Pine Established as Rice Boys

sources.title: Playboys, Ventures, Pulaski Park, Rice Boys - Chicago Gang History

edges.evidence: Jousters allied with the Playboys and Ventures to create the P.V.J or Playboys Ventures and Jousters.
In West Humboldt Park Playboys and Gaylords came together at North Ave and Kedvale to create the P.V.G or Playboys Ventures and Gaylords.

At school they quickly got to see that the Italian gangs were running the high school, primarily the Dominos, Lazy Gents, C-Notes and Gaylords. These Italian gangs bullied and harassed the Naturals and their friends because they would get attention from girls at school or would gather a following around them.

These Italian gangs bullied and harassed the Naturals and their friends because they would get attention from girls at school or would gather a following around them.

org:almighty-black-p-stone-nation.description: American street gang founded in Chicago in the late 1950s by Eugene Hairston and Jeff Fort. Originally the Blackstone Rangers, it later adopted an Islamic faction called El Rukn. The gang is involved in drug trafficking, extortion, money laundering, murder, and robbery, and is part of the People Nation alliance. It is considered Chicago's most powerful street gang and has been linked to terrorism conspiracies with Libya.

org:almighty-saints.description: The Almighty Saints are a Hispanic-American People Nation street gang founded in the early 1960s by Polish youth at Davis Square Park in Back of the Yards, Chicago. Later became largely Hispanic as the community's ethnic makeup changed. Named after 'The Saint' TV series (stickman logo). Unique in Chicago for maintaining the same territory since the 1960s. Initially refused to join Folk or People Nations (renegades), but joined People Nation in late 1980s/early 1990s through the Latin Kings for prison protection. In 2004, 'Operation Broken Halo' arrested 25 members. In 2018, member Ernesto Godinez shot undercover ATF agent Kevin Crump in the face during a nighttime operation — prompting a $61K reward manhunt. Godinez got 16 years.

org:almighty-gaylords-nation.description: The Almighty Gaylords Nation is a European-American Chicago street gang founded in 1952 in the West Town neighborhood at Grand and Noble Street, originally composed mostly of Italians. By 1979, they ranked as the fourth most powerful gang in Chicago with 1,500 members, known as 'Chicago's largest White street gang.' At their peak in the early 1980s they had 6,000 members across the North, West, and South Sides. Part of the People Nation alliance. Allied with Latin Kings, Vice Lords, and Indiana Aryan Brotherhood. They declined in the 1990s as leaders were incarcerated and white populations suburbanized. In 2011, nine suburban members were arrested for drug dealing and gun trafficking.

org:satan-disciples-chicago.description: The Insane Gangster Satan Disciples are a Latino (Mexican) Folk Nation street gang founded in 1960 by 'King Satan' (Ray) in North Lawndale, Chicago near 15th and Talman. Grew to almost 10,000 members with territory across Brighton Park, Heart of Chicago, Marquette Park, West Town, and suburbs. Operate in an organized crime manner prioritizing profitable territory.

org:black-disciples-chicago.description: The Black Disciples (BDs) are an African-American street gang with 15,000 members that originated in Chicago. In 1958, teenagers from Hyde Park, Englewood, and Kenwood formed the 'Devil's Disciples' — founders included David Barksdale, Richard Strong, Leonard Longstreet, and Prince Old Timer. In 1966, Barksdale created the 'Black Disciples Nation' to boost recruitment into the thousands. In 1969, Larry Hoover merged his Supreme Gangsters with Barksdale's Disciples to form the BGDN. After Barksdale died of kidney failure in 1974, ideological differences split the alliance into the Gangster Disciples and Black Disciples by 1981. Mickey Bull led the BDs until his murder by GDs in August 1991, triggering permanent war. In 1994, 11-year-old member Robert 'Yummy' Sandifer accidentally killed a bystander and was then executed by fellow BDs. Had 300+ sets by 1974. Present in 10+ states. Notable members include Chief Keef, Lil Durk, and King Von.

org:maniac-latin-disciples-chicago.description: The Maniac Latin Disciples (MLD) are a predominantly Hispanic but multi-racial Folk Nation street gang founded in 1966 in Humboldt Park, Chicago with 7,500-10,000 members. Originated as a Puerto Rican baseball club (Latin Scorpions) harassed by white gangs like Simon City Royals and Gaylords. Albert 'Hitler' Hernandez positioned himself as King at Hirsch & Rockwell — he was stabbed to death by two Latin Kings in 1972 at age 18, igniting permanent war. Dominated the heroin trade at Rockwell & Potomac ('The Twilight Zone'). In 1989, leaders met with Spanish Cobras and Two Sixers to form Spanish Growth and Development (SGD), modeled after the Chicago Outfit. Spread to Ohio, Quad Cities, Boston, Wisconsin, Michigan, Indiana, Tennessee, Texas, New York, and Florida. Identifiers: heart with devil horns/tail, the Monk, swastika, trident, Detroit Tigers 'D'.

org:imperial-gangsters-chicago.description: The Almighty Imperial Gangsters are a Latino (Puerto Rican) Folk Nation street gang founded in 1970 in Logan Square, Chicago. Originally called 'Imperial Spanish Gangsters,' started by Carlos 'Mexico' Quintanilla who merged with the 'Mighty Grand' crew (Greek, Norwegian, Polish and Ukrainian kids fighting Italian gangs). Colors of black and pink adopted in 1975 after Latin Kings shot and killed 17-year-old female member Nora 'Morena' Salazar. Part of the United Latino Organization (1978-1992). Their rounded crown symbol comes from the Imperial Margarine brand. Territory in Logan Square, Hermosa, Belmont-Cragin, and Franklin Park suburb ('The Jungle').

org:two-six.description: The Two Six (Gangster Two Six) are a Latino (Mexican) Folk Nation street gang founded circa 1970 in Little Village (South Lawndale), Chicago. Started as 'Twenty Sixth Street Boys' — a group of teens at 26th and Sawyer who hung out at a Jack in the Box, named after the street Latin Kings called them by. The Ayala family transformed them into a major gang in 1977, establishing colors (black and beige from khaki pants at the Alfonso and Elias store) and connections to Mexican drug cartels through the Montemayor family. Leader David 'El Jefe' Ayala ran the gang from prison until 1996. Grew to nearly 10,000 members with sections across Chicago's southwest side and suburbs including Cicero, Berwyn, and Joliet.

org:almighty-insane-popes-nation.description: The North Side Almighty Insane Popes are a mostly European-American Folk Nation street gang formed in the late 1950s in Chicago. Originally a Greek greaser gang at Lawrence and Rockwell that had problems with Latin Kings. Rather than joining the Simon City Royals, they formed their own gang. Name originally stood for 'Protecting Our People Eliminating Scum.' Peak membership of 300-500 in mid-1960s to early 1970s. Main membership was Polish (Albany Park), German, Irish, and Scandinavian (Portage Park, Jefferson Park). In the 1980s, some members were children of Chicago firefighters and police officers. Allied with Simon City Royals and Imperial Gangsters.

org:la-raza-chicago.description: La Raza Nation is a Hispanic (Mexican/Central American) Folk Nation street gang founded in Chicago in 1973. Colors are green, white, and red (Mexican flag). Though Folk-affiliated, they've warred with fellow Folk gangs including Gangster Disciples and Maniac Latin Disciples. Expanded beyond Chicago to Wisconsin, Ohio, Florida, and Georgia. In 1985, opened a chapter in West Humboldt Park ('DK Town'). In 2004, 89 high-ranking members arrested in Naples, FL. In 2005, listed by ICE as one of 54 violent gangs targeted in Operation Community Shield. 17% of US law enforcement agencies reported La Raza presence in their jurisdictions. Symbols include the Mexican flag, cross, and six-point star.

org:spanish-gangster-disciples-chicago.description: The Spanish Gangster Disciples (SGD) are a predominantly Indigenous Latino American Folk Nation street gang founded in the early 1980s by Rudy Rios (former Latin Scorpions/MLD member) in South Chicago at 88th & Houston. An offshoot of the Gangster Disciples — Rios transformed the prison-based 'Spanish Growth and Development' concept into a street gang with 'blessings' from the BGD hierarchy. The SGD concept aimed to unify all 'Latin Folks' organizations. A separate Northside chapter formed in 1983 at Clark & Olive, becoming known for high-quality marijuana sales. Founder Rudy Rios was assassinated in 1995, triggering leadership instability — two subsequent leaders were also killed. Internal feuds with 'Demon' (a rogue leader) caused wars with former allies MLDs and Imperial Gangsters. Ambrose took over most of their South Chicago territory by 1997. Still active in Albany Park, suburbs (Elgin, Wheeling, Palatine, Waukegan).

edges.evidence: Ambrose set up a serious section that viciously battled Latin King, Latin Counts, Spanish Vice Lords, Vice Lords, Latin Dragons and Spanish Gangster Disciples at times.
The Latin Stones mainly engaged in wars with Gangster Disciples, Ambrose and especially Spanish Gangster Disciples.

SGDs were advancing on multiple neighborhoods Two Six had a presence in such as Albany Park, Belmont-Cragin, Portage Park and even Little Village for awhile. These SGD settlements brought lots of war with Two Six
More problems would arise in Elgin when the Deuces shot a member of the Spanish Gangster Disciples named Gizmo in the back of the head with a .22 caliber pistol which eventually killed him.
By this time the Latin Dragons developed a heavy rivalry with King Cobras and Spanish Gangster Disciples.
Dragons also united with the Spanish Gangster Disciples and then joined the Folk alliance both in prison and on the streets.
The Stones arrived right at about the same time as the Spanish Gangster Disciples and immediately they started gunning at each other.
During their time in prison, they were allied with the new "Folks" alliance that was developing between 1978-1979.

The Saints ran 87th and Houston while Royal Knights ran 88th to 89th and Commercial area, these groups were neighbors to each other... because the Saints previously ran 87th and Houston for decades and because the last remnant of their members were involved, they wanted their name as part of this. The new group was initially called "Saint Disciples."
In all these neighborhoods the SGDs found intense rivalry with the Latin Kings making them one of their first enemies up north.
Eventually in the 1980s the SGDs opened Olive and Clark in the Edgewater neighborhood which was dominated by the P.R. Stones street gang and once they found out about SGDs a nasty war broke out
88th and Houston fell apart and Ambrose grew a set on that corner but this was granted to Ambrose from SGD in 1995, Ambrose did not muscle in and take it.
The only Folks on the southeast side were SGDs, Gangster Disciples and King Cobras.
The Folk Hispanic gangs Vice Lords absolutely will not deal with are: La Raza, Spanish Gangster Disciples, Satan Disciples, Brazers and Ambrose
Maintain working relationships with select Gangster Disciple subsets, particularly Spanish Gangster Disciple (SGD) sets.

org:unknown-assassins.description: Unknown Assassins was a Folk Nation street gang founded 1982 in Chicago. Disbanded around 2000.

org:maniac-campbell-boys.description: Maniac Campbell Boys is a Folk Nation street gang founded 1984 in Chicago.

sources.title: Maniac Campbell Boys - Chicago Gang History

edges.evidence: Now Maniac Campbell Boys got into vicious wars with Insane Dragons, Spanish Cobras, YLO Cobras and Insane Campbell Boys.
Eventually the Jivers from East Humboldt Park stopped warring with the MLDs and Maniac Campbell Boys and began hanging out with them again
Right away the Maniac Campbell Boys became a "Maniac" allied organization and were taken right into the Folk alliance under the wing of the Maniac Latin Disciples.
Right away the Maniac Campbell Boys became a "Maniac" allied organization and were taken right into the Folk alliance under the wing of the Maniac Latin Disciples.
Now Maniac Campbell Boys got into vicious wars with Insane Dragons, Spanish Cobras, YLO Cobras and Insane Campbell Boys.
Now Maniac Campbell Boys got into vicious wars with Insane Dragons, Spanish Cobras, YLO Cobras and Insane Campbell Boys.
Now Maniac Campbell Boys got into vicious wars with Insane Dragons, Spanish Cobras, YLO Cobras and Insane Campbell Boys.

org:two-ones-folks-21stleavitt.description: Two Ones (Folks, 21st&Leavitt) was a Folk Nation street gang founded 1984 in Chicago. Disbanded around 2000.

org:insane-majestics.description: Insane Majestics is a Folk Nation street gang founded circa 1985 in Chicago.

edges.evidence: SDs only allied with Gangster Disciples from Cicero and Insane Majestics, the Majestics were turned Insane and brought into Folks thanks to the Cicero SDs.
the MPCs were secretly meeting with the Satan Disciples to discuss joining the Folk Nation under the SD's wing
The Two Two Boys of 31st and Laramie took the MPCs under their wing and basically mentored them all about gang banging and assisted them in their wars with the People Nation gangs in the area.
The Two Twos were so upset they started a vicious war with the Majestics right on each other's front lawns.
The arch enemies of Majestics are the Maniac Latin Disciples and Latin Kings.
The arch enemies of Majestics are the Maniac Latin Disciples and Latin Kings.
In 1988 drama started to come to the MPCs as Satan Disciples, Two Sixs and Latin Kings began giving them a hard time.

org:krazy-get-down-boys.description: Ashburn neighborhood 90s-present years Sections of Ashburn 77th to 79th, Cicero to Tripp (Rainey Park, Purple City) Homan from 77th to 79th 83rd to 85th, Kostner to Kenneth 81st & Kostner 81st & Tripp 90s, 2000s, 2020s (present years) 83rd Place & Knox 90s-present years 79th & Pulask

sources.title: Krazy Get Down Boys - Chicago Gang History

edges.evidence: Ambrose and KGBs were now considered 'first cousins.'

Hitman G took over the KGBs and arranged for the KGBs to go from being a neutral party crew/future Ambrose to having their own full fledged gang that would be allied with Ambrose and the Folk alliance.
In the year 1993, the Satan Disciples moved into nearby 59th and Spaulding in the Gage Park community and this sparked a war with the Satan Disciples because of their aggressive recruitment.
KGBs would eventually conflict with fellow Folks Two Six and La Raza.
KGBs would eventually conflict with fellow Folks Two Six and La Raza.
The KGBs would also fight a vicious war with Saints and Latin Kings.
The KGBs would also fight a vicious war with Saints and Latin Kings.

org:outlaw-gangster-disciples.description: A subset of the Gangster Disciples street gang, formed in the early 1990s in Chicago's Cabrini-Green housing project by Charles 'Big Chuck' Dorsey. It engages in drug trafficking, robbery, extortion, and murder, with an estimated 25,000–50,000 members as of 2009. The gang has expanded to Chicago's South Side and has been documented in the U.S. military.

org:spook-hunters.description: White racist youth gang based in South Gate, California, active from the 1940s to the 1950s. Formed in reaction to the growing African American population in Los Angeles, the gang opposed integration and racial segregation, starting fights and intimidating black youths. Their activities prompted the formation of black protective gangs such as the Slausons.

org:aryan-nations.description: Aryan Nations is a North American neo-Nazi organization founded in the 1970s by Richard Girnt Butler, originally based in Hayden Lake, Idaho. It adheres to Christian Identity doctrine, a white supremacist interpretation of Christianity. The group was classified as a terrorist threat by the FBI in 2001 and described as the first nationwide terrorist network in the US and Canada.

org:lynwood-vikings.description: The Lynwood Vikings is a deputy gang within the Los Angeles County Sheriff's Department (LASD), formerly based at the Lynwood station. Composed of sworn deputy sheriffs, it has been described as a neo-Nazi, white supremacist gang engaged in racially motivated hostility. Members have been involved in police brutality and corruption, with tattoos symbolizing officer-involved shootings.

edges.evidence: Disciples flipped many Del Vikings and the Del Vikings would remain loyal to Disciple concepts

org:aryan-brotherhood-of-texas.description: Texas is a white supremacist prison gang operating in Texas state prisons. Founded in the early 1980s by Texas inmates after being denied affiliation with the original Aryan Brotherhood, it emerged as one of the largest and most violent prison gangs in the United States. The group is responsible for numerous murders, drug trafficking, extortion, and other violent crimes.

org:the-order.description: Neo-Nazi organization active in the United States from September 1983 to December 1984. Founded by Robert Jay Mathews, the group raised funds through armed robbery and was involved in the murder of radio host Alan Berg. Inspired by The Turner Diaries, it aimed to overthrow the U.S. government and establish a white homeland. The group was dismantled after a shootout that killed Mathews.

sources.title: The Covenant%2C the Sword%2C and the Arm of the Lord — Wikipedia

org:white-aryan-resistance.description: White Aryan Resistance (WAR) was a white supremacist and neo-Nazi organization in the United States, founded and formerly led by former Ku Klux Klan Grand Dragon Tom Metzger. It was based in Warsaw, Indiana, and described itself as an 'openly white-racist' organization. The group published a newspaper and produced a public-access cable television show. It is now defunct.

org:american-front.description: White supremacist organization founded in San Francisco, California by Bob Heick in 1984. Originally modeled after the British National Front, it later transformed into a political organization with skinhead and non-skinhead members. The group held White Workers Day marches and published the tabloid Aryan Warrior. By 1989, it had units in 14 American states.

org:aryan-circle.description: The Aryan Circle is a white supremacist neo-Nazi prison gang operating in the United States, founded in 1985 by Mark 'Cowboy' Gaspard in the Texas Department of Corrections as a splinter group from the Aryan Brotherhood of Texas. It is the second-largest prison gang in Texas, with members involved in drug trafficking, murder, and extortion. The gang remains active in state and federal prisons.

org:public-enemy-no-1.description: Public Enemy No. 1 (PEN1) is a street gang with prison presence formed in Long Beach, California, in the 1980s. Based in Orange County, it engages in identity theft, credit card fraud, and methamphetamine trafficking. The gang has ties to the Aryan Brotherhood and Nazi Lowriders. As of 2004, it had around 200 members statewide, with an estimated 400-500 members currently.

org:hammerskins.description: Neo-Nazi group formed in 1988 in Dallas, Texas, focused on producing and promoting white power rock music. It has chapters in the US, Canada, Europe, Australia, and New Zealand, and is described as the best-organized neo-Nazi skinhead group in the US. Members have been involved in violent attacks and hate crimes, though not centrally organized.

sources.title: Outlaws MC criminal allegations and incidents — Wikipedia

org:solid-wood-soldiers.description: Neo-Nazi prison gang and organized crime syndicate based in Texas, founded in 1990 at Snake River Correctional Institution in Oregon. Known for drug trafficking, assault, murder, and racketeering, with strong ties to other white supremacist groups. Uses distinctive tattoos and symbols, including the number four representing honor, courage, respect, and loyalty.

org:aryan-republican-army.description: White nationalist terrorist gang that robbed 22 banks in the Midwest from 1994 to 1996. Founded in 1992 by Donna Langan and Richard Lee Guthrie Jr., the group stockpiled weapons for a race war and allegedly conspired with Timothy McVeigh. Members were arrested in 1996 after informants cooperated with the FBI.

org:universal-aryan-brotherhood.description: Active neo-Nazi white supremacist prison gang in the United States, primarily based in Oklahoma. Founded in 1993 by Wilhelm Mast within the Oklahoma Department of Corrections. The gang operates both inside and outside prison walls, engaging in racketeering, kidnapping, murder, drug trafficking, and other crimes. It has no connection to the original Aryan Brotherhood.

org:imperial-klans-of-america.description: White supremacist, white nationalist, neo-Nazi paramilitary organization founded in 1996 by Ron Edwards in Dawson Springs, Kentucky. It was the second largest Klan group in the United States until the late 2000s, and briefly the largest in the early 2000s. The group has been weakened by legal judgments and Edwards' imprisonment, but remained active as of 2020 with only two chapters by 2012.

org:soldiers-of-aryan-culture.description: A large white supremacist prison gang in the United States, founded in 1997 by Tracy David 'Tinman' Swena in Utah. The gang operates both inside and outside prison walls, engaging in drug trafficking, extortion, and violence. It has a paramilitary ranking structure and requires members to attack sex offenders and informants.

org:european-kindred.description: The European Kindred (EK) is a white supremacist prison and street gang founded in 1998 in the Oregon prison system by David Patrick Kennedy. It operates primarily in Oregon, with an estimated 500 members. The gang is involved in drug trafficking, extortion, assaults, and dog fighting, and is known for its violent initiation and exit rituals.

org:fresnecks-the-fresnecks.description: The Fresnecks are a white supremacist street gang based in Fresno, California, aligned with the Aryan Brotherhood. They were the target of a major 2020 federal takedown (Operation Lucky Charm) resulting in over 100 arrests for violent crimes and drug trafficking.

sources.title: Fresnecks The Fresnecks — Prison Gang Project

org:keystone-united.description: Neo-Nazi group based in Pennsylvania, founded in 2001 by five men from Harrisburg. Originally focused on uniting white power skinheads across Pennsylvania, it expanded to multiple chapters and became one of the largest single-state racist skinhead crews in the US.

org:atomwaffen-division.description: International far-right extremist and neo-Nazi network formed in 2015 and based in the Southern United States. It expanded across the US and several other countries, described as one of the most violent neo-Nazi movements of the 21st century. Members have been responsible for murders, bombings, and planned terrorist attacks.

org:the-base.description: The Base is an international neo-Nazi organization and paramilitary training network formed in 2018 by Rinaldo Nazzaro. It advocates accelerationist and white nationalist ideology, aiming to establish white ethnostates through terrorism and violent overthrow of governments. The group operates training camps and has links to other far-right extremist groups.

org:barrio-maravilla-east-los-angeles.description: The Barrio Maravilla (MV) also known as Maravilla “Chapters” or Maravilisos consist of several smaller individual Mexican-American street gangs who originated in Unincorporated East Los Angeles, California. The Maravillas has been around since the early 1930s and are considered the first gangs in East Los Angeles.

org:el-hoyo-maravilla.description: El Hoyo Maravilla are primarily a Mexican-American criminal street gang located in Unincorporated East Los Angeles, California. They originated in 1935, the 1st generation was known as the “Originals” and the second generation was known as the “Cherries”.

org:happy-valley-los-angeles.description: Happy Valley Varrio Happy Valley Rifa is located in the Lincoln Heights section of Northeast Los Angeles just south of Rose Hill. This neighborhood has been around since at least the late 1930s and early 1940s and most members lived off Sierra Street and Lincoln Park Avenue.

org:whitefence.description: Eastside Varrio White Fence is a predominately Hispanic-American street gang from the Boyle Heights area of Los Angeles. It is considered one of the oldest and most respected neighborhoods in all of Los Angeles. It dates back to the late 1920s and they have been active in the same community for nearly 100 years. Their rivalry with El Hoyo Maravilla is the oldest gang rivalry in all of Southern California, and probably the entire United States.

org:varrio-avenues-43rd-57th-cyclones.description: The Avenues (Avenidas/AVEs) are a Mexican-American Sureño street gang in Northeast Los Angeles with ~800 members covering Highland Park, Cypress Park, Glassell Park, and Eagle Rock — once nearly 6 square miles. Started as a social club in the 1940s by the Flores brothers, they turned criminal when heroin hit in the late 1960s. Under Mexican Mafia control. Notorious for hate crimes against African-Americans (described as 'ethnic cleansing' by SPLC). In 1995, members killed 3-year-old Stephanie Kuhen (prompting President Clinton to speak out). In 2006, 43rd Aves members were federally prosecuted for hate crimes. Connected to the Sinaloa Cartel for meth/heroin supply. Main cliques: 43rd, 57th, Cypress, Division, Drew Street.

edges.evidence: After a three-month war the Vice Lords destroyed the Braves and this only left one other big gang, the Egyptian Cobras

org:marianna-maravilla-east-los-angeles.description: The Marianna Maravilla (MMV), are a Mexican-American subset of the larger Barrio Maravilla Gang, which consist several of smaller criminal street gangs operating in the same barrio in East Los Angeles, California.

sources.title: Marianna Maravilla East Los Angeles — UnitedGangs

org:primera-flats-in-boyle-heights-los-a.description: Eastside Primera Flats is a predominately Hispanic street gang located in the Boyle Heights section of Los Angeles just east of the Los Angeles River. Primera Flats is among the oldest street gangs in Los Angeles dating back to the Russian community that occupied the flats during the very early 1900s.

org:azmv.description: Arizona Maravilla (AMV) is a Mexican-American street gang and clique within the Maravilla gang cluster in East Los Angeles. The Maravilla area spans 8.25 square miles with at least 14 active gangs, making it one of the oldest concentrations of Latino gang activity in the US.

org:fishermv.description: Fisher Maravilla (FSLMV) is a predominately Latino street gang in East Los Angeles and they are one among many other MaraVilla gangs in East Los Angeles.

org:fraser-maravilla.description: Fraser Maravilla is a clique of the Maravilla gang network in East Los Angeles. The Maravilla area contains approximately 3,500 gang members across at least 14 active cliques, with roots dating to the 1930s.

org:gage-maravilla.description: Gage MaraVilla is a Chicano street gang and clique of the historic Maravilla gang network in East Los Angeles, based near Gage Avenue in the unincorporated Belvedere area.

org:high-times-maravilla.description: High Times Maravilla is a Chicano street gang and clique of the historic Maravilla gang network in East Los Angeles. Named distinctly from other Maravilla cliques, they operate within the 8.25-square-mile Maravilla area that has contained at least 14 active gangs since the mid-20th century.

org:juarez-maravilla.description: Juarez MaraVilla is a Chicano street gang and clique of the historic Maravilla gang network in East Los Angeles.

org:kernmv.description: Kern Maravilla (KMV) is a predominately Latino street gang based in East Los Angeles and it is part of the large Maravilla association of several varrios throughout Los Angeles.

org:lomitamv.description: Lomita Maravilla (LMV) is a predominately Latino street gang located in East Los Angeles, an unincorporated section of Los Angeles County.

org:lopez-maravilla-los-angeles.description: Lopez MaraVilla is a predominately Hispanic-American street gang located in the unincorporated area of East Los Angeles. It is among one of the oldest Hispanic street gangs in East LA, going back to about the 1940s. They are one among many other MaraVilla gangs in East Los Angeles.

org:lote-maravilla.description: Lote Maravilla is a Chicano street gang and clique of the historic Maravilla gang network in East Los Angeles, one of at least 14 active cliques in the 8.25-square-mile Maravilla area with roots dating to the 1930s.

org:maravilla-family-gangs.description: The MaraVilla organization were one of the first gangs who refused to be taxed or share a portion of their criminal earnings with the Mexican Mafia (La Eme).

org:moeriya-maravilla.description: Moeriya Maravilla is a clique of the Maravilla gang complex in East Los Angeles, operating within the 8.25-square-mile Maravilla area with origins in the 1930s-era Mexican American barrios.

org:pomeroy-maravilla.description: A Chicano street gang and clique of the historic Maravilla gang network in East Los Angeles, named after Pomeroy Street. The Maravilla gangs originated in the 1930s-1940s.

org:project-boys-maravilla.description: A Chicano street gang from the East Los Angeles Maravilla neighborhood, associated with the housing projects. The Maravilla gangs were instrumental in the formation of the Mexican Mafia prison gang in the 1950s.

org:raskals-maravilla.description: Raskals Maravilla is a clique of the Maravilla gang complex in East Los Angeles, one of the oldest Mexican American gang networks dating back to the 1930s. The Maravilla area contains at least 14 active gangs aligned with the Mexican Mafia.

org:rock-maravilla.description: Rock Maravilla is a clique of the Maravilla gang network in East Los Angeles, originating in the 1930s Belvedere/Maravilla housing project area. The Maravilla gangs are notable for their 1990s rebellion against the Mexican Mafia.

org:cuatro-flats.description: Cuatro Flats is a predominately Latino street gang in the Boyle Heights section of Los Angeles off 4th Street.

org:cypress-park.description: Cypress Park is a long-standing Latino street gang based in the Cypress Park neighborhood of Northeast Los Angeles, dating back to the zoot suit era. Connected to the Mexican Mafia, they have been the target of federal RICO prosecutions for drug trafficking, extortion, and violent crimes.

org:geraghty-loma-from-east-la.description: Geraghty Loma is a predominately Hispanic street gang located in the unincorporated area of East Los Angeles. They are among one of the oldest gangs in the area.

sources.title: Geraghty Loma from East LA — StreetGangs

org:choppers12-los-angeles.description: Choppers 12 is a predominately Latino street gang located on the border of East Los Angeles, unincorporated LA County and the City of Commerce. They were founded around 1952 as a motorcylce social club and the number “12” referred to Midnight.

org:bighazard.description: Boyle Heights Big Hazard is a predominately Hispanic street gang located in the Boyle Heights section of Los Angeles in mostly the Ramona Gardens Housing projects owned by the City of Los Angeles.

org:breed-street.description: Breed Street is a Latino street gang based on Breed Street in the Boyle Heights neighborhood of East Los Angeles, one of the oldest gang-impacted areas in the city with gangs dating to the 1930s.

org:north.description: Monte Vista is a predominately Hispanic street gang located in Highland Park near Monte Vista & Avenue 53 where another gang, the Avenues, are located.

org:elhoyosoto.description: El Hoyo Soto was a predominately Hispanic gang in the Boyle Heights area of Los Angeles that was active from the 1940s to the 1960s.

org:frogtown.description: Varrio Frog Town Rifa (VFTR), also known as Frogtown 13, is a Latino Sureño street gang based in the Elysian Valley (Frogtown) neighborhood of Northeast Los Angeles along the Los Angeles River. They were part of a Mexican Mafia-orchestrated coalition of three Northeast LA gangs targeted in a 2016 federal RICO indictment for drug trafficking and tax collection from narcotics dealers.

org:glassell.description: Varrio Glassell Park was a predominately Latino street gang in the Glassell Park community in Northeast Los Angeles.

org:lileastside.description: Lil East Side is a predominately Hispanic street gangs in the Boyle Heights community of Los Angeles.

org:lowellstreet.description: Lowell Street Locos are a predominately Hispanic street gang located in the northeast section of Los Angeles.

org:nuevo-estrada-in-boyle-heights-estra.description: Varrio Nuevo Estrada is a predominately Hispanic street gang from the Boyle Heights section of Los Angeles. They formed in the Estrada Courts housing projects off Olympic Boulevard during the 1950s in response to the older more established varrios in the area taking on the name “Nuevo” to signify a new generation varrio.

sources.title: Nuevo Estrada In Boyle Heights Estrada Courts — Wikipedia

org:tercera-flats.description: Tercera Flats is a predominantly Latino street gang in the Boyle Heights neighborhood of East Los Angeles, one of the oldest barrio gangs in the area with roots dating to the 1940s.

org:rascals.description: Atwater Village Thee Rascals are a predominately Latino street gang in the Atwater section of Northeast Los Angeles.

org:varrio-boyle-heights-v-bhts-in-boyle.description: A Latino Sureño-affiliated street gang based in the Boyle Heights neighborhood of East Los Angeles, one of many barrio gangs in the historically Mexican-American community.

sources.title: Varrio Boyle Heights (v. BHTS) in Boyle Heights — StreetGangs

org:city-terrace.description: Varrio City Terrace (VCT) is a predominantly Mexican-American street gang based in the City Terrace neighborhood of unincorporated East Los Angeles.

org:highlandpark.description: Varrio HighLand Park (HLP) is a predominately Hispanic street gang located in the Highland Park area in Northeast Los Angeles in the City of Los Angeles.

org:tinyboys.description: Boyle Heights Varrio Tiny Boys are a predominately Latino street gang located in the Boyle Heights section of Los Angeles.

org:clarence-street-locos.description: A Latino Sureño-affiliated street gang based near Clarence Street in Boyle Heights, East Los Angeles.

org:clika-los-primos-clps-in-east-la.description: A Latino street gang based in East Los Angeles. 'Los Primos' means 'The Cousins' in Spanish, operating as a Sureño-affiliated clique.

sources.title: Clika Los Primos (CLPS) in East LA — StreetGangs

org:crazy-riders-crs.description: A street gang based in East Los Angeles with largely Central American membership, particularly Salvadoran and Guatemalan immigrants. They were notably at war with the 18th Street Gang in the late 1980s and have been linked to multiple murders.

org:ghetto-style-crew.description: Ghetto Style Crew is a tagging crew and street gang based in the Boyle Heights area of East Los Angeles, operating in a densely gang-populated area containing over 30 active gangs within a 15-square-mile area.

org:krazy-ass-mexicans.description: The Krazy Ass Mexicans (KAM) are a Latino street gang based in East Los Angeles that has operated for multiple generations. In 2002, a gang injunction was filed against KAM for terrorizing the East Los Angeles community.

org:laguna-park-vikings.description: Laguna Park Vikings are a predominately Hispanic street gang in the East LA section of Los Angeles County.

org:winter-gardens-in-east-los-angeles.description: Winter Gardens are a predominately Latino street gangs in East Los Angeles, an unincorporated section of Los Angeles County. They are located between Olympic (north) and the 5 Freeway (south) from Atlantic (east) to Arizona (west).

sources.title: Winter Gardens in East Los Angeles — StreetGangs

org:stoners-13-in-east-la.description: Varrio Stoners 13 are a predominately Latino street gang located in East LA in unincorporated Los Angels County on the far eastside just north of the City of Commerce. The neighborhood is roughly around the community of Saybrook Park on Olympic and Westside Drive.

org:kingkobras.description: East Los Angeles King Kobras are a predominately Latino street gang located in the Unincorporated East Los Angeles within Los Angeles County.

org:fordmv.description: Ford MaraVilla is a predominately Hispanic street gang in the an unincorporated section of Los Angeles County called East Los Angeles.

org:the-lott-2nd-hood.description: The Lott is a predominately Hispanic street gang that started their initial varrio in East Los Angeles during the late 1970s stoner era. A second varrio started on the east side of East LA bordering the City of Montebello.

org:el-sereno-rifa-los-angeles.description: El Sereno Rifa is a predominately Latino street gang located in the El Sereno part of north east Los Angeles. Their main clicks of ESR have been The Ditch Locos, Guardia Street Locos, Ithaca Street Locos (ISL) and Locke Street.

org:hawaiian-gardens.description: Varrio Hate Gang are a Mexican-American street gang located in Hawaiian Gardens, Southeast Los Angeles County, California. Their history dates back to the 1940s with over 2,000 active members, best known for their hate crimes against African-Americans.

org:echo-park-in-los-angelels-los-angele.description: Varrio Echo Park is a predominately Latino street gang located in the Echo Park community of Los Angeles. Since the 1990s, this community has went through a radical transformation as developers been constructing new luxury buildings, building owners have been renovating existing properties and small businesses have expanded into the area.

sources.title: Echo Park In Los Angelels — Wikipedia

org:el-monte-flores.description: El Monte Flores (EMF) is the largest Hispanic gang in the San Gabriel Valley and one of the oldest in LA County, founded around 1950 in El Monte, California. Named after Barrio Las Flores (the flower fields/nurseries of El Monte). With 2,000+ members, they claim El Monte, South El Monte, North El Monte, and Mayflower Village. Sureño-affiliated and Mexican Mafia-controlled. In 2014, 41 members linked to the Mexican Mafia were indicted. Colors are blue and gray. They've expanded to Victorville, Arizona, Washington, New Mexico, Chicago, and Florida.

org:puente-13.description: Puente 13 (P13) is a Mexican-American Sureño street gang in La Puente, California, founded circa 1953 as the 'Bridgetown Gentlemen' (puente = bridge in Spanish). Mexican Mafia-backed — in 2013, two carnal (leaders) brothers were sentenced to life in federal RICO case. Claims the entire city of La Puente in the San Gabriel Valley. In 2018, 17 members were indicted on identity theft and methamphetamine charges after a DEA/Secret Service investigation that revealed they were smuggling drugs into Pitchess Detention Center. Known for violent criminal activities and illicit contributions to La Eme.

org:azusa-13.description: Azusa 13 is a Mexican American street gang based in Azusa, California, founded in the 1960s. It is one of the most aggressive Sureño gangs, with around 400 active members. The gang is affiliated with the Mexican Mafia and engages in drug trafficking, extortion, and hate crimes, including a campaign of ethnic cleansing against African Americans.

org:cherryville-in-pomona.description: Ville Pomona (CVP) is a predominately Latino gang neighborhood in Pomona that formed during the 1960s. It has several clicks including, Los Cherries, Los Dukes, and Mas Locotes.

org:valinda-flats-gang.description: Valinda Flats 13 Gang are primarily a Mexican-American criminal street gang located in the city of Valinda, San Gabriel Valley region of Los Angeles, CA.

org:outlaws-motorcycle-club.description: American Outlaws Association, is an international motorcycle gang founded in 1935 in McCook, Illinois. Classified as an organized crime syndicate by law enforcement, it is involved in drug trafficking, extortion, money laundering, and violent acts. The club has over 5,000 members across 441 chapters in 43 countries and is still active.

sources.title: Bandidos MC criminal allegations and incidents — Wikipedia

edges.evidence: A group of men in their early 20s had enough of the Outlaws and created their own club to deal with the rival biker gang that they called the 'Gay Lords.'

org:market-street-commandos.description: The Market Street Commandos was an outlaw motorcycle club that participated in the 1947 Hollister incident alongside the Boozefighters and Pissed Off Bastards of Bloomington. The club merged with the Hells Angels in 1954 to become their San Francisco chapter.

sources.title: Pissed Off Bastards of Bloomington — Wikipedia

org:galloping-goose-motorcycle-club.description: Outlaw motorcycle club founded in 1942 in Los Angeles, California by Dick Hershberg. Initially an informal racing team, it chartered in 1946 and expanded across the Midwest and South. Considered a second-tier outlaw motorcycle gang by law enforcement, it has been involved in violent crimes and has support clubs.

sources.title: Hells Angels MC criminal allegations and incidents — Wikipedia
Outlaws MC criminal allegations and incidents — Wikipedia

org:pissed-off-bastards-of-bloomington.description: An outlaw motorcycle club founded in Bloomington, California in 1945 by Norman Briggs. The club participated in the 1947 Hollister riot, which inspired the film The Wild One. It is considered a precursor to the Hells Angels, as a former member founded that club. The club remains active in California, Nevada, and Utah.

sources.title: Pissed Off Bastards Of Bloomington — Wikipedia

org:hells-angels-motorcycle-club.description: The Hells Angels Motorcycle Club is an international outlaw biker gang that expanded to Australia in 1975, establishing chapters in Melbourne and Sydney. It has approximately 250 members and 14 chapters in Australia, involved in drug trafficking, violent crimes, and money laundering through legitimate businesses.

org:sons-of-satan-motorcycle-club.description: One percenter outlaw motorcycle club based in Lancaster, Pennsylvania, founded in 1949 by returning WWII veterans. Originally a family-oriented club, it became a violent gang under John 'Satan' Marron, who later became National President of the Pagan's Motorcycle Club. The club is a brother club to the Pagans and wears a diamond 'P' patch to signify allegiance.

sources.title: Sons Of Satan Motorcycle Club — Wikipedia
Hells Angels MC criminal allegations and incidents — Wikipedia

org:highwaymen-motorcycle-club.description: The Highwaymen Motorcycle Club is a one-percenter outlaw motorcycle club founded in Detroit, Michigan in 1954. It is the largest motorcycle club in the Detroit area with over 400 members and chapters in several U.S. states. The club has been the subject of major FBI investigations in 1973, 1987, and 2007, resulting in convictions for racketeering, drug trafficking, and other crimes.

sources.title: Hells Angels MC criminal allegations and incidents in British Columbia — Wikipedia
Outlaws MC criminal allegations and incidents — Wikipedia

org:gypsy-joker-motorcycle-club.description: The Gypsy Joker Motorcycle Club is a one-percenter motorcycle club founded in San Bernardino, California on April 1, 1956. It expanded internationally, gaining notoriety in Australia, the United States, Germany, and Norway. The club is known for its violent criminal activities, including drug trafficking, homicide, and arson.

sources.title: Bandidos MC criminal allegations and incidents — Wikipedia
Bandidos MC criminal allegations and incidents in Australia — Wikipedia

org:chosen-few-motorcycle-club.description: The Chosen Few Motorcycle Club is the first mixed-race 1%er outlaw motorcycle club, founded around 1959 in Los Angeles, California by Lionel Ricks and others. It has chapters across the United States and internationally in the Philippines. The club is known for its outlaw status and diverse membership.

sources.title: Outlaws MC criminal allegations and incidents — Wikipedia

org:pagans-motorcycle-club.description: Outlaw motorcycle club formed by Lou Dobkin in 1959 in Prince George's County, Maryland. Known for territorial conflicts with the Hells Angels and other clubs. Active across the United States and Puerto Rico with over 100 chapters. Involved in traditional organized crime, including ties to La Cosa Nostra.

org:straight-satans-motorcycle-club.description: The Straight Satans Motorcycle Club was a one-percenter outlaw motorcycle gang based in Southern California, founded in July 1959 by three bikers known as Father Bill, Little Jerry, and Glick. It was among the earliest outlaw motorcycle clubs in the Los Angeles area, but remained localized without chapters beyond its original base.

sources.title: Mongols MC criminal allegations and incidents — Wikipedia

org:diablos-motorcycle-club.description: Outlaw motorcycle club founded in San Bernardino, California in 1961 by John J. 'Cadillac Jack' Baltas. Has chapters across the United States, with a significant presence in New England. Known for its red-and-black devil insignia and considered a second-tier outlaw motorcycle gang by law enforcement.

sources.title: Bandidos MC criminal allegations and incidents — Wikipedia
Bandidos MC criminal allegations and incidents in Australia — Wikipedia

org:el-forastero-motorcycle-club.description: One-percenter motorcycle club founded in 1962 by Tom Fugle and Harlan 'Tiny' Brower in Sioux City, Iowa. Known for criminal activities including motorcycle theft and methamphetamine distribution. Considered a second-tier outlaw club after the 'Big Four'. Has chapters in Iowa, Minnesota, Kansas, and Missouri.

sources.title: Hells Angels MC criminal allegations and incidents — Wikipedia
Outlaws MC criminal allegations and incidents — Wikipedia

org:dirty-dozen-motorcycle-club.description: The Dirty Dozen Motorcycle Club was an outlaw motorcycle club based in Arizona, founded in 1964 by twelve founding members. It became the largest and most violent motorcycle gang in the state, involved in drug trafficking, arms trafficking, prostitution, assault, and murder. The club merged with the Hells Angels in 1997.

sources.title: Mongols MC criminal allegations and incidents — Wikipedia

org:breed-motorcycle-club.description: The Breed Motorcycle Club was a one-percenter motorcycle club founded in Asbury Park, New Jersey in 1965 by Salvatore DeIulio. It engaged in drug trafficking, extortion, and other criminal activities, and was involved in conflicts with the Hells Angels and Pagans. The club disbanded in 2006 after prominent members were indicted on racketeering and drug trafficking charges.

sources.title: Hells Angels MC criminal allegations and incidents — Wikipedia
Outlaws MC criminal allegations and incidents — Wikipedia

org:iron-horsemen-motorcycle-club.description: American outlaw motorcycle club founded in 1965 in Cincinnati, Ohio. Known for drug trafficking and violent crimes, considered a second-tier outlaw motorcycle gang. Members have been convicted for drug dealing and murder, with operations spanning multiple states.

sources.title: Bandidos MC criminal allegations and incidents in the United States — Wikipedia
Hells Angels MC criminal allegations and incidents — Wikipedia
Outlaws MC criminal allegations and incidents — Wikipedia

org:bandidos-motorcycle-club.description: The Bandidos Motorcycle Club is a motorcycle gang classified as a criminal organization by law enforcement in multiple countries. In Denmark, the club has ten chapters and about 150 members involved in drug dealing, extortion, fraud, weapons trafficking, and violent crimes. The Danish Bandidos originated from the Morticians club, which rebranded as Undertakers and patched over by Bandidos in 1993.

sources.title: Bandidos MC criminal allegations and incidents — Wikipedia
Bandidos MC criminal allegations and incidents in the United States — Wikipedia
Bandidos MC criminal allegations and incidents in Denmark — Wikipedia

org:sons-of-silence.description: International outlaw motorcycle club founded in Niwot, Colorado in 1966 by Bruce Gale Richardson. Designated a criminal organization by the U.S. Department of Justice. Has over 250 members with 35 chapters in 12 U.S. states and Germany. Sixth-largest motorcycle club worldwide.

sources.title: Hells Angels MC criminal allegations and incidents — Wikipedia
Outlaws MC criminal allegations and incidents — Wikipedia

org:vagos-motorcycle-club.description: The Vagos Motorcycle Club is a one percenter outlaw motorcycle club formed in the 1960s in San Bernardino, California. With approximately 5,000 members across 200 chapters, it operates primarily in the Western United States, Mexico, and Europe. The club's insignia features Loki, the Norse god of mischief, riding a motorcycle.

sources.title: Bandidos MC criminal allegations and incidents — Wikipedia
Bandidos MC criminal allegations and incidents in the United States — Wikipedia

org:devils-diciples-motorcycle-club.description: An outlaw motorcycle club founded in Fontana, California in 1967. The club has chapters across multiple U.S. states and is considered a second-tier outlaw motorcycle club by law enforcement. Members have been involved in firearms and narcotics offenses, including a major methamphetamine case in Detroit.

sources.title: Hells Angels MC criminal allegations and incidents — Wikipedia
Outlaws MC criminal allegations and incidents — Wikipedia

edges.evidence: Not only did each of their gangs fight the Egyptian Cobras and the Devil's Disciples north and west of them, they also fought each other tooth and nail

The Devil's Disciples proposed an alliance with the Disciples that would be so tight it would be a brotherhood. The proposition was for the Devil's Disciples to extend membership into the Disciple Nation for the Disciples

Jerome Cogwell was the younger brother of Mickey Cogwell and was about 20 years old when he was killed. The murder was done by the Devil's Disciples which was the main gang allied with the Disciple alliance.

org:hells-lovers-motorcycle-club.description: Multi-ethnic outlaw motorcycle club founded in Chicago in 1967 by Frank 'Claim-Jumper' Rios. Active in various U.S. states with over 1,500 members. Associated with drug trafficking and motorcycle theft, and has been involved in violent incidents with rival clubs.

sources.title: Wheels of Soul Motorcycle Club — Wikipedia

org:sin-city-deciples-motorcycle-club.description: The Sin City Deciples Motorcycle Club is a mixed-race one-percenter outlaw motorcycle club founded in Gary, Indiana in 1967. It is one of the oldest and most well-known black outlaw motorcycle clubs in the United States, with chapters across the U.S. and international presence. The club is involved in illicit drug sales and is regarded as a criminal organization by law enforcement.

sources.title: Sin City Deciples Motorcycle Club — Wikipedia

org:warlocks-motorcycle-club-pennsylvani.description: American outlaw motorcycle club formed in Philadelphia in 1967. The club is involved in narcotics distribution, prostitution, kidnapping, burglary, sale of stolen property, violent crime, and murder. It is most prominent in the Delaware Valley and has chapters in several Mid-Atlantic states. Membership is restricted to white males over 18 who own a Harley-Davidson motorcycle.

org:warlocks-motorcycle-club-florida.description: International outlaw motorcycle club founded in 1967 in Florida by ex-US naval servicemen. Known as a 'One Percenter' club with chapters in the US, Canada, England, and Germany. The mother chapter remains in Orlando, Florida. Founder Tom 'Grub' Freeland died in 2019.

org:wheels-of-soul-motorcycle-club.description: Multiracial one-percenter outlaw motorcycle club founded in 1967 in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. Primarily based in Philadelphia, with chapters in at least 25 states and Puerto Rico. Engages in drug trafficking, robbery, murder, and other violent crimes. Deemed a violent gang by federal officials.

sources.title: Wheels Of Soul Motorcycle Club — Wikipedia
Bandidos MC criminal allegations and incidents in the United States — Wikipedia
Sin City Deciples Motorcycle Club — Wikipedia

org:hessians-motorcycle-club.description: American one-percenter outlaw motorcycle club founded on March 7, 1968, in California by Thomas F. Maniscalco. The club expanded across the western United States and was involved in drug trafficking, murder, robbery, and intimidation. Founder Maniscalco was convicted for three murders related to methamphetamine disputes. The club remains active with chapters in multiple states.

sources.title: Hells Angels MC criminal allegations and incidents — Wikipedia

org:brother-speed-motorcycle-club.description: American outlaw motorcycle club formed in Boise, Idaho in 1969 by a group of high school friends. Active in Idaho, Washington, and Oregon, it has been involved in methamphetamine trafficking and alliances with other clubs like the Bandidos. The club's colors are black and gold, and its center patch features a winged skull.

sources.title: Bandidos MC criminal allegations and incidents — Wikipedia

org:cossacks-motorcycle-club.description: American outlaw motorcycle club founded in Tyler, Texas in 1969 by six bikers. One of the largest outlaw biker groups in Texas, known for conflicts with the Bandidos Motorcycle Club, including the 2015 Waco shootout. Has chapters in Texas, Ohio, Indiana, and Virginia.

sources.title: Bandidos MC criminal allegations and incidents — Wikipedia
Bandidos MC criminal allegations and incidents in the United States — Wikipedia

org:free-souls-motorcycle-club.description: Outlaw motorcycle club founded in Eugene, Oregon in 1969. Has chapters in Oregon, Washington, Germany, and Australia. Its center patch features an ankh over a motorcycle wheel on handlebars, with blue and white colors.

org:mongols-mc.description: The Mongols Motorcycle Club is the 3rd-largest outlaw motorcycle club in the world with 4,000-5,000 members in 29 countries. Founded December 5, 1969 in Montebello, California by Hispanic Vietnam veterans who were excluded from the Hells Angels due to race. Allied with the Mexican Mafia — La Eme members recruited heavily from the Mongols. Designated an organized crime group by the DOJ, Australian Federal Police, and Europol. The Hells Angels ceded Southern California to the Mongols in the 1980s. The US government tried and failed multiple times to seize their trademarked logo through civil forfeiture. Involved in methamphetamine trafficking, money laundering, extortion, and murder.

sources.title: Bandidos MC criminal allegations and incidents — Wikipedia
Bandidos MC criminal allegations and incidents in Australia — Wikipedia

org:sadistic-souls-motorcycle-club.description: White supremacist outlaw motorcycle club founded in 2010 by ex-KKK member Dennis McGiffen. Acts as the militant wing of Aryan Nations, with chapters across the US and several other countries. Listed as an active neo-Nazi group by the Southern Poverty Law Center since 2014.

org:original-block-pirus-151.description: Original Block Pirus are a predominately African-American street gang located on the westside of Compton. Their turf is adjacent to the Compton Airport and they share their turf with Compton Varrio 155, a predominately Latino street gang. The OBPs were found around 1971.

org:eastside-fruit-town-pirus-in-compton.description: Fruit Town Pirus are a predominately African-American street gang on the eastside of Compton. They formed during the very early 1970s and are among the oldest Piru sets in Compton.

sources.title: Eastside Fruit Town Pirus in Compton — StreetGangs

org:lime-hood-pirus.description: The Lime Hood Pirus are a predominantly African-American Blood-affiliated gang on the east side of Compton, California, with territory from Rosecrans to Compton Boulevard between Atlantic and Gibson Avenues.

org:lueders-park-pirus.description: East Side Lueders Park Pirus are a predominately African-American street gang located on the eastside of Compton, CA. This area was originally known as East Side Piru, but the East Side Pirus splintered into several Piru sets with Lueders Park Pirus as the first Piru set on the eastside. During the early 1970s the Lueders Park Piru and the Compton Varrio 70s were closely aligned.

org:water-front-pirus.description: The Waterfront Piru (H2O Piru) is a Blood set based at the Dana Strand Village Housing Projects in Wilmington, in the Los Angeles Harbor area, operating in one of LA's most gang-affected harbor communities.

org:mid-wilshire-bloods.description: The Mid Wilshire Bloods are a predominantly African-American Blood gang based in the Mid-Wilshire area of Los Angeles, operating in the neighborhood between Western Avenue and La Brea Avenue along Wilshire Boulevard.

org:parke-nine-bloods.description: A Blood gang set based in the Pasadena area of Los Angeles County, one of the African-American Blood sets in the Pasadena/Altadena corridor.

org:project-gangster-bloods-in-pasadena.description: An African-American Blood gang set based in housing projects in the Riverside/Inland Empire area of Southern California.

sources.title: Project Gangster Bloods in Pasadena — StreetGangs

org:barjug.description: An Asian-American street gang active in the greater Los Angeles area, composed primarily of Southeast Asian youth.

org:varrio-posole-locos.description: Varrio Posole Locos 13 (VPLS) is an Mexican-American criminal street gang, located in the Eastside neighborhood of Oceanside, San Diego County, California.

org:standards.description: ACLU Wins Higher Legal Standards for Gang Injunctions; Innocent Protected From Arbitrary Restrictions ACLU Wins Higher Legal Standards for Gang Injunctions; Innocent Protected From Arbitrary Restrictions FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE Thursday, May 10, 2001 SAN DIEGO — The California Court of Appeal ruled today that prosecutors must first prove through clear and convincing evidence that a young man is a gang member before using an anti-gang injunction to restrict his right to engage in everyday act.

org:barrio-logan-heights-gang.description: The Logan Heights Gang was established in Southeast San Diego when several Mexican-American street gangs from Barrio Logan unified, with roots tracing back to car clubs in the 1970s. They became notoriously known as enforcers for the Arellano-Felix Drug Organization (Tijuana Cartel), carrying out contract killings on both sides of the US-Mexico border. Members were arrested in 2024 for running a protection racket extorting businesses in Barrio Logan.

sources.title: DOJ - Strike Against San Diego Gangs

org:lopers-13.description: Lopers 13 is a Mexican American street gang founded in Santa Ana, California, in the late 1980s or early 1990s. It is the largest gang in Santa Ana by territory, controlling areas including Main Street, Minnie Street, First Street, and Edinger Avenue. The gang is involved in drug trafficking and has been targeted by law enforcement operations.

org:mesa.description: Mesa Locos (MLS) are primarily an Mexican-American street gang located in the Mesa Margarita, neighborhood on the West Side of Oceanside, in San Diego County, California. This gang originated sometime in the early 1980s, around the Libby Lake Park area, between Calle Vallecitos and Calle Montecito.

org:fresno-bulldogs.description: Primarily Mexican American criminal street and prison gang based in Fresno, California, founded in 1984. One of the biggest drug gangs in Central California with an estimated 12,000 members. Engaged in drug trafficking, human trafficking, murder, and other crimes. Unaffiliated with Sureños or Norteños, and rivals with multiple prison gangs.

sources.title: Fresno Bulldogs Operation Shock Collar — DOJ

org:forming-kaos.description: Forming Kaos (FK) is a Sureño street gang based in Costa Mesa, Orange County, California. Started as a party/tagger crew in the early 1990s, became a formal street gang by 1999-2000 after absorbing East Side FK. Mexican Mafia-affiliated — they extort drug dealers and forward proceeds to imprisoned La Eme members. In 2011, 'Operation Black Flag' (3.5-year FBI investigation) led to 99 arrests of Mexican Mafia-affiliated gang members, with 17 FK members indicted on federal RICO charges. Territory bounded by Fairview Road, Placentia Avenue, 19th Street, and West Wilson Street.

org:dorner-blocc-criminal-moreno-valley.description: The Edgemont Dorner Blocc Criminal Gang (DBCG) also known as the Dorner Blocc Criminals (EDBC). Which consist of three individual gangs, known as the Dorner blocc Crips (DBC), Edgemont Criminals Gang (ECG) and the Young Paper Chasers (YPC) who emerged as one powerhouse criminal gang in the Moreno Valley, region of Riverside County of California.

sources.title: Dorner Blocc Criminal Moreno Valley — Wikipedia

org:g-mobb.description: Parkway Mobb, is a multi-ethnic hybrid street gang that originated in the Phoenix Park housing apartments (formerly Franklin Villa) in the Parkway neighborhood of Sacramento, California. Founded by teenagers who migrated from Oakland.

org:clanton14th-original-hood-los-angele.description: Clanton 14th Street is a predominately Hispanic gang originally from Los Angeles’ eastside. The original Clanton varrio started in the early 1920s on Clanton Street which was located between San Pedro and Central Avenue. The territory of Clanton varrio once went from Olympic Blvd (north) to Santa Barbara Avenue (south) which is now known as Martin Luther King Jr.

org:eastwilmas.description: Los Angeles Varrio Eastside Wilmas is a predominately Hispanic street gang in the Wilmington community of Los Angeles.

org:rancho-san-pedro-los-angeles.description: Rancho San Pedro is a predominately Latino street gang located in the San Pedro community in the city of Los Angeles on the far southern tip of the city. The varrio was founded in the late 1950s or early 1960s as Mexicans were migrating into this section of the city during the post WW2 boom.

org:lelandpark.description: Leland Park is a Latino street gang based in the San Pedro/Harbor area of Los Angeles, named after the Leland Park neighborhood. The Harbor area has been home to numerous gangs under Mexican Mafia control since the 1980s.

org:mid-city-stoners.description: City Stoners 13 are a Mexican-American criminal street gang located in the City of Los Angeles and Long Beach, California.

org:longos-13.description: The Varrio Longo 13 are the largest Sureño gang throughout the Harbor Area of Long Beach, California. The Longo 13 is made up of three sets: The East Side Longos (ESL13) which is the largest of the three, with an estimate of over 600 members. The second largest of the three are the West Side Longos (WSL13), with around 300 members, followed by the North Side Longos (NSL13).

org:barrio-small-town.description: The Barrio Small Town 13 (BST13) and Pueblo Chico which means Small Town in Spanish this gang is majority Hispanic located in the downtown area of Long Beach, California. Their neighborhood stretches from 4th Street to 7th Street between Golden Avenue and Pacific Ave. The barrio was established in the early 1970s, on 4th street in the golden projects.

org:varrio-204th-street.description: Street 13, are a Mexican-American criminal street gang based in the Torrance and Harbor Gateway areas of Southern California.

org:wilmas-13.description: Wilmas 13 is a Mexican American street gang based in Wilmington, Los Angeles, California, controlled by the Mexican Mafia. It has four sub-gangs and is involved in drug trafficking, arms trafficking, murder, and extortion. The gang has been subject to federal racketeering indictments and civil injunctions.

org:sanpedro.description: San Pedro 13 (SP13) is a Sureño street gang based in the San Pedro neighborhood of Los Angeles. One of the oldest Hispanic gangs in the Harbor area.

org:sons-of-samoa.description: Sons of Samoa (SOS) is a Crips-affiliated street gang based in west Long Beach, California, predominantly Samoan American. By 1985 had about 200 members. Active in Alaska, California, Missouri, Utah, and Washington per a 2011 FBI report. In the 1980s heavily involved in cocaine and PCP sales, robbery, burglary, and extortion. Uses the Seattle Mariners 'S' logo. The Suicidals are a subset.

org:westside-locos.description: Predominantly Latino-American street gang based in San Fernando Valley, Glendale, with history dating back to the 1950s. They reside mainly in the Riverside Rancho neighborhood and are known for murder. Their main subset is Chicos Malos or Lake street. The gang is part of a larger Locos 13 alliance throughout Los Angeles County.

org:varrio-san-fers.description: Varrio San Fers 13 (VSF13), are an Mexican-American Sureno street gang based in the San Fernando Valley, Los Angeles County, California.

org:north-hollywood-locos.description: A Latino Sureño-affiliated street gang based in the North Hollywood area of the San Fernando Valley.

org:van-owen-street-locos.description: A Latino Sureño-affiliated street gang based near Van Owen Street in the San Fernando Valley, part of the network of SFV Hispanic gangs documented since the 1990s.

org:barriovannuys.description: Los Angeles Barrio Van Nuys (BVN) is a predominately Hispanic street gang located in the Van Nuys community of the San Fernando Valley in the City of Los Angeles Clicks of this gang include North Side, South Side, Delano Street, Dukes, Cyclones, Jokers, Poor Boys, Tiny Locos, Traviesos, and the Locos.

org:boys-from-the-hood.description: A Sureño-affiliated Latino street gang based in the San Fernando Valley area of Los Angeles, active since at least the 1990s.

org:dead-end-boys.description: A Latino Sureño-affiliated street gang based in the Pacoima area of the San Fernando Valley, Los Angeles.

org:high-times-familia.description: Latino street gang in the San Fernando Valley, documented among active SFV gangs since at least the late 1990s.

org:langdonstreet.description: Sepas), is a predominately Hispanic street gang located in the North Hills section of the West San Fernando Valley, Los Angeles.

org:latin-times-pacoima.description: A Latino Sureño-affiliated street gang based in the Pacoima neighborhood of the San Fernando Valley, Los Angeles.

org:little-valley.description: Latino street gang based in the San Fernando Valley, one of numerous SFV barrio gangs that emerged during the 1960s–1970s as the Valley's Latino population grew.

org:north-hollywood-boyz.description: Latino street gang based in North Hollywood in the San Fernando Valley, active since at least the late 1990s.

org:pacoima-flats-pf.description: This neighborhood in the past has controlled Pacoima, yet many have moved on and become peace makers bussiness owners etc.

org:pacoima-humphrey-boyz.description: Latino street gang based on Humphrey Avenue in the Pacoima neighborhood of the San Fernando Valley, one of many street-named gangs in the heavily gang-impacted Pacoima area which has over a dozen active sets.

org:pacoima-project-boyz-aka-paca-projec.description: Pacoima are a predominately Hispanic street gang based in the San Fernando Gardens housing projects in Pacoima which is in the City of Los Angeles.

sources.title: Pacoima Project Boyz, aka Paca Project Boys — StreetGangs

org:pacoima-vaught-von-street.description: Latino street gang based on Vaught Street in Pacoima, San Fernando Valley, one of many street-named cliques in the heavily gang-impacted Pacoima area.

org:paxton-street-locos-psl-pacoima.description: Latino street gang based on Paxton Street in Pacoima, San Fernando Valley, active since at least the 1990s.

org:reseda-south-side.description: A Latino Sureño-affiliated street gang based on the south side of the Reseda neighborhood in the San Fernando Valley.

org:reseda-west-side.description: A Latino Sureño-affiliated street gang based on the west side of the Reseda neighborhood in the San Fernando Valley.

org:west-valley-crazys.description: Latino street gang based in the western San Fernando Valley, part of the network of SFV Hispanic gangs active since the 1990s.

org:bryant-san-fernando-valley-los-angel.description: San Fernando Valley Bryant Street is a predominately Latino street gang in the North Hills section of the San Fernando Valley in the City of Los Angeles. The gang started on Bryant Street in the late 1970s.

org:blythe-bst-los-angeles.description: Blythe Street (BST) is a predominately Hispanic-American street gang located in the Panorama City section of Los Angeles in the West San Fernando Valley. During the late 1970s, Blythe Street was originally a click or subgroup of the gang Barrio Van Nuys, but it broke away from BVN to establish their own neighborhood.

org:vineland-boys.description: The Vineland Boys is a Hispanic street gang in the San Fernando Valley with approximately 300 members. Founded in January 1988 in Sun Valley by former 18th Street gang members, named after Vineland Avenue in North Hollywood. Initially clashed with the Mexican Mafia until founder Teddy Lopez was murdered by La Eme at a Monterey Park nightclub in 1998 — they then joined the Sureño coalition. In 2003, member David Garcia fatally shot Burbank Police Officer Matthew Pavelka, prompting 'Operation Silent Night' with 1,300 officers and 23 arrests. Allied with Armenian Power and the Israeli Abergil crime family (ecstasy trafficking). Ties to the Sinaloa Cartel were revealed during the Pavelka investigation.

org:columbusstreet.description: Columbus Street (CST) is a predominately Hispanic street gang located in the North Hills community of the San Fernando Valley in the City of Los Angeles.

org:pirus.description: The Pirus are an umbrella street gang alliance within the larger Bloods alliance, founded in 1969 on Piru Street in Compton, California by Sylvester 'Puddin' Scott and Vincent Benson Owens. Originally called the 300 Playboy Piru Bloods, they formed to resist Crip dominance and allied with the Brims, Bishops, and Denver Lanes to create the Bloods. There are 15 known Piru sets in Greater Los Angeles. Piru sets use burgundy or red bandanas for identification.

org:bartender-pirus.description: Bartender Pirus was a short-lived predominately African-American street gang located on the eastside of Compton during the 1970s.

org:bloods.description: The Bloods are a predominantly African-American street gang founded in Los Angeles, California in 1972. They are known for their red attire and hand signs spelling 'Blood'. The gang consists of numerous subgroups called 'sets' and is engaged in drug trafficking, extortion, murder, robbery, and pimping.

sources.title: Almighty Black P. Stone Nation — Wikipedia

edges.evidence: most Crip-identified sets historically maintain adversarial relationships with several Blood-affiliated groups

org:pasadena-denver-lanes.description: Ls) are a predominately African-American street gang located in King’s Manor Apartments in Pasadena, California.

org:west-covina-mob-piru.description: West Covina Mob Piru is a predominately African-American street gang in the city of West Covina in Los Angeles County.

sources.title: Team West Covina Mob Piru West Covina Mob Piru — StreetGangs

org:simon-city-royals-chicago.description: The Simon City Royals (Almighty Simon City Royal Nation) are a predominantly white street and prison gang founded in 1952 as Simon City (a greaser gang at Simons Park in Chicago's Humboldt Park) and renamed in 1969 after merging with the Ashland Royals. One of the oldest and largest white gangs in the US. In 1978, they joined Larry Hoover's Folk Nation alliance. Originally multiethnic (Mexican, Puerto Rican, Cuban, White, Arabic members). They've expanded from Chicago to Wisconsin, Mississippi, and Alabama — one of four most prominent gangs in Mississippi. In 2022, 21 members were RICO-indicted, with 37 total convicted by 2024. Involved in drug trafficking, human trafficking, and loan sharking in prisons.

sources.title: Simon City Royals - Chicago Gang History

edges.evidence: Another rivalry to begin that year was between Eagles and the Ashland Royals that would eventually evolve into the Simon City Royals by the end of the year 1969.
In 1992 things got real bloody between the Cobras and the Simon City Royals.
Flip City Kings had a deep rivalry with the Simon City Royals and Insane Popes.

In the year 1973, the Gangster Disciples (known as Black Gangster Disciples at the time) were looking to establish business with gangs that were not of the African American race. Black Gangster Disciples connected with the Ashland Vikings, a mostly Puerto Rican group, the Simon City Royals, a mostly white group and Ambrose, a mostly Mexican group.
Brazers found common ground with the Simon City Royals in that neighborhood who were also fighting a heavy battle with Latin Kings.
In the year 1979, Imperial Gangsters first started to refer to themselves as "Almighty" as this more of less unofficial alliance first started with Latin Eagles and Simon City Royals.
Latin Kings were very close to the Deuces as they fought Simon City Royals and Paulina Barry Community viciously.
the earliest Los Bebes fought the Royals from Stone Park and Franklin Park too.
Folk Nation — c. 1979 – 2000 or later

In the early 70s, the original Deuce's only arch enemy was the Simon City Royals and would become their first arch enemy since the Royals first formed in 1971. The murder of Arab became completely unforgivable for the Simon City Royals and the two gangs became sworn arch enemies for life after the murder.

On a warm day in 1976, Royals and Unknowns were hanging out while drinking beers together and listening to music having a good time at Wrightwood and Wayne. All of a sudden frank pulled out a small 25 caliber pistol and shot Capone to death without saying a word and in a stone cold manner. Even William Fender had no idea his brother was going to shoot Capone, it seemingly was a spur of the moment action, and this started the war.
The first enemies of these Lakeview P.R. Stones were the Simon City Royals who also arrived in the neighborhood in 1968

Now Almighty Insane Popes and Simon City Royals both hated Gaylords, Latin Kings and Insane Deuces with a passion. With the hate of the Gaylords soon followed a hatred of the entire U.F.O unity which brought the Royals to war with C-Notes.
A war with the Ashland Vikings was costly between 1996-1998 and this led to Clark and Farwell's extinction, the Vikings also went extinct in that area but La Raza lived on.
A racial clash soon ensued, and the Puerto Rican youths of East Humboldt Park formed the "Noble Knights" street gang to defend themselves against Simon City and other mostly white clubs.
The Hell's Devils now joined the fight against Latin Kings, Spanish Cobras, Simon City Royals and Paulina and Barry Community.

The P.V.P and G.C.G extended their alliance over a broader network and connected with all the white gangs including enemies like the Almighty Popes and Simon City Royals. This new alliance was called the 'White Power Organization,' or 'W.P.O.'
they viciously battled Palmer Street Gaylords, Lawndale Gaylords, the Simon City Royals from Drake and Wolfram and Christiana and Wellington

The original Leavitt and Schiller Insane Unknowns were friends with Simon City members from Logan Square and some Simon City residing in northern Wicker Park. When Simon City officially disbanded in 1970 they gave away their black sweaters to friends of theirs like TJ
These PBCs also developed a strong conflict with Simon City Royals.
In 1994, the PBCs joined the 'Almighty' alliance and invested in an actual alliance with the Simon City Royals.
The WPO broke up after Gaylords went to war with Simon City Royals and Insane Popes. Now this meant P.V.P, P.V.R. and P.V.J all had to war against them too.
The Future Stones had the same rivals as the P.R. Stones such as Simon City Royals, Spanish Cobras and Assyrian Eagles.

org:spanish-cobras-chicago.description: The Insane Spanish Cobras are a primarily Latino (Puerto Rican/Mexican) Folk Nation street gang founded circa 1958 in Bridgeport, Chicago as 'Loco De Culebro' (Crazy Snakes). Richard 'King Cobra' Medina created the 'Insane' concepts in the mid-1970s, adopting black and green colors. Medina was assassinated by Insane Unknowns in 1979 at age 20. In 1989, Cobras, Maniac Latin Disciples, and Two Sixers formed Spanish Growth and Development (SGD), modeled after the Chicago Outfit, to control shootings — it eventually collapsed. The Insane-Maniac war escalated in 1996 making Chicago Tribune headlines. Led by Anibal 'Tuffy C' Santiago. Present in Chicago (North/West sides), Milwaukee, Racine, Kenosha, Detroit, Ohio, Connecticut, and Florida.

edges.evidence: After this beating death of the Latin Eagle war ensued between Latin Eagles and Spanish Cobras putting a dent in the ULO coalition.
In 1992 things got real bloody between the Cobras and the Simon City Royals.
Spanish Cobras, Maniac Latin Disciples, Imperial Gangsters and Latin Eagles came together in an alliance called 'United Latino Organization' or 'U.L.O.'

By 1976 these groups all had established sections in northwest West Humboldt Park. Latin Disciples started out at Avers and Division...In current years it is hard to imagine Spanish Cobras and Maniac Latin Disciples as allies but it was real in the 70s and 80s.
